  "newString": "                let ty::SubtypeRelation { a_is_expected: _, sub_ty, super_ty } = data;\n                // both must be type variables, or the other would've been instantiated\n                assert!(sub_ty.is_ty_var() && super_ty.is_ty_var());\n                self.emit_inference_failure_err(\n                    obligation.cause.body_id,\n                    span,\n                    sub_ty.into(),",
  "oldString": "                let ty::SubtypeRelation { a_is_expected: _, a, b } = data;\n                // both must be type variables, or the other would've been instantiated\n                assert!(a.is_ty_var() && b.is_ty_var());\n                self.emit_inference_failure_err(\n                    obligation.cause.body_id,\n                    span,\n                    a.into(),",
  "originalFile": "use std::ops::ControlFlow;\n\nuse rustc_errors::{Applicability, Diag, E0283, E0284, E0790, MultiSpan, struct_span_code_err};\nuse rustc_hir as hir;\nuse rustc_hir::LangItem;\nuse rustc_hir::def::{DefKind, Res};\nuse rustc_hir::def_id::{CRATE_DEF_ID, DefId};\nuse rustc_hir::intravisit::Visitor as _;\nuse rustc_infer::infer::{BoundRegionConversionTime, InferCtxt};\nuse rustc_infer::traits::util::elaborate;\nuse rustc_infer::traits::{\n    Obligation, ObligationCause, ObligationCauseCode, PolyTraitObligation, PredicateObligation,\n};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::{self, Ty, TyCtxt, TypeVisitable as _, TypeVisitableExt as _};\nuse rustc_session::parse::feature_err_unstable_feature_bound;\nuse rustc_span::{DUMMY_SP, ErrorGuaranteed, Span};\nuse tracing::{debug, instrument};\n\nuse crate::error_reporting::TypeErrCtxt;\nuse crate::error_reporting::infer::need_type_info::TypeAnnotationNeeded;\nuse crate::error_reporting::traits::{FindExprBySpan, to_pretty_impl_header};\nuse crate::traits::ObligationCtxt;\nuse crate::traits::query::evaluate_obligation::InferCtxtExt;\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub enum CandidateSource {\n    DefId(DefId),\n    ParamEnv(Span),\n}\n\npub fn compute_applicable_impls_for_diagnostics<'tcx>(\n    infcx: &InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n    obligation: &PolyTraitObligation<'tcx>,\n) -> Vec<CandidateSource> {\n    let tcx = infcx.tcx;\n    let param_env = obligation.param_env;\n\n    let predicate_polarity = obligation.predicate.skip_binder().polarity;\n\n    let impl_may_apply = |impl_def_id| {\n        let ocx = ObligationCtxt::new(infcx);\n        infcx.enter_forall(obligation.predicate, |placeholder_obligation| {\n            let obligation_trait_ref = ocx.normalize(\n                &ObligationCause::dummy(),\n                param_env,\n                placeholder_obligation.trait_ref,\n            );\n\n            let impl_args = infcx.fresh_args_for_item(DUMMY_SP, impl_def_id);\n            let impl_trait_ref = tcx.impl_trait_ref(impl_def_id).instantiate(tcx, impl_args);\n            let impl_trait_ref =\n                ocx.normalize(&ObligationCause::dummy(), param_env, impl_trait_ref);\n\n            if let Err(_) =\n                ocx.eq(&ObligationCause::dummy(), param_env, obligation_trait_ref, impl_trait_ref)\n            {\n                return false;\n            }\n\n            let impl_trait_header = tcx.impl_trait_header(impl_def_id);\n            let impl_polarity = impl_trait_header.polarity;\n\n            match (impl_polarity, predicate_polarity) {\n                (ty::ImplPolarity::Positive, ty::PredicatePolarity::Positive)\n                | (ty::ImplPolarity::Negative, ty::PredicatePolarity::Negative) => {}\n                _ => return false,\n            }\n\n            let obligations = tcx\n                .predicates_of(impl_def_id)\n                .instantiate(tcx, impl_args)\n                .into_iter()\n                .map(|(predicate, _)| {\n                    Obligation::new(tcx, ObligationCause::dummy(), param_env, predicate)\n                })\n                // Kinda hacky, but let's just throw away obligations that overflow.\n                // This may reduce the accuracy of this check (if the obligation guides\n                // inference or it actually resulted in error after others are processed)\n                // ... but this is diagnostics code.\n                .filter(|obligation| {\n                    infcx.next_trait_solver() || infcx.evaluate_obligation(obligation).is_ok()\n                });\n            ocx.register_obligations(obligations);\n\n            ocx.try_evaluate_obligations().is_empty()\n        })\n    };\n\n    let param_env_candidate_may_apply = |poly_trait_predicate: ty::PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx>| {\n        let ocx = ObligationCtxt::new(infcx);\n        infcx.enter_forall(obligation.predicate, |placeholder_obligation| {\n            let obligation_trait_ref = ocx.normalize(\n                &ObligationCause::dummy(),\n                param_env,\n                placeholder_obligation.trait_ref,\n            );\n\n            let param_env_predicate = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(\n                DUMMY_SP,\n                BoundRegionConversionTime::HigherRankedType,\n                poly_trait_predicate,\n            );\n            let param_env_trait_ref =\n                ocx.normalize(&ObligationCause::dummy(), param_env, param_env_predicate.trait_ref);\n\n            if let Err(_) = ocx.eq(\n                &ObligationCause::dummy(),\n                param_env,\n                obligation_trait_ref,\n                param_env_trait_ref,\n            ) {\n                return false;\n            }\n\n            ocx.try_evaluate_obligations().is_empty()\n        })\n    };\n\n    let mut ambiguities = Vec::new();\n\n    tcx.for_each_relevant_impl(\n        obligation.predicate.def_id(),\n        obligation.predicate.skip_binder().trait_ref.self_ty(),\n        |impl_def_id| {\n            if infcx.probe(|_| impl_may_apply(impl_def_id)) {\n                ambiguities.push(CandidateSource::DefId(impl_def_id))\n            }\n        },\n    );\n\n    // If our `body_id` has been set (and isn't just from a dummy obligation cause),\n    // then try to look for a param-env clause that would apply. The way we compute\n    // this is somewhat manual, since we need the spans, so we elaborate this directly\n    // from `predicates_of` rather than actually looking at the param-env which\n    // otherwise would be more appropriate.\n    let body_id = obligation.cause.body_id;\n    if body_id != CRATE_DEF_ID {\n        let predicates = tcx.predicates_of(body_id.to_def_id()).instantiate_identity(tcx);\n        for (pred, span) in elaborate(tcx, predicates.into_iter()) {\n            let kind = pred.kind();\n            if let ty::ClauseKind::Trait(trait_pred) = kind.skip_binder()\n                && param_env_candidate_may_apply(kind.rebind(trait_pred))\n            {\n                if kind.rebind(trait_pred.trait_ref)\n                    == ty::Binder::dummy(ty::TraitRef::identity(tcx, trait_pred.def_id()))\n                {\n                    ambiguities.push(CandidateSource::ParamEnv(tcx.def_span(trait_pred.def_id())))\n                } else {\n                    ambiguities.push(CandidateSource::ParamEnv(span))\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    ambiguities\n}\n\nimpl<'a, 'tcx> TypeErrCtxt<'a, 'tcx> {\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"debug\")]\n    pub(super) fn maybe_report_ambiguity(\n        &self,\n        obligation: &PredicateObligation<'tcx>,\n    ) -> ErrorGuaranteed {\n        // Unable to successfully determine, probably means\n        // insufficient type information, but could mean\n        // ambiguous impls. The latter *ought* to be a\n        // coherence violation, so we don't report it here.\n\n        let predicate = self.resolve_vars_if_possible(obligation.predicate);\n        let span = obligation.cause.span;\n        let mut long_ty_path = None;\n\n        debug!(?predicate, obligation.cause.code = ?obligation.cause.code());\n\n        // Ambiguity errors are often caused as fallout from earlier errors.\n        // We ignore them if this `infcx` is tainted in some cases below.\n\n        let bound_predicate = predicate.kind();\n        let mut err = match bound_predicate.skip_binder() {\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::Trait(data)) => {\n                let trait_pred = bound_predicate.rebind(data);\n                debug!(?trait_pred);\n\n                if let Err(e) = predicate.error_reported() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n\n                if let Err(guar) = self.tcx.ensure_ok().coherent_trait(trait_pred.def_id()) {\n                    // Avoid bogus \"type annotations needed `Foo: Bar`\" errors on `impl Bar for Foo` in case\n                    // other `Foo` impls are incoherent.\n                    return guar;\n                }\n\n                // This is kind of a hack: it frequently happens that some earlier\n                // error prevents types from being fully inferred, and then we get\n                // a bunch of uninteresting errors saying something like \"<generic\n                // #0> doesn't implement Sized\". It may even be true that we\n                // could just skip over all checks where the self-ty is an\n                // inference variable, but I was afraid that there might be an\n                // inference variable created, registered as an obligation, and\n                // then never forced by writeback, and hence by skipping here we'd\n                // be ignoring the fact that we don't KNOW the type works\n                // out. Though even that would probably be harmless, given that\n                // we're only talking about builtin traits, which are known to be\n                // inhabited. We used to check for `self.tainted_by_errors()` to\n                // avoid inundating the user with unnecessary errors, but we now\n                // check upstream for type errors and don't add the obligations to\n                // begin with in those cases.\n                if matches!(\n                    self.tcx.as_lang_item(trait_pred.def_id()),\n                    Some(LangItem::Sized | LangItem::MetaSized)\n                ) {\n                    return match self.tainted_by_errors() {\n                        None => self\n                            .emit_inference_failure_err(\n                                obligation.cause.body_id,\n                                span,\n                                trait_pred.self_ty().skip_binder().into(),\n                                TypeAnnotationNeeded::E0282,\n                                false,\n                            )\n                            .emit(),\n                        Some(e) => e,\n                    };\n                }\n\n                // Typically, this ambiguity should only happen if\n                // there are unresolved type inference variables\n                // (otherwise it would suggest a coherence\n                // failure). But given #21974 that is not necessarily\n                // the case -- we can have multiple where clauses that\n                // are only distinguished by a region, which results\n                // in an ambiguity even when all types are fully\n                // known, since we don't dispatch based on region\n                // relationships.\n\n                // Pick the first generic parameter that still contains inference variables as the one\n                // we're going to emit an error for. If there are none (see above), fall back to\n                // a more general error.\n                let term = data\n                    .trait_ref\n                    .args\n                    .iter()\n                    .filter_map(ty::GenericArg::as_term)\n                    .find(|s| s.has_non_region_infer());\n\n                let mut err = if let Some(term) = term {\n                    self.emit_inference_failure_err(\n                        obligation.cause.body_id,\n                        span,\n                        term,\n                        TypeAnnotationNeeded::E0283,\n                        true,\n                    )\n                } else {\n                    struct_span_code_err!(\n                        self.dcx(),\n                        span,\n                        E0283,\n                        \"type annotations needed: cannot satisfy `{}`\",\n                        self.tcx.short_string(predicate, &mut long_ty_path),\n                    )\n                    .with_long_ty_path(long_ty_path)\n                };\n\n                let mut ambiguities = compute_applicable_impls_for_diagnostics(\n                    self.infcx,\n                    &obligation.with(self.tcx, trait_pred),\n                );\n                let has_non_region_infer = trait_pred\n                    .skip_binder()\n                    .trait_ref\n                    .args\n                    .types()\n                    .any(|t| !t.is_ty_or_numeric_infer());\n                // It doesn't make sense to talk about applicable impls if there are more than a\n                // handful of them. If there are a lot of them, but only a few of them have no type\n                // params, we only show those, as they are more likely to be useful/intended.\n                if ambiguities.len() > 5 {\n                    let infcx = self.infcx;\n                    if !ambiguities.iter().all(|option| match option {\n                        CandidateSource::DefId(did) => infcx.tcx.generics_of(*did).count() == 0,\n                        CandidateSource::ParamEnv(_) => true,\n                    }) {\n                        // If not all are blanket impls, we filter blanked impls out.\n                        ambiguities.retain(|option| match option {\n                            CandidateSource::DefId(did) => infcx.tcx.generics_of(*did).count() == 0,\n                            CandidateSource::ParamEnv(_) => true,\n                        });\n                    }\n                }\n                if ambiguities.len() > 1 && ambiguities.len() < 10 && has_non_region_infer {\n                    if let Some(e) = self.tainted_by_errors()\n                        && term.is_none()\n                    {\n                        // If `arg.is_none()`, then this is probably two param-env\n                        // candidates or impl candidates that are equal modulo lifetimes.\n                        // Therefore, if we've already emitted an error, just skip this\n                        // one, since it's not particularly actionable.\n                        err.cancel();\n                        return e;\n                    }\n                    self.annotate_source_of_ambiguity(&mut err, &ambiguities, predicate);\n                } else {\n                    if let Some(e) = self.tainted_by_errors() {\n                        err.cancel();\n                        return e;\n                    }\n                    let pred = self.tcx.short_string(predicate, &mut err.long_ty_path());\n                    err.note(format!(\"cannot satisfy `{pred}`\"));\n                    let impl_candidates =\n                        self.find_similar_impl_candidates(predicate.as_trait_clause().unwrap());\n                    if impl_candidates.len() < 40 {\n                        self.report_similar_impl_candidates(\n                            impl_candidates.as_slice(),\n                            trait_pred,\n                            obligation.cause.body_id,\n                            &mut err,\n                            false,\n                            obligation.param_env,\n                        );\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if let ObligationCauseCode::WhereClause(def_id, _)\n                | ObligationCauseCode::WhereClauseInExpr(def_id, ..) = *obligation.cause.code()\n                {\n                    self.suggest_fully_qualified_path(&mut err, def_id, span, trait_pred.def_id());\n                }\n\n                if term.is_some_and(|term| term.as_type().is_some())\n                    && let Some(body) = self.tcx.hir_maybe_body_owned_by(obligation.cause.body_id)\n                {\n                    let mut expr_finder = FindExprBySpan::new(span, self.tcx);\n                    expr_finder.visit_expr(&body.value);\n\n                    if let Some(hir::Expr {\n                        kind:\n                            hir::ExprKind::Call(\n                                hir::Expr {\n                                    kind: hir::ExprKind::Path(hir::QPath::Resolved(None, path)),\n                                    ..\n                                },\n                                _,\n                            )\n                            | hir::ExprKind::Path(hir::QPath::Resolved(None, path)),\n                        ..\n                    }) = expr_finder.result\n                        && let [\n                            ..,\n                            trait_path_segment @ hir::PathSegment {\n                                res: Res::Def(DefKind::Trait, trait_id),\n                                ..\n                            },\n                            hir::PathSegment {\n                                ident: assoc_item_ident,\n                                res: Res::Def(_, item_id),\n                                ..\n                            },\n                        ] = path.segments\n                        && data.trait_ref.def_id == *trait_id\n                        && self.tcx.trait_of_assoc(*item_id) == Some(*trait_id)\n                        && let None = self.tainted_by_errors()\n                    {\n                        let assoc_item = self.tcx.associated_item(item_id);\n                        let (verb, noun) = match assoc_item.kind {\n                            ty::AssocKind::Const { .. } => (\"refer to the\", \"constant\"),\n                            ty::AssocKind::Fn { .. } => (\"call\", \"function\"),\n                            // This is already covered by E0223, but this following single match\n                            // arm doesn't hurt here.\n                            ty::AssocKind::Type { .. } => (\"refer to the\", \"type\"),\n                        };\n\n                        // Replace the more general E0283 with a more specific error\n                        err.cancel();\n                        err = self.dcx().struct_span_err(\n                            span,\n                            format!(\n                                \"cannot {verb} associated {noun} on trait without specifying the \\\n                                 corresponding `impl` type\",\n                            ),\n                        );\n                        err.code(E0790);\n\n                        if item_id.is_local() {\n                            let trait_ident = self.tcx.item_name(*trait_id);\n                            err.span_label(\n                                self.tcx.def_span(*item_id),\n                                format!(\"`{trait_ident}::{assoc_item_ident}` defined here\"),\n                            );\n                        }\n\n                        err.span_label(span, format!(\"cannot {verb} associated {noun} of trait\"));\n\n                        let trait_impls = self.tcx.trait_impls_of(data.trait_ref.def_id);\n\n                        if let Some(impl_def_id) =\n                            trait_impls.non_blanket_impls().values().flatten().next()\n                        {\n                            let non_blanket_impl_count =\n                                trait_impls.non_blanket_impls().values().flatten().count();\n                            // If there is only one implementation of the trait, suggest using it.\n                            // Otherwise, use a placeholder comment for the implementation.\n                            let (message, self_types) = if non_blanket_impl_count == 1 {\n                                (\n                                    \"use the fully-qualified path to the only available \\\n                                     implementation\",\n                                    vec![format!(\n                                        \"{}\",\n                                        self.tcx.type_of(impl_def_id).instantiate_identity()\n                                    )],\n                                )\n                            } else if non_blanket_impl_count < 20 {\n                                (\n                                    \"use a fully-qualified path to one of the available \\\n                                     implementations\",\n                                    trait_impls\n                                        .non_blanket_impls()\n                                        .values()\n                                        .flatten()\n                                        .map(|id| {\n                                            format!(\n                                                \"{}\",\n                                                self.tcx.type_of(id).instantiate_identity()\n                                            )\n                                        })\n                                        .collect::<Vec<String>>(),\n                                )\n                            } else {\n                                (\n                                    \"use a fully-qualified path to a specific available \\\n                                     implementation\",\n                                    vec![\"/* self type */\".to_string()],\n                                )\n                            };\n                            let suggestions: Vec<_> = self_types\n                                .into_iter()\n                                .map(|self_type| {\n                                    let mut suggestions = vec![(\n                                        path.span.shrink_to_lo(),\n                                        format!(\"<{self_type} as \"),\n                                    )];\n                                    if let Some(generic_arg) = trait_path_segment.args {\n                                        let between_span = trait_path_segment\n                                            .ident\n                                            .span\n                                            .between(generic_arg.span_ext);\n                                        // get rid of :: between Trait and <type>\n                                        // must be '::' between them, otherwise the parser won't accept the code\n                                        suggestions.push((between_span, \"\".to_string()));\n                                        suggestions.push((\n                                            generic_arg.span_ext.shrink_to_hi(),\n                                            \">\".to_string(),\n                                        ));\n                                    } else {\n                                        suggestions.push((\n                                            trait_path_segment.ident.span.shrink_to_hi(),\n                                            \">\".to_string(),\n                                        ));\n                                    }\n                                    suggestions\n                                })\n                                .collect();\n                            err.multipart_suggestions(\n                                message,\n                                suggestions,\n                                Applicability::MaybeIncorrect,\n                            );\n                        }\n                    }\n                };\n\n                err\n            }\n\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::WellFormed(term)) => {\n                // Same hacky approach as above to avoid deluging user\n                // with error messages.\n\n                if let Err(e) = term.error_reported() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n                if let Some(e) = self.tainted_by_errors() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n\n                self.emit_inference_failure_err(\n                    obligation.cause.body_id,\n                    span,\n                    term,\n                    TypeAnnotationNeeded::E0282,\n                    false,\n                )\n            }\n\n            ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(data) => {\n                if let Err(e) = data.error_reported() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n                if let Some(e) = self.tainted_by_errors() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n                let ty::SubtypeRelation { a_is_expected: _, a, b } = data;\n                // both must be type variables, or the other would've been instantiated\n                assert!(a.is_ty_var() && b.is_ty_var());\n                self.emit_inference_failure_err(\n                    obligation.cause.body_id,\n                    span,\n                    a.into(),\n                    TypeAnnotationNeeded::E0282,\n                    true,\n                )\n            }\n\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::Projection(data)) => {\n                if let Err(e) = predicate.error_reported() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n                if let Some(e) = self.tainted_by_errors() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n\n                if let Err(guar) = self\n                    .tcx\n                    .ensure_ok()\n                    .coherent_trait(self.tcx.parent(data.projection_term.def_id))\n                {\n                    // Avoid bogus \"type annotations needed `Foo: Bar`\" errors on `impl Bar for Foo` in case\n                    // other `Foo` impls are incoherent.\n                    return guar;\n                }\n                let term = data\n                    .projection_term\n                    .args\n                    .iter()\n                    .filter_map(ty::GenericArg::as_term)\n                    .chain([data.term])\n                    .find(|g| g.has_non_region_infer());\n                let predicate = self.tcx.short_string(predicate, &mut long_ty_path);\n                if let Some(term) = term {\n                    self.emit_inference_failure_err(\n                        obligation.cause.body_id,\n                        span,\n                        term,\n                        TypeAnnotationNeeded::E0284,\n                        true,\n                    )\n                    .with_note(format!(\"cannot satisfy `{predicate}`\"))\n                    .with_long_ty_path(long_ty_path)\n                } else {\n                    // If we can't find a generic parameter, just print a generic error\n                    struct_span_code_err!(\n                        self.dcx(),\n                        span,\n                        E0284,\n                        \"type annotations needed: cannot satisfy `{predicate}`\",\n                    )\n                    .with_span_label(span, format!(\"cannot satisfy `{predicate}`\"))\n                    .with_long_ty_path(long_ty_path)\n                }\n            }\n\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::ConstEvaluatable(data)) => {\n                if let Err(e) = predicate.error_reported() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n                if let Some(e) = self.tainted_by_errors() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n                let term =\n                    data.walk().filter_map(ty::GenericArg::as_term).find(|term| term.is_infer());\n                if let Some(term) = term {\n                    self.emit_inference_failure_err(\n                        obligation.cause.body_id,\n                        span,\n                        term,\n                        TypeAnnotationNeeded::E0284,\n                        true,\n                    )\n                } else {\n                    // If we can't find a generic parameter, just print a generic error\n                    let predicate = self.tcx.short_string(predicate, &mut long_ty_path);\n                    struct_span_code_err!(\n                        self.dcx(),\n                        span,\n                        E0284,\n                        \"type annotations needed: cannot satisfy `{predicate}`\",\n                    )\n                    .with_span_label(span, format!(\"cannot satisfy `{predicate}`\"))\n                    .with_long_ty_path(long_ty_path)\n                }\n            }\n\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::ConstArgHasType(ct, ..)) => self\n                .emit_inference_failure_err(\n                    obligation.cause.body_id,\n                    span,\n                    ct.into(),\n                    TypeAnnotationNeeded::E0284,\n                    true,\n                ),\n\n            ty::PredicateKind::NormalizesTo(ty::NormalizesTo { alias, term })\n                if term.is_infer() =>\n            {\n                if let Some(e) = self.tainted_by_errors() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n                let alias = self.tcx.short_string(alias, &mut long_ty_path);\n                struct_span_code_err!(\n                    self.dcx(),\n                    span,\n                    E0284,\n                    \"type annotations needed: cannot normalize `{alias}`\",\n                )\n                .with_span_label(span, format!(\"cannot normalize `{alias}`\"))\n                .with_long_ty_path(long_ty_path)\n            }\n\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::UnstableFeature(sym)) => {\n                if let Some(e) = self.tainted_by_errors() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n\n                if self.tcx.features().staged_api() {\n                    self.dcx().struct_span_err(\n                        span,\n                        format!(\"unstable feature `{sym}` is used without being enabled.\"),\n                    ).with_help(format!(\"The feature can be enabled by marking the current item with `#[unstable_feature_bound({sym})]`\"))\n                } else {\n                    feature_err_unstable_feature_bound(\n                        &self.tcx.sess,\n                        sym,\n                        span,\n                        format!(\"use of unstable library feature `{sym}`\"),\n                    )\n                }\n            }\n\n            _ => {\n                if let Some(e) = self.tainted_by_errors() {\n                    return e;\n                }\n                let predicate = self.tcx.short_string(predicate, &mut long_ty_path);\n                struct_span_code_err!(\n                    self.dcx(),\n                    span,\n                    E0284,\n                    \"type annotations needed: cannot satisfy `{predicate}`\",\n                )\n                .with_span_label(span, format!(\"cannot satisfy `{predicate}`\"))\n                .with_long_ty_path(long_ty_path)\n            }\n        };\n        self.note_obligation_cause(&mut err, obligation);\n        err.emit()\n    }\n\n    fn annotate_source_of_ambiguity(\n        &self,\n        err: &mut Diag<'_>,\n        ambiguities: &[CandidateSource],\n        predicate: ty::Predicate<'tcx>,\n    ) {\n        let mut spans = vec![];\n        let mut crates = vec![];\n        let mut post = vec![];\n        let mut has_param_env = false;\n        for ambiguity in ambiguities {\n            match ambiguity {\n                CandidateSource::DefId(impl_def_id) => match self.tcx.span_of_impl(*impl_def_id) {\n                    Ok(span) => spans.push(span),\n                    Err(name) => {\n                        crates.push(name);\n                        if let Some(header) = to_pretty_impl_header(self.tcx, *impl_def_id) {\n                            post.push(header);\n                        }\n                    }\n                },\n                CandidateSource::ParamEnv(span) => {\n                    has_param_env = true;\n                    spans.push(*span);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        let mut crate_names: Vec<_> = crates.iter().map(|n| format!(\"`{n}`\")).collect();\n        crate_names.sort();\n        crate_names.dedup();\n        post.sort();\n        post.dedup();\n\n        if self.tainted_by_errors().is_some()\n            && (crate_names.len() == 1\n                && spans.len() == 0\n                && [\"`core`\", \"`alloc`\", \"`std`\"].contains(&crate_names[0].as_str())\n                || predicate.visit_with(&mut HasNumericInferVisitor).is_break())\n        {\n            // Avoid complaining about other inference issues for expressions like\n            // `42 >> 1`, where the types are still `{integer}`, but we want to\n            // Do we need `trait_ref.skip_binder().self_ty().is_numeric() &&` too?\n            // NOTE(eddyb) this was `.cancel()`, but `err`\n            // is borrowed, so we can't fully defuse it.\n            err.downgrade_to_delayed_bug();\n            return;\n        }\n\n        let msg = format!(\n            \"multiple `impl`s{} satisfying `{}` found\",\n            if has_param_env { \" or `where` clauses\" } else { \"\" },\n            predicate\n        );\n        let post = if post.len() > 1 || (post.len() == 1 && post[0].contains('\\n')) {\n            format!(\":\\n{}\", post.iter().map(|p| format!(\"- {p}\")).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(\"\\n\"),)\n        } else if post.len() == 1 {\n            format!(\": `{}`\", post[0])\n        } else {\n            String::new()\n        };\n\n        match (spans.len(), crates.len(), crate_names.len()) {\n            (0, 0, 0) => {\n                err.note(format!(\"cannot satisfy `{predicate}`\"));\n            }\n            (0, _, 1) => {\n                err.note(format!(\"{} in the `{}` crate{}\", msg, crates[0], post,));\n            }\n            (0, _, _) => {\n                err.note(format!(\n                    \"{} in the following crates: {}{}\",\n                    msg,\n                    crate_names.join(\", \"),\n                    post,\n                ));\n            }\n            (_, 0, 0) => {\n                let span: MultiSpan = spans.into();\n                err.span_note(span, msg);\n            }\n            (_, 1, 1) => {\n                let span: MultiSpan = spans.into();\n                err.span_note(span, msg);\n                err.note(format!(\"and another `impl` found in the `{}` crate{}\", crates[0], post,));\n            }\n            _ => {\n                let span: MultiSpan = spans.into();\n                err.span_note(span, msg);\n                err.note(format!(\n                    \"and more `impl`s found in the following crates: {}{}\",\n                    crate_names.join(\", \"),\n                    post,\n                ));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nstruct HasNumericInferVisitor;\n\nimpl<'tcx> ty::TypeVisitor<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for HasNumericInferVisitor {\n    type Result = ControlFlow<()>;\n\n    fn visit_ty(&mut self, ty: Ty<'tcx>) -> Self::Result {\n        if matches!(ty.kind(), ty::Infer(ty::FloatVar(_) | ty::IntVar(_))) {\n            ControlFlow::Break(())\n        } else {\n            ControlFlow::Continue(())\n        }\n    }\n}\n",

  "newString": "            ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation { a_is_expected: _, sub_ty, super_ty }) => {\n                self.add_ty(sub_ty);\n                self.add_ty(super_ty);\n            }",
  "oldString": "            ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation { a_is_expected: _, a, b }) => {\n                self.add_ty(a);\n                self.add_ty(b);\n            }",
  "originalFile": "use crate::inherent::*;\nuse crate::visit::Flags;\nuse crate::{self as ty, Interner};\n\nbitflags::bitflags! {\n    /// Flags that we track on types. These flags are propagated upwards\n    /// through the type during type construction, so that we can quickly check\n    /// whether the type has various kinds of types in it without recursing\n    /// over the type itself.\n    #[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Clone, Copy)]\n    pub struct TypeFlags: u32 {\n        // Does this have parameters? Used to determine whether instantiation is\n        // required.\n        /// Does this have `Param`?\n        const HAS_TY_PARAM                = 1 << 0;\n        /// Does this have `ReEarlyParam`?\n        const HAS_RE_PARAM                = 1 << 1;\n        /// Does this have `ConstKind::Param`?\n        const HAS_CT_PARAM                = 1 << 2;\n\n        const HAS_PARAM                   = TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PARAM.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_RE_PARAM.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PARAM.bits();\n\n        /// Does this have `Infer`?\n        const HAS_TY_INFER                = 1 << 3;\n        /// Does this have `ReVar`?\n        const HAS_RE_INFER                = 1 << 4;\n        /// Does this have `ConstKind::Infer`?\n        const HAS_CT_INFER                = 1 << 5;\n\n        /// Does this have inference variables? Used to determine whether\n        /// inference is required.\n        const HAS_INFER                   = TypeFlags::HAS_TY_INFER.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_RE_INFER.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_INFER.bits();\n\n        /// Does this have `Placeholder`?\n        const HAS_TY_PLACEHOLDER          = 1 << 6;\n        /// Does this have `RePlaceholder`?\n        const HAS_RE_PLACEHOLDER          = 1 << 7;\n        /// Does this have `ConstKind::Placeholder`?\n        const HAS_CT_PLACEHOLDER          = 1 << 8;\n\n        /// Does this have placeholders?\n        const HAS_PLACEHOLDER             = TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PLACEHOLDER.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_RE_PLACEHOLDER.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PLACEHOLDER.bits();\n\n        /// `true` if there are \"names\" of regions and so forth\n        /// that are local to a particular fn/inferctxt\n        const HAS_FREE_LOCAL_REGIONS      = 1 << 9;\n\n        /// `true` if there are \"names\" of types and regions and so forth\n        /// that are local to a particular fn\n        const HAS_FREE_LOCAL_NAMES        = TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PARAM.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PARAM.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_TY_INFER.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_INFER.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PLACEHOLDER.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PLACEHOLDER.bits()\n                                          // We consider 'freshened' types and constants\n                                          // to depend on a particular fn.\n                                          // The freshening process throws away information,\n                                          // which can make things unsuitable for use in a global\n                                          // cache. Note that there is no 'fresh lifetime' flag -\n                                          // freshening replaces all lifetimes with `ReErased`,\n                                          // which is different from how types/const are freshened.\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_TY_FRESH.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_FRESH.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_FREE_LOCAL_REGIONS.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_RE_ERASED.bits();\n\n        /// Does this have `Projection`?\n        const HAS_TY_PROJECTION           = 1 << 10;\n        /// Does this have `Free` aliases?\n        const HAS_TY_FREE_ALIAS                 = 1 << 11;\n        /// Does this have `Opaque`?\n        const HAS_TY_OPAQUE               = 1 << 12;\n        /// Does this have `Inherent`?\n        const HAS_TY_INHERENT             = 1 << 13;\n        /// Does this have `ConstKind::Unevaluated`?\n        const HAS_CT_PROJECTION           = 1 << 14;\n\n        /// Does this have `Alias` or `ConstKind::Unevaluated`?\n        ///\n        /// Rephrased, could this term be normalized further?\n        const HAS_ALIAS                   = TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PROJECTION.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_TY_FREE_ALIAS.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_TY_OPAQUE.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_TY_INHERENT.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PROJECTION.bits();\n\n        /// Is an error type/lifetime/const reachable?\n        const HAS_ERROR                   = 1 << 15;\n\n        /// Does this have any region that \"appears free\" in the type?\n        /// Basically anything but `ReBound` and `ReErased`.\n        const HAS_FREE_REGIONS            = 1 << 16;\n\n        /// Does this have any `ReBound` regions?\n        const HAS_RE_BOUND                = 1 << 17;\n        /// Does this have any `Bound` types?\n        const HAS_TY_BOUND                = 1 << 18;\n        /// Does this have any `ConstKind::Bound` consts?\n        const HAS_CT_BOUND                = 1 << 19;\n        /// Does this have any bound variables?\n        /// Used to check if a global bound is safe to evaluate.\n        const HAS_BOUND_VARS              = TypeFlags::HAS_RE_BOUND.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_TY_BOUND.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_BOUND.bits();\n\n        /// Does this have any `ReErased` regions?\n        const HAS_RE_ERASED               = 1 << 20;\n\n        /// Does this value have parameters/placeholders/inference variables which could be\n        /// replaced later, in a way that would change the results of `impl` specialization?\n        const STILL_FURTHER_SPECIALIZABLE = TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PARAM.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PLACEHOLDER.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_TY_INFER.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PARAM.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PLACEHOLDER.bits()\n                                          | TypeFlags::HAS_CT_INFER.bits();\n\n        /// Does this value have `InferTy::FreshTy/FreshIntTy/FreshFloatTy`?\n        const HAS_TY_FRESH                = 1 << 21;\n\n        /// Does this value have `InferConst::Fresh`?\n        const HAS_CT_FRESH                = 1 << 22;\n\n        /// Does this have any binders with bound vars (e.g. that need to be anonymized)?\n        const HAS_BINDER_VARS             = 1 << 23;\n\n        /// Does this type have any coroutines in it?\n        const HAS_TY_CORO                 = 1 << 24;\n\n        /// Does this have have a `Bound(BoundVarIndexKind::Canonical, _)`?\n        const HAS_CANONICAL_BOUND      = 1 << 25;\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct FlagComputation<I> {\n    pub flags: TypeFlags,\n\n    /// see `Ty::outer_exclusive_binder` for details\n    pub outer_exclusive_binder: ty::DebruijnIndex,\n\n    interner: std::marker::PhantomData<I>,\n}\n\nimpl<I: Interner> FlagComputation<I> {\n    fn new() -> FlagComputation<I> {\n        FlagComputation {\n            flags: TypeFlags::empty(),\n            outer_exclusive_binder: ty::INNERMOST,\n            interner: std::marker::PhantomData,\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[allow(rustc::usage_of_ty_tykind)]\n    pub fn for_kind(kind: &ty::TyKind<I>) -> FlagComputation<I> {\n        let mut result = FlagComputation::new();\n        result.add_kind(kind);\n        result\n    }\n\n    pub fn for_predicate(binder: ty::Binder<I, ty::PredicateKind<I>>) -> FlagComputation<I> {\n        let mut result = FlagComputation::new();\n        result.add_predicate(binder);\n        result\n    }\n\n    pub fn for_const_kind(kind: &ty::ConstKind<I>) -> FlagComputation<I> {\n        let mut result = FlagComputation::new();\n        result.add_const_kind(kind);\n        result\n    }\n\n    pub fn for_clauses(clauses: &[I::Clause]) -> FlagComputation<I> {\n        let mut result = FlagComputation::new();\n        for c in clauses {\n            result.add_flags(c.as_predicate().flags());\n            result.add_exclusive_binder(c.as_predicate().outer_exclusive_binder());\n        }\n        result\n    }\n\n    fn add_flags(&mut self, flags: TypeFlags) {\n        self.flags = self.flags | flags;\n    }\n\n    /// indicates that `self` refers to something at binding level `binder`\n    fn add_bound_var(&mut self, binder: ty::DebruijnIndex) {\n        let exclusive_binder = binder.shifted_in(1);\n        self.add_exclusive_binder(exclusive_binder);\n    }\n\n    /// indicates that `self` refers to something *inside* binding\n    /// level `binder` -- not bound by `binder`, but bound by the next\n    /// binder internal to it\n    fn add_exclusive_binder(&mut self, exclusive_binder: ty::DebruijnIndex) {\n        self.outer_exclusive_binder = self.outer_exclusive_binder.max(exclusive_binder);\n    }\n\n    /// Adds the flags/depth from a set of types that appear within the current type, but within a\n    /// region binder.\n    fn bound_computation<T, F>(&mut self, value: ty::Binder<I, T>, f: F)\n    where\n        F: FnOnce(&mut Self, T),\n    {\n        let mut computation = FlagComputation::new();\n\n        if !value.bound_vars().is_empty() {\n            computation.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_BINDER_VARS);\n        }\n\n        f(&mut computation, value.skip_binder());\n\n        self.add_flags(computation.flags);\n\n        // The types that contributed to `computation` occurred within\n        // a region binder, so subtract one from the region depth\n        // within when adding the depth to `self`.\n        let outer_exclusive_binder = computation.outer_exclusive_binder;\n        if outer_exclusive_binder > ty::INNERMOST {\n            self.add_exclusive_binder(outer_exclusive_binder.shifted_out(1));\n        } // otherwise, this binder captures nothing\n    }\n\n    #[allow(rustc::usage_of_ty_tykind)]\n    fn add_kind(&mut self, kind: &ty::TyKind<I>) {\n        match *kind {\n            ty::Bool\n            | ty::Char\n            | ty::Int(_)\n            | ty::Float(_)\n            | ty::Uint(_)\n            | ty::Never\n            | ty::Str\n            | ty::Foreign(..) => {}\n\n            ty::Error(_) => self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_ERROR),\n\n            ty::Param(_) => {\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PARAM);\n            }\n\n            ty::Closure(_, args)\n            | ty::CoroutineClosure(_, args)\n            | ty::CoroutineWitness(_, args) => {\n                self.add_args(args.as_slice());\n            }\n\n            ty::Coroutine(_, args) => {\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_TY_CORO);\n                self.add_args(args.as_slice());\n            }\n\n            ty::Bound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Canonical, _) => {\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_TY_BOUND);\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CANONICAL_BOUND);\n            }\n\n            ty::Bound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Bound(debruijn), _) => {\n                self.add_bound_var(debruijn);\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_TY_BOUND);\n            }\n\n            ty::Placeholder(..) => {\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PLACEHOLDER);\n            }\n\n            ty::Infer(infer) => match infer {\n                ty::FreshTy(_) | ty::FreshIntTy(_) | ty::FreshFloatTy(_) => {\n                    self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_TY_FRESH)\n                }\n\n                ty::TyVar(_) | ty::IntVar(_) | ty::FloatVar(_) => {\n                    self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_TY_INFER)\n                }\n            },\n\n            ty::Adt(_, args) => {\n                self.add_args(args.as_slice());\n            }\n\n            ty::Alias(kind, data) => {\n                self.add_flags(match kind {\n                    ty::Projection => TypeFlags::HAS_TY_PROJECTION,\n                    ty::Free => TypeFlags::HAS_TY_FREE_ALIAS,\n                    ty::Opaque => TypeFlags::HAS_TY_OPAQUE,\n                    ty::Inherent => TypeFlags::HAS_TY_INHERENT,\n                });\n\n                self.add_alias_ty(data);\n            }\n\n            ty::Dynamic(obj, r) => {\n                for predicate in obj.iter() {\n                    self.bound_computation(predicate, |computation, predicate| match predicate {\n                        ty::ExistentialPredicate::Trait(tr) => {\n                            computation.add_args(tr.args.as_slice())\n                        }\n                        ty::ExistentialPredicate::Projection(p) => {\n                            computation.add_existential_projection(&p);\n                        }\n                        ty::ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(_) => {}\n                    });\n                }\n\n                self.add_region(r);\n            }\n\n            ty::Array(tt, len) => {\n                self.add_ty(tt);\n                self.add_const(len);\n            }\n\n            ty::Pat(ty, pat) => {\n                self.add_ty(ty);\n                self.add_ty_pat(pat);\n            }\n\n            ty::Slice(tt) => self.add_ty(tt),\n\n            ty::RawPtr(ty, _) => {\n                self.add_ty(ty);\n            }\n\n            ty::Ref(r, ty, _) => {\n                self.add_region(r);\n                self.add_ty(ty);\n            }\n\n            ty::Tuple(types) => {\n                self.add_tys(types);\n            }\n\n            ty::FnDef(_, args) => {\n                self.add_args(args.as_slice());\n            }\n\n            ty::FnPtr(sig_tys, _) => self.bound_computation(sig_tys, |computation, sig_tys| {\n                computation.add_tys(sig_tys.inputs_and_output);\n            }),\n\n            ty::UnsafeBinder(bound_ty) => {\n                self.bound_computation(bound_ty.into(), |computation, ty| {\n                    computation.add_ty(ty);\n                })\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn add_ty_pat(&mut self, pat: <I as Interner>::Pat) {\n        self.add_flags(pat.flags());\n        self.add_exclusive_binder(pat.outer_exclusive_binder());\n    }\n\n    fn add_predicate(&mut self, binder: ty::Binder<I, ty::PredicateKind<I>>) {\n        self.bound_computation(binder, |computation, atom| computation.add_predicate_atom(atom));\n    }\n\n    fn add_predicate_atom(&mut self, atom: ty::PredicateKind<I>) {\n        match atom {\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::Trait(trait_pred)) => {\n                self.add_args(trait_pred.trait_ref.args.as_slice());\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::HostEffect(ty::HostEffectPredicate {\n                trait_ref,\n                constness: _,\n            })) => {\n                self.add_args(trait_ref.args.as_slice());\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::RegionOutlives(ty::OutlivesPredicate(\n                a,\n                b,\n            ))) => {\n                self.add_region(a);\n                self.add_region(b);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::TypeOutlives(ty::OutlivesPredicate(\n                ty,\n                region,\n            ))) => {\n                self.add_ty(ty);\n                self.add_region(region);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::ConstArgHasType(ct, ty)) => {\n                self.add_const(ct);\n                self.add_ty(ty);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation { a_is_expected: _, a, b }) => {\n                self.add_ty(a);\n                self.add_ty(b);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(ty::CoercePredicate { a, b }) => {\n                self.add_ty(a);\n                self.add_ty(b);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::Projection(ty::ProjectionPredicate {\n                projection_term,\n                term,\n            })) => {\n                self.add_alias_term(projection_term);\n                self.add_term(term);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::WellFormed(term)) => {\n                self.add_term(term);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::DynCompatible(_def_id) => {}\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::ConstEvaluatable(uv)) => {\n                self.add_const(uv);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::ConstEquate(expected, found) => {\n                self.add_const(expected);\n                self.add_const(found);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::NormalizesTo(ty::NormalizesTo { alias, term }) => {\n                self.add_alias_term(alias);\n                self.add_term(term);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(t1, t2, _) => {\n                self.add_term(t1);\n                self.add_term(t2);\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::UnstableFeature(_sym)) => {}\n            ty::PredicateKind::Ambiguous => {}\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn add_ty(&mut self, ty: I::Ty) {\n        self.add_flags(ty.flags());\n        self.add_exclusive_binder(ty.outer_exclusive_binder());\n    }\n\n    fn add_tys(&mut self, tys: I::Tys) {\n        for ty in tys.iter() {\n            self.add_ty(ty);\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn add_region(&mut self, r: I::Region) {\n        self.add_flags(r.flags());\n        if let ty::ReBound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Bound(debruijn), _) = r.kind() {\n            self.add_bound_var(debruijn);\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn add_const(&mut self, c: I::Const) {\n        self.add_flags(c.flags());\n        self.add_exclusive_binder(c.outer_exclusive_binder());\n    }\n\n    fn add_const_kind(&mut self, c: &ty::ConstKind<I>) {\n        match *c {\n            ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(uv) => {\n                self.add_args(uv.args.as_slice());\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PROJECTION);\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Infer(infer) => match infer {\n                ty::InferConst::Fresh(_) => self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CT_FRESH),\n                ty::InferConst::Var(_) => self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CT_INFER),\n            },\n            ty::ConstKind::Bound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Bound(debruijn), _) => {\n                self.add_bound_var(debruijn);\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CT_BOUND);\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Bound(ty::BoundVarIndexKind::Canonical, _) => {\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CT_BOUND);\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CANONICAL_BOUND);\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Param(_) => {\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PARAM);\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Placeholder(_) => {\n                self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_CT_PLACEHOLDER);\n            }\n            ty::ConstKind::Value(cv) => self.add_ty(cv.ty()),\n            ty::ConstKind::Expr(e) => self.add_args(e.args().as_slice()),\n            ty::ConstKind::Error(_) => self.add_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_ERROR),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn add_existential_projection(&mut self, projection: &ty::ExistentialProjection<I>) {\n        self.add_args(projection.args.as_slice());\n        match projection.term.kind() {\n            ty::TermKind::Ty(ty) => self.add_ty(ty),\n            ty::TermKind::Const(ct) => self.add_const(ct),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn add_alias_ty(&mut self, alias_ty: ty::AliasTy<I>) {\n        self.add_args(alias_ty.args.as_slice());\n    }\n\n    fn add_alias_term(&mut self, alias_term: ty::AliasTerm<I>) {\n        self.add_args(alias_term.args.as_slice());\n    }\n\n    fn add_args(&mut self, args: &[I::GenericArg]) {\n        for arg in args {\n            match arg.kind() {\n                ty::GenericArgKind::Type(ty) => self.add_ty(ty),\n                ty::GenericArgKind::Lifetime(lt) => self.add_region(lt),\n                ty::GenericArgKind::Const(ct) => self.add_const(ct),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn add_term(&mut self, term: I::Term) {\n        match term.kind() {\n            ty::TermKind::Ty(ty) => self.add_ty(ty),\n            ty::TermKind::Const(ct) => self.add_const(ct),\n        }\n    }\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [

  "originalFile": "use std::ops::Deref;\n\nuse rustc_data_structures::fx::FxHashSet;\nuse rustc_hir::LangItem;\nuse rustc_hir::def_id::{CRATE_DEF_ID, DefId};\nuse rustc_infer::infer::canonical::query_response::make_query_region_constraints;\nuse rustc_infer::infer::canonical::{\n    Canonical, CanonicalExt as _, CanonicalQueryInput, CanonicalVarKind, CanonicalVarValues,\n};\nuse rustc_infer::infer::{InferCtxt, RegionVariableOrigin, SubregionOrigin, TyCtxtInferExt};\nuse rustc_infer::traits::solve::Goal;\nuse rustc_middle::traits::query::NoSolution;\nuse rustc_middle::traits::solve::Certainty;\nuse rustc_middle::ty::{\n    self, Ty, TyCtxt, TypeFlags, TypeFoldable, TypeVisitableExt as _, TypingMode,\n};\nuse rustc_span::{DUMMY_SP, ErrorGuaranteed, Span};\n\nuse crate::traits::{EvaluateConstErr, ObligationCause, sizedness_fast_path, specialization_graph};\n\n#[repr(transparent)]\npub struct SolverDelegate<'tcx>(InferCtxt<'tcx>);\n\nimpl<'a, 'tcx> From<&'a InferCtxt<'tcx>> for &'a SolverDelegate<'tcx> {\n    fn from(infcx: &'a InferCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\n        // SAFETY: `repr(transparent)`\n        unsafe { std::mem::transmute(infcx) }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Deref for SolverDelegate<'tcx> {\n    type Target = InferCtxt<'tcx>;\n\n    fn deref(&self) -> &Self::Target {\n        &self.0\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> rustc_next_trait_solver::delegate::SolverDelegate for SolverDelegate<'tcx> {\n    type Infcx = InferCtxt<'tcx>;\n    type Interner = TyCtxt<'tcx>;\n\n    fn cx(&self) -> TyCtxt<'tcx> {\n        self.0.tcx\n    }\n\n    fn build_with_canonical<V>(\n        interner: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n        canonical: &CanonicalQueryInput<'tcx, V>,\n    ) -> (Self, V, CanonicalVarValues<'tcx>)\n    where\n        V: TypeFoldable<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        let (infcx, value, vars) = interner\n            .infer_ctxt()\n            .with_next_trait_solver(true)\n            .build_with_canonical(DUMMY_SP, canonical);\n        (SolverDelegate(infcx), value, vars)\n    }\n\n    fn compute_goal_fast_path(\n        &self,\n        goal: Goal<'tcx, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>,\n        span: Span,\n    ) -> Option<Certainty> {\n        if let Some(trait_pred) = goal.predicate.as_trait_clause() {\n            if self.shallow_resolve(trait_pred.self_ty().skip_binder()).is_ty_var()\n                // We don't do this fast path when opaques are defined since we may\n                // eventually use opaques to incompletely guide inference via ty var\n                // self types.\n                // FIXME: Properly consider opaques here.\n                && self.inner.borrow_mut().opaque_types().is_empty()\n            {\n                return Some(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS);\n            }\n\n            if trait_pred.polarity() == ty::PredicatePolarity::Positive {\n                match self.0.tcx.as_lang_item(trait_pred.def_id()) {\n                    Some(LangItem::Sized) | Some(LangItem::MetaSized) => {\n                        let predicate = self.resolve_vars_if_possible(goal.predicate);\n                        if sizedness_fast_path(self.tcx, predicate, goal.param_env) {\n                            return Some(Certainty::Yes);\n                        }\n                    }\n                    Some(LangItem::Copy | LangItem::Clone) => {\n                        let self_ty =\n                            self.resolve_vars_if_possible(trait_pred.self_ty().skip_binder());\n                        // Unlike `Sized` traits, which always prefer the built-in impl,\n                        // `Copy`/`Clone` may be shadowed by a param-env candidate which\n                        // could force a lifetime error or guide inference. While that's\n                        // not generally desirable, it is observable, so for now let's\n                        // ignore this fast path for types that have regions or infer.\n                        if !self_ty\n                            .has_type_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_FREE_REGIONS | TypeFlags::HAS_INFER)\n                            && self_ty.is_trivially_pure_clone_copy()\n                        {\n                            return Some(Certainty::Yes);\n                        }\n                    }\n                    _ => {}\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        let pred = goal.predicate.kind();\n        match pred.no_bound_vars()? {\n            ty::PredicateKind::DynCompatible(def_id) if self.0.tcx.is_dyn_compatible(def_id) => {\n                Some(Certainty::Yes)\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::RegionOutlives(outlives)) => {\n                self.0.sub_regions(\n                    SubregionOrigin::RelateRegionParamBound(span, None),\n                    outlives.1,\n                    outlives.0,\n                );\n                Some(Certainty::Yes)\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::TypeOutlives(outlives)) => {\n                self.0.register_type_outlives_constraint(\n                    outlives.0,\n                    outlives.1,\n                    &ObligationCause::dummy_with_span(span),\n                );\n\n                Some(Certainty::Yes)\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation { a, b, .. })\n            | ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(ty::CoercePredicate { a, b }) => {\n                match (self.shallow_resolve(a).kind(), self.shallow_resolve(b).kind()) {\n                    (&ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_vid)), &ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                        self.sub_unify_ty_vids_raw(a_vid, b_vid);\n                        Some(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)\n                    }\n                    _ => None,\n                }\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::ConstArgHasType(ct, _)) => {\n                if self.shallow_resolve_const(ct).is_ct_infer() {\n                    Some(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)\n                } else {\n                    None\n                }\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::WellFormed(arg)) => {\n                let arg = self.shallow_resolve_term(arg);\n                if arg.is_trivially_wf(self.tcx) {\n                    Some(Certainty::Yes)\n                } else if arg.is_infer() {\n                    Some(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)\n                } else {\n                    None\n                }\n            }\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn fresh_var_for_kind_with_span(\n        &self,\n        arg: ty::GenericArg<'tcx>,\n        span: Span,\n    ) -> ty::GenericArg<'tcx> {\n        match arg.kind() {\n            ty::GenericArgKind::Lifetime(_) => {\n                self.next_region_var(RegionVariableOrigin::Misc(span)).into()\n            }\n            ty::GenericArgKind::Type(_) => self.next_ty_var(span).into(),\n            ty::GenericArgKind::Const(_) => self.next_const_var(span).into(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn leak_check(&self, max_input_universe: ty::UniverseIndex) -> Result<(), NoSolution> {\n        self.0.leak_check(max_input_universe, None).map_err(|_| NoSolution)\n    }\n\n    fn evaluate_const(\n        &self,\n        param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n        uv: ty::UnevaluatedConst<'tcx>,\n    ) -> Option<ty::Const<'tcx>> {\n        let ct = ty::Const::new_unevaluated(self.tcx, uv);\n\n        match crate::traits::try_evaluate_const(&self.0, ct, param_env) {\n            Ok(ct) => Some(ct),\n            Err(EvaluateConstErr::EvaluationFailure(e)) => Some(ty::Const::new_error(self.tcx, e)),\n            Err(\n                EvaluateConstErr::InvalidConstParamTy(_) | EvaluateConstErr::HasGenericsOrInfers,\n            ) => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn well_formed_goals(\n        &self,\n        param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n        term: ty::Term<'tcx>,\n    ) -> Option<Vec<Goal<'tcx, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>> {\n        crate::traits::wf::unnormalized_obligations(\n            &self.0,\n            param_env,\n            term,\n            DUMMY_SP,\n            CRATE_DEF_ID,\n        )\n        .map(|obligations| obligations.into_iter().map(|obligation| obligation.as_goal()).collect())\n    }\n\n    fn make_deduplicated_outlives_constraints(&self) -> Vec<ty::ArgOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>> {\n        // Cannot use `take_registered_region_obligations` as we may compute the response\n        // inside of a `probe` whenever we have multiple choices inside of the solver.\n        let region_obligations = self.0.inner.borrow().region_obligations().to_owned();\n        let region_assumptions = self.0.inner.borrow().region_assumptions().to_owned();\n        let region_constraints = self.0.with_region_constraints(|region_constraints| {\n            make_query_region_constraints(\n                region_obligations,\n                region_constraints,\n                region_assumptions,\n            )\n        });\n\n        let mut seen = FxHashSet::default();\n        region_constraints\n            .outlives\n            .into_iter()\n            .filter(|&(outlives, _)| seen.insert(outlives))\n            .map(|(outlives, _)| outlives)\n            .collect()\n    }\n\n    fn instantiate_canonical<V>(\n        &self,\n        canonical: Canonical<'tcx, V>,\n        values: CanonicalVarValues<'tcx>,\n    ) -> V\n    where\n        V: TypeFoldable<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        canonical.instantiate(self.tcx, &values)\n    }\n\n    fn instantiate_canonical_var(\n        &self,\n        kind: CanonicalVarKind<'tcx>,\n        span: Span,\n        var_values: &[ty::GenericArg<'tcx>],\n        universe_map: impl Fn(ty::UniverseIndex) -> ty::UniverseIndex,\n    ) -> ty::GenericArg<'tcx> {\n        self.0.instantiate_canonical_var(span, kind, var_values, universe_map)\n    }\n\n    fn add_item_bounds_for_hidden_type(\n        &self,\n        def_id: DefId,\n        args: ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>,\n        param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n        hidden_ty: Ty<'tcx>,\n        goals: &mut Vec<Goal<'tcx, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>,\n    ) {\n        self.0.add_item_bounds_for_hidden_type(def_id, args, param_env, hidden_ty, goals);\n    }\n\n    fn fetch_eligible_assoc_item(\n        &self,\n        goal_trait_ref: ty::TraitRef<'tcx>,\n        trait_assoc_def_id: DefId,\n        impl_def_id: DefId,\n    ) -> Result<Option<DefId>, ErrorGuaranteed> {\n        let node_item = specialization_graph::assoc_def(self.tcx, impl_def_id, trait_assoc_def_id)?;\n\n        let eligible = if node_item.is_final() {\n            // Non-specializable items are always projectable.\n            true\n        } else {\n            // Only reveal a specializable default if we're past type-checking\n            // and the obligation is monomorphic, otherwise passes such as\n            // transmute checking and polymorphic MIR optimizations could\n            // get a result which isn't correct for all monomorphizations.\n            match self.typing_mode() {\n                TypingMode::Coherence\n                | TypingMode::Analysis { .. }\n                | TypingMode::Borrowck { .. }\n                | TypingMode::PostBorrowckAnalysis { .. } => false,\n                TypingMode::PostAnalysis => {\n                    let poly_trait_ref = self.resolve_vars_if_possible(goal_trait_ref);\n                    !poly_trait_ref.still_further_specializable()\n                }\n            }\n        };\n\n        // FIXME: Check for defaultness here may cause diagnostics problems.\n        if eligible { Ok(Some(node_item.item.def_id)) } else { Ok(None) }\n    }\n\n    // FIXME: This actually should destructure the `Result` we get from transmutability and\n    // register candidates. We probably need to register >1 since we may have an OR of ANDs.\n    fn is_transmutable(\n        &self,\n        dst: Ty<'tcx>,\n        src: Ty<'tcx>,\n        assume: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n    ) -> Result<Certainty, NoSolution> {\n        // Erase regions because we compute layouts in `rustc_transmute`,\n        // which will ICE for region vars.\n        let (dst, src) = self.tcx.erase_and_anonymize_regions((dst, src));\n\n        let Some(assume) = rustc_transmute::Assume::from_const(self.tcx, assume) else {\n            return Err(NoSolution);\n        };\n\n        // FIXME(transmutability): This really should be returning nested goals for `Answer::If*`\n        match rustc_transmute::TransmuteTypeEnv::new(self.0.tcx)\n            .is_transmutable(rustc_transmute::Types { src, dst }, assume)\n        {\n            rustc_transmute::Answer::Yes => Ok(Certainty::Yes),\n            rustc_transmute::Answer::No(_) | rustc_transmute::Answer::If(_) => Err(NoSolution),\n        }\n    }\n}\n",
