  "originalFile": "use std::ops::Deref;\n\nuse rustc_data_structures::fx::FxHashSet;\nuse rustc_hir::LangItem;\nuse rustc_hir::def_id::{CRATE_DEF_ID, DefId};\nuse rustc_infer::infer::canonical::query_response::make_query_region_constraints;\nuse rustc_infer::infer::canonical::{\n    Canonical, CanonicalExt as _, CanonicalQueryInput, CanonicalVarKind, CanonicalVarValues,\n};\nuse rustc_infer::infer::{InferCtxt, RegionVariableOrigin, SubregionOrigin, TyCtxtInferExt};\nuse rustc_infer::traits::solve::Goal;\nuse rustc_middle::traits::query::NoSolution;\nuse rustc_middle::traits::solve::Certainty;\nuse rustc_middle::ty::{\n    self, Ty, TyCtxt, TypeFlags, TypeFoldable, TypeVisitableExt as _, TypingMode,\n};\nuse rustc_span::{DUMMY_SP, ErrorGuaranteed, Span};\n\nuse crate::traits::{EvaluateConstErr, ObligationCause, sizedness_fast_path, specialization_graph};\n\n#[repr(transparent)]\npub struct SolverDelegate<'tcx>(InferCtxt<'tcx>);\n\nimpl<'a, 'tcx> From<&'a InferCtxt<'tcx>> for &'a SolverDelegate<'tcx> {\n    fn from(infcx: &'a InferCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\n        // SAFETY: `repr(transparent)`\n        unsafe { std::mem::transmute(infcx) }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Deref for SolverDelegate<'tcx> {\n    type Target = InferCtxt<'tcx>;\n\n    fn deref(&self) -> &Self::Target {\n        &self.0\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> rustc_next_trait_solver::delegate::SolverDelegate for SolverDelegate<'tcx> {\n    type Infcx = InferCtxt<'tcx>;\n    type Interner = TyCtxt<'tcx>;\n\n    fn cx(&self) -> TyCtxt<'tcx> {\n        self.0.tcx\n    }\n\n    fn build_with_canonical<V>(\n        interner: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n        canonical: &CanonicalQueryInput<'tcx, V>,\n    ) -> (Self, V, CanonicalVarValues<'tcx>)\n    where\n        V: TypeFoldable<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        let (infcx, value, vars) = interner\n            .infer_ctxt()\n            .with_next_trait_solver(true)\n            .build_with_canonical(DUMMY_SP, canonical);\n        (SolverDelegate(infcx), value, vars)\n    }\n\n    fn compute_goal_fast_path(\n        &self,\n        goal: Goal<'tcx, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>,\n        span: Span,\n    ) -> Option<Certainty> {\n        if let Some(trait_pred) = goal.predicate.as_trait_clause() {\n            if self.shallow_resolve(trait_pred.self_ty().skip_binder()).is_ty_var()\n                // We don't do this fast path when opaques are defined since we may\n                // eventually use opaques to incompletely guide inference via ty var\n                // self types.\n                // FIXME: Properly consider opaques here.\n                && self.inner.borrow_mut().opaque_types().is_empty()\n            {\n                return Some(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS);\n            }\n\n            if trait_pred.polarity() == ty::PredicatePolarity::Positive {\n                match self.0.tcx.as_lang_item(trait_pred.def_id()) {\n                    Some(LangItem::Sized) | Some(LangItem::MetaSized) => {\n                        let predicate = self.resolve_vars_if_possible(goal.predicate);\n                        if sizedness_fast_path(self.tcx, predicate, goal.param_env) {\n                            return Some(Certainty::Yes);\n                        }\n                    }\n                    Some(LangItem::Copy | LangItem::Clone) => {\n                        let self_ty =\n                            self.resolve_vars_if_possible(trait_pred.self_ty().skip_binder());\n                        // Unlike `Sized` traits, which always prefer the built-in impl,\n                        // `Copy`/`Clone` may be shadowed by a param-env candidate which\n                        // could force a lifetime error or guide inference. While that's\n                        // not generally desirable, it is observable, so for now let's\n                        // ignore this fast path for types that have regions or infer.\n                        if !self_ty\n                            .has_type_flags(TypeFlags::HAS_FREE_REGIONS | TypeFlags::HAS_INFER)\n                            && self_ty.is_trivially_pure_clone_copy()\n                        {\n                            return Some(Certainty::Yes);\n                        }\n                    }\n                    _ => {}\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        let pred = goal.predicate.kind();\n        match pred.no_bound_vars()? {\n            ty::PredicateKind::DynCompatible(def_id) if self.0.tcx.is_dyn_compatible(def_id) => {\n                Some(Certainty::Yes)\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::RegionOutlives(outlives)) => {\n                self.0.sub_regions(\n                    SubregionOrigin::RelateRegionParamBound(span, None),\n                    outlives.1,\n                    outlives.0,\n                );\n                Some(Certainty::Yes)\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::TypeOutlives(outlives)) => {\n                self.0.register_type_outlives_constraint(\n                    outlives.0,\n                    outlives.1,\n                    &ObligationCause::dummy_with_span(span),\n                );\n\n                Some(Certainty::Yes)\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation { sub_ty, super_ty, .. })\n            | ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(ty::CoercePredicate { a, b }) => {\n                let (a, b) = match &pred {\n                    ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(_) => (sub_ty, super_ty),\n                    ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(ty::CoercePredicate { a, b }) => (a, b),\n                    _ => unreachable!(),\n                };\n                match (self.shallow_resolve(a).kind(), self.shallow_resolve(b).kind()) {\n                    (&ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_vid)), &ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                        self.sub_unify_ty_vids_raw(a_vid, b_vid);\n                        Some(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)\n                    }\n                    _ => None,\n                }\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::ConstArgHasType(ct, _)) => {\n                if self.shallow_resolve_const(ct).is_ct_infer() {\n                    Some(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)\n                } else {\n                    None\n                }\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::WellFormed(arg)) => {\n                let arg = self.shallow_resolve_term(arg);\n                if arg.is_trivially_wf(self.tcx) {\n                    Some(Certainty::Yes)\n                } else if arg.is_infer() {\n                    Some(Certainty::AMBIGUOUS)\n                } else {\n                    None\n                }\n            }\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn fresh_var_for_kind_with_span(\n        &self,\n        arg: ty::GenericArg<'tcx>,\n        span: Span,\n    ) -> ty::GenericArg<'tcx> {\n        match arg.kind() {\n            ty::GenericArgKind::Lifetime(_) => {\n                self.next_region_var(RegionVariableOrigin::Misc(span)).into()\n            }\n            ty::GenericArgKind::Type(_) => self.next_ty_var(span).into(),\n            ty::GenericArgKind::Const(_) => self.next_const_var(span).into(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn leak_check(&self, max_input_universe: ty::UniverseIndex) -> Result<(), NoSolution> {\n        self.0.leak_check(max_input_universe, None).map_err(|_| NoSolution)\n    }\n\n    fn evaluate_const(\n        &self,\n        param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n        uv: ty::UnevaluatedConst<'tcx>,\n    ) -> Option<ty::Const<'tcx>> {\n        let ct = ty::Const::new_unevaluated(self.tcx, uv);\n\n        match crate::traits::try_evaluate_const(&self.0, ct, param_env) {\n            Ok(ct) => Some(ct),\n            Err(EvaluateConstErr::EvaluationFailure(e)) => Some(ty::Const::new_error(self.tcx, e)),\n            Err(\n                EvaluateConstErr::InvalidConstParamTy(_) | EvaluateConstErr::HasGenericsOrInfers,\n            ) => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn well_formed_goals(\n        &self,\n        param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n        term: ty::Term<'tcx>,\n    ) -> Option<Vec<Goal<'tcx, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>> {\n        crate::traits::wf::unnormalized_obligations(\n            &self.0,\n            param_env,\n            term,\n            DUMMY_SP,\n            CRATE_DEF_ID,\n        )\n        .map(|obligations| obligations.into_iter().map(|obligation| obligation.as_goal()).collect())\n    }\n\n    fn make_deduplicated_outlives_constraints(&self) -> Vec<ty::ArgOutlivesPredicate<'tcx>> {\n        // Cannot use `take_registered_region_obligations` as we may compute the response\n        // inside of a `probe` whenever we have multiple choices inside of the solver.\n        let region_obligations = self.0.inner.borrow().region_obligations().to_owned();\n        let region_assumptions = self.0.inner.borrow().region_assumptions().to_owned();\n        let region_constraints = self.0.with_region_constraints(|region_constraints| {\n            make_query_region_constraints(\n                region_obligations,\n                region_constraints,\n                region_assumptions,\n            )\n        });\n\n        let mut seen = FxHashSet::default();\n        region_constraints\n            .outlives\n            .into_iter()\n            .filter(|&(outlives, _)| seen.insert(outlives))\n            .map(|(outlives, _)| outlives)\n            .collect()\n    }\n\n    fn instantiate_canonical<V>(\n        &self,\n        canonical: Canonical<'tcx, V>,\n        values: CanonicalVarValues<'tcx>,\n    ) -> V\n    where\n        V: TypeFoldable<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        canonical.instantiate(self.tcx, &values)\n    }\n\n    fn instantiate_canonical_var(\n        &self,\n        kind: CanonicalVarKind<'tcx>,\n        span: Span,\n        var_values: &[ty::GenericArg<'tcx>],\n        universe_map: impl Fn(ty::UniverseIndex) -> ty::UniverseIndex,\n    ) -> ty::GenericArg<'tcx> {\n        self.0.instantiate_canonical_var(span, kind, var_values, universe_map)\n    }\n\n    fn add_item_bounds_for_hidden_type(\n        &self,\n        def_id: DefId,\n        args: ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>,\n        param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n        hidden_ty: Ty<'tcx>,\n        goals: &mut Vec<Goal<'tcx, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>,\n    ) {\n        self.0.add_item_bounds_for_hidden_type(def_id, args, param_env, hidden_ty, goals);\n    }\n\n    fn fetch_eligible_assoc_item(\n        &self,\n        goal_trait_ref: ty::TraitRef<'tcx>,\n        trait_assoc_def_id: DefId,\n        impl_def_id: DefId,\n    ) -> Result<Option<DefId>, ErrorGuaranteed> {\n        let node_item = specialization_graph::assoc_def(self.tcx, impl_def_id, trait_assoc_def_id)?;\n\n        let eligible = if node_item.is_final() {\n            // Non-specializable items are always projectable.\n            true\n        } else {\n            // Only reveal a specializable default if we're past type-checking\n            // and the obligation is monomorphic, otherwise passes such as\n            // transmute checking and polymorphic MIR optimizations could\n            // get a result which isn't correct for all monomorphizations.\n            match self.typing_mode() {\n                TypingMode::Coherence\n                | TypingMode::Analysis { .. }\n                | TypingMode::Borrowck { .. }\n                | TypingMode::PostBorrowckAnalysis { .. } => false,\n                TypingMode::PostAnalysis => {\n                    let poly_trait_ref = self.resolve_vars_if_possible(goal_trait_ref);\n                    !poly_trait_ref.still_further_specializable()\n                }\n            }\n        };\n\n        // FIXME: Check for defaultness here may cause diagnostics problems.\n        if eligible { Ok(Some(node_item.item.def_id)) } else { Ok(None) }\n    }\n\n    // FIXME: This actually should destructure the `Result` we get from transmutability and\n    // register candidates. We probably need to register >1 since we may have an OR of ANDs.\n    fn is_transmutable(\n        &self,\n        dst: Ty<'tcx>,\n        src: Ty<'tcx>,\n        assume: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n    ) -> Result<Certainty, NoSolution> {\n        // Erase regions because we compute layouts in `rustc_transmute`,\n        // which will ICE for region vars.\n        let (dst, src) = self.tcx.erase_and_anonymize_regions((dst, src));\n\n        let Some(assume) = rustc_transmute::Assume::from_const(self.tcx, assume) else {\n            return Err(NoSolution);\n        };\n\n        // FIXME(transmutability): This really should be returning nested goals for `Answer::If*`\n        match rustc_transmute::TransmuteTypeEnv::new(self.0.tcx)\n            .is_transmutable(rustc_transmute::Types { src, dst }, assume)\n        {\n            rustc_transmute::Answer::Yes => Ok(Certainty::Yes),\n            rustc_transmute::Answer::No(_) | rustc_transmute::Answer::If(_) => Err(NoSolution),\n        }\n    }\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {

        "             }",

{
  "filePath": "/workspace/code/compiler/rustc_trait_selection/src/error_reporting/traits/overflow.rs",
  "newString": "                    ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation { sub_ty, super_ty, a_is_expected: _ }) => {\n                        struct_span_code_err!(\n                            self.dcx(),\n                            span,\n                            E0275,\n                            \"overflow assigning `{sub_ty}` to `{super_ty}`\",\n                        )\n                    }\n                    ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(ty::CoercePredicate { a, b }) => {\n                        struct_span_code_err!(\n                            self.dcx(),\n                            span,\n                            E0275,\n                            \"overflow assigning `{a}` to `{b}`\",\n                        )\n                    }",
  "oldString": "                    ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation { a, b, a_is_expected: _ })\n                    | ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(ty::CoercePredicate { a, b }) => {\n                        struct_span_code_err!(\n                            self.dcx(),\n                            span,\n                            E0275,\n                            \"overflow assigning `{a}` to `{b}`\",\n                        )\n                    }",
  "originalFile": "use std::fmt;\n\nuse rustc_errors::{Diag, E0275, EmissionGuarantee, ErrorGuaranteed, struct_span_code_err};\nuse rustc_hir::def::Namespace;\nuse rustc_hir::def_id::LOCAL_CRATE;\nuse rustc_hir::limit::Limit;\nuse rustc_infer::traits::{Obligation, PredicateObligation};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::print::{FmtPrinter, Print};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::{self, TyCtxt, Upcast};\nuse rustc_span::Span;\nuse tracing::debug;\n\nuse crate::error_reporting::TypeErrCtxt;\n\npub enum OverflowCause<'tcx> {\n    DeeplyNormalize(ty::AliasTerm<'tcx>),\n    TraitSolver(ty::Predicate<'tcx>),\n}\n\npub fn suggest_new_overflow_limit<'tcx, G: EmissionGuarantee>(\n    tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n    err: &mut Diag<'_, G>,\n) {\n    let suggested_limit = match tcx.recursion_limit() {\n        Limit(0) => Limit(2),\n        limit => limit * 2,\n    };\n    err.help(format!(\n        \"consider increasing the recursion limit by adding a \\\n         `#![recursion_limit = \\\"{}\\\"]` attribute to your crate (`{}`)\",\n        suggested_limit,\n        tcx.crate_name(LOCAL_CRATE),\n    ));\n}\n\nimpl<'a, 'tcx> TypeErrCtxt<'a, 'tcx> {\n    /// Reports that an overflow has occurred and halts compilation. We\n    /// halt compilation unconditionally because it is important that\n    /// overflows never be masked -- they basically represent computations\n    /// whose result could not be truly determined and thus we can't say\n    /// if the program type checks or not -- and they are unusual\n    /// occurrences in any case.\n    pub fn report_overflow_error(\n        &self,\n        cause: OverflowCause<'tcx>,\n        span: Span,\n        suggest_increasing_limit: bool,\n        mutate: impl FnOnce(&mut Diag<'_>),\n    ) -> ! {\n        let mut err = self.build_overflow_error(cause, span, suggest_increasing_limit);\n        mutate(&mut err);\n        err.emit().raise_fatal();\n    }\n\n    pub fn build_overflow_error(\n        &self,\n        cause: OverflowCause<'tcx>,\n        span: Span,\n        suggest_increasing_limit: bool,\n    ) -> Diag<'a> {\n        fn with_short_path<'tcx, T>(tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>, value: T) -> String\n        where\n            T: fmt::Display + Print<'tcx, FmtPrinter<'tcx, 'tcx>>,\n        {\n            let s = value.to_string();\n            if s.len() > 50 {\n                // We don't need to save the type to a file, we will be talking about this type already\n                // in a separate note when we explain the obligation, so it will be available that way.\n                let mut p: FmtPrinter<'_, '_> =\n                    FmtPrinter::new_with_limit(tcx, Namespace::TypeNS, Limit(6));\n                value.print(&mut p).unwrap();\n                p.into_buffer()\n            } else {\n                s\n            }\n        }\n\n        let mut err = match cause {\n            OverflowCause::DeeplyNormalize(alias_term) => {\n                let alias_term = self.resolve_vars_if_possible(alias_term);\n                let kind = alias_term.kind(self.tcx).descr();\n                let alias_str = with_short_path(self.tcx, alias_term);\n                struct_span_code_err!(\n                    self.dcx(),\n                    span,\n                    E0275,\n                    \"overflow normalizing the {kind} `{alias_str}`\",\n                )\n            }\n            OverflowCause::TraitSolver(predicate) => {\n                let predicate = self.resolve_vars_if_possible(predicate);\n                match predicate.kind().skip_binder() {\n                    ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation { a, b, a_is_expected: _ })\n                    | ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(ty::CoercePredicate { a, b }) => {\n                        struct_span_code_err!(\n                            self.dcx(),\n                            span,\n                            E0275,\n                            \"overflow assigning `{a}` to `{b}`\",\n                        )\n                    }\n                    _ => {\n                        let pred_str = with_short_path(self.tcx, predicate);\n                        struct_span_code_err!(\n                            self.dcx(),\n                            span,\n                            E0275,\n                            \"overflow evaluating the requirement `{pred_str}`\",\n                        )\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        };\n\n        if suggest_increasing_limit {\n            suggest_new_overflow_limit(self.tcx, &mut err);\n        }\n\n        err\n    }\n\n    /// Reports that an overflow has occurred and halts compilation. We\n    /// halt compilation unconditionally because it is important that\n    /// overflows never be masked -- they basically represent computations\n    /// whose result could not be truly determined and thus we can't say\n    /// if the program type checks or not -- and they are unusual\n    /// occurrences in any case.\n    pub fn report_overflow_obligation<T>(\n        &self,\n        obligation: &Obligation<'tcx, T>,\n        suggest_increasing_limit: bool,\n    ) -> !\n    where\n        T: Upcast<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Predicate<'tcx>> + Clone,\n    {\n        let predicate = obligation.predicate.clone().upcast(self.tcx);\n        let predicate = self.resolve_vars_if_possible(predicate);\n        self.report_overflow_error(\n            OverflowCause::TraitSolver(predicate),\n            obligation.cause.span,\n            suggest_increasing_limit,\n            |err| {\n                self.note_obligation_cause_code(\n                    obligation.cause.body_id,\n                    err,\n                    predicate,\n                    obligation.param_env,\n                    obligation.cause.code(),\n                    &mut vec![],\n                    &mut Default::default(),\n                );\n            },\n        );\n    }\n\n    /// Reports that a cycle was detected which led to overflow and halts\n    /// compilation. This is equivalent to `report_overflow_obligation` except\n    /// that we can give a more helpful error message (and, in particular,\n    /// we do not suggest increasing the overflow limit, which is not\n    /// going to help).\n    pub fn report_overflow_obligation_cycle(&self, cycle: &[PredicateObligation<'tcx>]) -> ! {\n        let cycle = self.resolve_vars_if_possible(cycle.to_owned());\n        assert!(!cycle.is_empty());\n\n        debug!(?cycle, \"report_overflow_error_cycle\");\n\n        // The 'deepest' obligation is most likely to have a useful\n        // cause 'backtrace'\n        self.report_overflow_obligation(\n            cycle.iter().max_by_key(|p| p.recursion_depth).unwrap(),\n            false,\n        );\n    }\n\n    pub fn report_overflow_no_abort(\n        &self,\n        obligation: PredicateObligation<'tcx>,\n        suggest_increasing_limit: bool,\n    ) -> ErrorGuaranteed {\n        let obligation = self.resolve_vars_if_possible(obligation);\n        let mut err = self.build_overflow_error(\n            OverflowCause::TraitSolver(obligation.predicate),\n            obligation.cause.span,\n            suggest_increasing_limit,\n        );\n        self.note_obligation_cause(&mut err, &obligation);\n        err.emit()\n    }\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "             OverflowCause::TraitSolver(predicate) => {",
        "                 let predicate = self.resolve_vars_if_possible(predicate);",
        "                 match predicate.kind().skip_binder() {",
        "-                    ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation { a, b, a_is_expected: _ })",
        "-                    | ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(ty::CoercePredicate { a, b }) => {",
        "+                    ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation { sub_ty, super_ty, a_is_expected: _ }) => {",

        "                             self.dcx(),",
        "                             span,",
        "                             E0275,",
        "+                            \"overflow assigning `{sub_ty}` to `{super_ty}`\",",
        "+                        )",
        "+                    }",
        "+                    ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(ty::CoercePredicate { a, b }) => {",
        "+                        struct_span_code_err!(",
        "+                            self.dcx(),",
        "+                            span,",
        "+                            E0275,",
        "                             \"overflow assigning `{a}` to `{b}`\",",
        "                         )",

Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/code/compiler/rustc_infer/src/infer/relate/type_relating.rs",
  "newString": "                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation {\n                                a_is_expected: true,\n                                sub_ty: a,\n                                super_ty: b,\n                            })),",
  "oldString": "                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation {\n                                a_is_expected: true,\n                                a,\n                                b,\n                            })),",
  "originalFile": "use rustc_middle::traits::solve::Goal;\nuse rustc_middle::ty::relate::combine::{super_combine_consts, super_combine_tys};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::relate::{\n    Relate, RelateResult, TypeRelation, relate_args_invariantly, relate_args_with_variances,\n};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::{self, DelayedSet, Ty, TyCtxt, TyVar};\nuse rustc_span::Span;\nuse tracing::{debug, instrument};\n\nuse crate::infer::BoundRegionConversionTime::HigherRankedType;\nuse crate::infer::relate::{PredicateEmittingRelation, StructurallyRelateAliases};\nuse crate::infer::{DefineOpaqueTypes, InferCtxt, SubregionOrigin, TypeTrace};\nuse crate::traits::{Obligation, PredicateObligations};\n\n/// Enforce that `a` is equal to or a subtype of `b`.\npub(crate) struct TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n    infcx: &'infcx InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n\n    // Immutable fields\n    trace: TypeTrace<'tcx>,\n    param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n    define_opaque_types: DefineOpaqueTypes,\n\n    // Mutable fields.\n    ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    obligations: PredicateObligations<'tcx>,\n    /// The cache only tracks the `ambient_variance` as it's the\n    /// only field which is mutable and which meaningfully changes\n    /// the result when relating types.\n    ///\n    /// The cache does not track whether the state of the\n    /// `InferCtxt` has been changed or whether we've added any\n    /// obligations to `self.goals`. Whether a goal is added\n    /// once or multiple times is not really meaningful.\n    ///\n    /// Changes in the inference state may delay some type inference to\n    /// the next fulfillment loop. Given that this loop is already\n    /// necessary, this is also not a meaningful change. Consider\n    /// the following three relations:\n    /// ```text\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ?0 eq u32\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ```\n    /// Without a cache, the second `Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>` would eagerly\n    /// constrain `?1` to `u32`. When using the cache entry from the\n    /// first time we've related these types, this only happens when\n    /// later proving the `Subtype(?0, ?1)` goal from the first relation.\n    cache: DelayedSet<(ty::Variance, Ty<'tcx>, Ty<'tcx>)>,\n}\n\nimpl<'infcx, 'tcx> TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n    pub(crate) fn new(\n        infcx: &'infcx InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n        trace: TypeTrace<'tcx>,\n        param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n        define_opaque_types: DefineOpaqueTypes,\n        ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    ) -> TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n        assert!(!infcx.next_trait_solver);\n        TypeRelating {\n            infcx,\n            trace,\n            param_env,\n            define_opaque_types,\n            ambient_variance,\n            obligations: PredicateObligations::new(),\n            cache: Default::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn into_obligations(self) -> PredicateObligations<'tcx> {\n        self.obligations\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> TypeRelation<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for TypeRelating<'_, 'tcx> {\n    fn cx(&self) -> TyCtxt<'tcx> {\n        self.infcx.tcx\n    }\n\n    fn relate_item_args(\n        &mut self,\n        item_def_id: rustc_hir::def_id::DefId,\n        a_arg: ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>,\n        b_arg: ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>> {\n        if self.ambient_variance == ty::Invariant {\n            // Avoid fetching the variance if we are in an invariant\n            // context; no need, and it can induce dependency cycles\n            // (e.g., #41849).\n            relate_args_invariantly(self, a_arg, b_arg)\n        } else {\n            let tcx = self.cx();\n            let opt_variances = tcx.variances_of(item_def_id);\n            relate_args_with_variances(self, item_def_id, opt_variances, a_arg, b_arg, false)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn relate_with_variance<T: Relate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>>(\n        &mut self,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        _info: ty::VarianceDiagInfo<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n        a: T,\n        b: T,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, T> {\n        let old_ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance;\n        self.ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance.xform(variance);\n        debug!(?self.ambient_variance, \"new ambient variance\");\n\n        let r = if self.ambient_variance == ty::Bivariant { Ok(a) } else { self.relate(a, b) };\n\n        self.ambient_variance = old_ambient_variance;\n        r\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn tys(&mut self, a: Ty<'tcx>, b: Ty<'tcx>) -> RelateResult<'tcx, Ty<'tcx>> {\n        if a == b {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        let infcx = self.infcx;\n        let a = infcx.shallow_resolve(a);\n        let b = infcx.shallow_resolve(b);\n\n        if self.cache.contains(&(self.ambient_variance, a, b)) {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        match (a.kind(), b.kind()) {\n            (&ty::Infer(TyVar(a_id)), &ty::Infer(TyVar(b_id))) => {\n                match self.ambient_variance {\n                    ty::Covariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `A <: B` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.obligations.push(Obligation::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation {\n                                a_is_expected: true,\n                                a,\n                                b,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Contravariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `B <: A` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.obligations.push(Obligation::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation {\n                                a_is_expected: false,\n                                a: b,\n                                b: a,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Invariant => {\n                        infcx.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().equate(a_id, b_id);\n                    }\n                    ty::Bivariant => {\n                        unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            (&ty::Infer(TyVar(a_vid)), _) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var(self, true, a_vid, self.ambient_variance, b)?;\n            }\n            (_, &ty::Infer(TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var(\n                    self,\n                    false,\n                    b_vid,\n                    self.ambient_variance.xform(ty::Contravariant),\n                    a,\n                )?;\n            }\n\n            (\n                &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id: a_def_id, .. }),\n                &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id: b_def_id, .. }),\n            ) if a_def_id == b_def_id => {\n                super_combine_tys(infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n\n            (&ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id, .. }), _)\n            | (_, &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id, .. }))\n                if self.define_opaque_types == DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes && def_id.is_local() =>\n            {\n                self.register_goals(infcx.handle_opaque_type(\n                    a,\n                    b,\n                    self.trace.cause.span,\n                    self.param_env(),\n                )?);\n            }\n\n            _ => {\n                super_combine_tys(infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        assert!(self.cache.insert((self.ambient_variance, a, b)));\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn regions(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n        b: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Region<'tcx>> {\n        let origin = SubregionOrigin::Subtype(Box::new(self.trace.clone()));\n\n        match self.ambient_variance {\n            // Subtype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('a: 'b) => SubRegion('b, 'a)\n            ty::Covariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_subregion(origin, b, a);\n            }\n            // Suptype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('b: 'a) => SubRegion('a, 'b)\n            ty::Contravariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_subregion(origin, a, b);\n            }\n            ty::Invariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_eqregion(origin, a, b);\n            }\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn consts(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n        b: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Const<'tcx>> {\n        super_combine_consts(self.infcx, self, a, b)\n    }\n\n    fn binders<T>(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Binder<'tcx, T>,\n        b: ty::Binder<'tcx, T>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Binder<'tcx, T>>\n    where\n        T: Relate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        if a == b {\n            // Do nothing\n        } else if let Some(a) = a.no_bound_vars()\n            && let Some(b) = b.no_bound_vars()\n        {\n            self.relate(a, b)?;\n        } else {\n            let span = self.trace.cause.span;\n            let infcx = self.infcx;\n\n            match self.ambient_variance {\n                // Checks whether `for<..> sub <: for<..> sup` holds.\n                //\n                // For this to hold, **all** instantiations of the super type\n                // have to be a super type of **at least one** instantiation of\n                // the subtype.\n                //\n                // This is implemented by first entering a new universe.\n                // We then replace all bound variables in `sup` with placeholders,\n                // and all bound variables in `sub` with inference vars.\n                // We can then just relate the two resulting types as normal.\n                //\n                // Note: this is a subtle algorithm. For a full explanation, please see\n                // the [rustc dev guide][rd]\n                //\n                // [rd]: https://rustc-dev-guide.rust-lang.org/borrow_check/region_inference/placeholders_and_universes.html\n                ty::Covariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                        let a = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, a);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n                ty::Contravariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                        let b = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, b);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n\n                // When **equating** binders, we check that there is a 1-to-1\n                // correspondence between the bound vars in both types.\n                //\n                // We do so by separately instantiating one of the binders with\n                // placeholders and the other with inference variables and then\n                // equating the instantiated types.\n                //\n                // We want `for<..> A == for<..> B` -- therefore we want\n                // `exists<..> A == for<..> B` and `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                // Check if `exists<..> A == for<..> B`\n                ty::Invariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                        let a = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, a);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n\n                    // Check if `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                    infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                        let b = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, b);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n                ty::Bivariant => {\n                    unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> PredicateEmittingRelation<InferCtxt<'tcx>> for TypeRelating<'_, 'tcx> {\n    fn span(&self) -> Span {\n        self.trace.span()\n    }\n\n    fn param_env(&self) -> ty::ParamEnv<'tcx> {\n        self.param_env\n    }\n\n    fn structurally_relate_aliases(&self) -> StructurallyRelateAliases {\n        StructurallyRelateAliases::No\n    }\n\n    fn register_predicates(\n        &mut self,\n        preds: impl IntoIterator<Item: ty::Upcast<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>,\n    ) {\n        self.obligations.extend(preds.into_iter().map(|pred| {\n            Obligation::new(self.infcx.tcx, self.trace.cause.clone(), self.param_env, pred)\n        }))\n    }\n\n    fn register_goals(&mut self, goals: impl IntoIterator<Item = Goal<'tcx, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>) {\n        self.obligations.extend(goals.into_iter().map(|goal| {\n            Obligation::new(\n                self.infcx.tcx,\n                self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                goal.param_env,\n                goal.predicate,\n            )\n        }))\n    }\n\n    fn register_alias_relate_predicate(&mut self, a: Ty<'tcx>, b: Ty<'tcx>) {\n        self.register_predicates([ty::Binder::dummy(match self.ambient_variance {\n            ty::Covariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            // a :> b is b <: a\n            ty::Contravariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                b.into(),\n                a.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            ty::Invariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Equate,\n            ),\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        })]);\n    }\n}\n",

Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/code/compiler/rustc_infer/src/infer/relate/type_relating.rs",
  "newString": "                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation {\n                                a_is_expected: false,\n                                sub_ty: b,\n                                super_ty: a,\n                            })),",
  "oldString": "                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation {\n                                a_is_expected: false,\n                                a: b,\n                                b: a,\n                            })),",
  "originalFile": "use rustc_middle::traits::solve::Goal;\nuse rustc_middle::ty::relate::combine::{super_combine_consts, super_combine_tys};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::relate::{\n    Relate, RelateResult, TypeRelation, relate_args_invariantly, relate_args_with_variances,\n};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::{self, DelayedSet, Ty, TyCtxt, TyVar};\nuse rustc_span::Span;\nuse tracing::{debug, instrument};\n\nuse crate::infer::BoundRegionConversionTime::HigherRankedType;\nuse crate::infer::relate::{PredicateEmittingRelation, StructurallyRelateAliases};\nuse crate::infer::{DefineOpaqueTypes, InferCtxt, SubregionOrigin, TypeTrace};\nuse crate::traits::{Obligation, PredicateObligations};\n\n/// Enforce that `a` is equal to or a subtype of `b`.\npub(crate) struct TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n    infcx: &'infcx InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n\n    // Immutable fields\n    trace: TypeTrace<'tcx>,\n    param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n    define_opaque_types: DefineOpaqueTypes,\n\n    // Mutable fields.\n    ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    obligations: PredicateObligations<'tcx>,\n    /// The cache only tracks the `ambient_variance` as it's the\n    /// only field which is mutable and which meaningfully changes\n    /// the result when relating types.\n    ///\n    /// The cache does not track whether the state of the\n    /// `InferCtxt` has been changed or whether we've added any\n    /// obligations to `self.goals`. Whether a goal is added\n    /// once or multiple times is not really meaningful.\n    ///\n    /// Changes in the inference state may delay some type inference to\n    /// the next fulfillment loop. Given that this loop is already\n    /// necessary, this is also not a meaningful change. Consider\n    /// the following three relations:\n    /// ```text\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ?0 eq u32\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ```\n    /// Without a cache, the second `Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>` would eagerly\n    /// constrain `?1` to `u32`. When using the cache entry from the\n    /// first time we've related these types, this only happens when\n    /// later proving the `Subtype(?0, ?1)` goal from the first relation.\n    cache: DelayedSet<(ty::Variance, Ty<'tcx>, Ty<'tcx>)>,\n}\n\nimpl<'infcx, 'tcx> TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n    pub(crate) fn new(\n        infcx: &'infcx InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n        trace: TypeTrace<'tcx>,\n        param_env: ty::ParamEnv<'tcx>,\n        define_opaque_types: DefineOpaqueTypes,\n        ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    ) -> TypeRelating<'infcx, 'tcx> {\n        assert!(!infcx.next_trait_solver);\n        TypeRelating {\n            infcx,\n            trace,\n            param_env,\n            define_opaque_types,\n            ambient_variance,\n            obligations: PredicateObligations::new(),\n            cache: Default::default(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub(crate) fn into_obligations(self) -> PredicateObligations<'tcx> {\n        self.obligations\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> TypeRelation<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for TypeRelating<'_, 'tcx> {\n    fn cx(&self) -> TyCtxt<'tcx> {\n        self.infcx.tcx\n    }\n\n    fn relate_item_args(\n        &mut self,\n        item_def_id: rustc_hir::def_id::DefId,\n        a_arg: ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>,\n        b_arg: ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::GenericArgsRef<'tcx>> {\n        if self.ambient_variance == ty::Invariant {\n            // Avoid fetching the variance if we are in an invariant\n            // context; no need, and it can induce dependency cycles\n            // (e.g., #41849).\n            relate_args_invariantly(self, a_arg, b_arg)\n        } else {\n            let tcx = self.cx();\n            let opt_variances = tcx.variances_of(item_def_id);\n            relate_args_with_variances(self, item_def_id, opt_variances, a_arg, b_arg, false)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn relate_with_variance<T: Relate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>>(\n        &mut self,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        _info: ty::VarianceDiagInfo<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n        a: T,\n        b: T,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, T> {\n        let old_ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance;\n        self.ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance.xform(variance);\n        debug!(?self.ambient_variance, \"new ambient variance\");\n\n        let r = if self.ambient_variance == ty::Bivariant { Ok(a) } else { self.relate(a, b) };\n\n        self.ambient_variance = old_ambient_variance;\n        r\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn tys(&mut self, a: Ty<'tcx>, b: Ty<'tcx>) -> RelateResult<'tcx, Ty<'tcx>> {\n        if a == b {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        let infcx = self.infcx;\n        let a = infcx.shallow_resolve(a);\n        let b = infcx.shallow_resolve(b);\n\n        if self.cache.contains(&(self.ambient_variance, a, b)) {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        match (a.kind(), b.kind()) {\n            (&ty::Infer(TyVar(a_id)), &ty::Infer(TyVar(b_id))) => {\n                match self.ambient_variance {\n                    ty::Covariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `A <: B` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.obligations.push(Obligation::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation {\n                                a_is_expected: true,\n                                sub_ty: a,\n                                super_ty: b,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Contravariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `B <: A` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.obligations.push(Obligation::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation {\n                                a_is_expected: false,\n                                a: b,\n                                b: a,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Invariant => {\n                        infcx.inner.borrow_mut().type_variables().equate(a_id, b_id);\n                    }\n                    ty::Bivariant => {\n                        unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            (&ty::Infer(TyVar(a_vid)), _) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var(self, true, a_vid, self.ambient_variance, b)?;\n            }\n            (_, &ty::Infer(TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var(\n                    self,\n                    false,\n                    b_vid,\n                    self.ambient_variance.xform(ty::Contravariant),\n                    a,\n                )?;\n            }\n\n            (\n                &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id: a_def_id, .. }),\n                &ty::Alias(ty::Opaque, ty::AliasTy { def_id: b_def_id, .. }),\n            ) if a_def_id == b_def_id => {\n              b,\n                    self.trace.cause.span,\n                    self.param_env(),\n                )?);\n            }\n\n            _ => {\n                super_combine_tys(infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        assert!(self.cache.insert((self.ambient_variance, a, b)));\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn regions(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n        b: ty::Region<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Region<'tcx>> {\n        let origin = SubregionOrigin::Subtype(Box::new(self.trace.clone()));\n\n        match self.ambient_variance {\n            // Subtype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('a: 'b) => SubRegion('b, 'a)\n            ty::Covariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_subregion(origin, b, a);\n            }\n            // Suptype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('b: 'a) => SubRegion('a, 'b)\n            ty::Contravariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_subregion(origin, a, b);\n            }\n            ty::Invariant => {\n                self.infcx\n                    .inner\n                    .borrow_mut()\n                    .unwrap_region_constraints()\n                    .make_eqregion(origin, a, b);\n            }\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn consts(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n        b: ty::Const<'tcx>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Const<'tcx>> {\n        super_combine_consts(self.infcx, self, a, b)\n    }\n\n    fn binders<T>(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Binder<'tcx, T>,\n        b: ty::Binder<'tcx, T>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<'tcx, ty::Binder<'tcx, T>>\n    where\n        T: Relate<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        if a == b {\n            // Do nothing\n        } else if let Some(a) = a.no_bound_vars()\n            && let Some(b) = b.no_bound_vars()\n        {\n            self.relate(a, b)?;\n        } else {\n            let span = self.trace.cause.span;\n            let infcx = self.infcx;\n\n            match self.ambient_variance {\n                // Checks whether `for<..> sub <: for<..> sup` holds.\n                //\n                // For this to hold, **all** instantiations of the super type\n                // have to be a super type of **at least one** instantiation of\n                // the subtype.\n                //\n                // This is implemented by first entering a new universe.\n                // We then replace all bound variables in `sup` with placeholders,\n                // and all bound variables in `sub` with inference vars.\n                // We can then just relate the two resulting types as normal.\n                //\n                // Note: this is a subtle algorithm. For a full explanation, please see\n                // the [rustc dev guide][rd]\n                //\n                // [rd]: https://rustc-dev-guide.rust-lang.org/borrow_check/region_inference/placeholders_and_universes.html\n                ty::Covariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                        let a = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, a);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n                ty::Contravariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                        let b = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, b);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n\n                // When **equating** binders, we check that there is a 1-to-1\n                // correspondence between the bound vars in both types.\n                //\n                // We do so by separately instantiating one of the binders with\n                // placeholders and the other with inference variables and then\n                // equating the instantiated types.\n                //\n                // We want `for<..> A == for<..> B` -- therefore we want\n                // `exists<..> A == for<..> B` and `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                // Check if `exists<..> A == for<..> B`\n                ty::Invariant => {\n                    infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                        let a = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, a);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n\n                    // Check if `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                    infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                        let b = infcx.instantiate_binder_with_fresh_vars(span, HigherRankedType, b);\n                        self.relate(a, b)\n                    })?;\n                }\n                ty::Bivariant => {\n                    unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> PredicateEmittingRelation<InferCtxt<'tcx>> for TypeRelating<'_, 'tcx> {\n    fn span(&self) -> Span {\n        self.trace.span()\n    }\n\n    fn param_env(&self) -> ty::ParamEnv<'tcx> {\n        self.param_env\n    }\n\n    fn structurally_relate_aliases(&self) -> StructurallyRelateAliases {\n        StructurallyRelateAliases::No\n    }\n\n    fn register_predicates(\n        &mut self,\n        preds: impl IntoIterator<Item: ty::Upcast<TyCtxt<'tcx>, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>,\n    ) {\n        self.obligations.extend(preds.into_iter().map(|pred| {\n            Obligation::new(self.infcx.tcx, self.trace.cause.clone(), self.param_env, pred)\n        }))\n    }\n\n    fn register_goals(&mut self, goals: impl IntoIterator<Item = Goal<'tcx, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>>) {\n        self.obligations.extend(goals.into_iter().map(|goal| {\n            Obligation::new(\n                self.infcx.tcx,\n                self.trace.cause.clone(),\n                goal.param_env,\n                goal.predicate,\n            )\n        }))\n    }\n\n    fn register_alias_relate_predicate(&mut self, a: Ty<'tcx>, b: Ty<'tcx>) {\n        self.register_predicates([ty::Binder::dummy(match self.ambient_variance {\n            ty::Covariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            // a :> b is b <: a\n            ty::Contravariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                b.into(),\n                a.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            ty::Invariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Equate,\n            ),\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        })]);\n    }\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "                             self.param_env,",
        "                             ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation {",

        "-                                a: b,",
        "-                                b: a,",
        "+                                sub_ty: b,",
        "+                                super_ty: a,",
        "                             })),",
        "                         ));",
        "                     }"
      ],

}
