  "originalFile": "use std::marker::PhantomData;\n\nuse rustc_data_structures::obligation_forest::{\n    Error, ForestObligation, ObligationForest, ObligationProcessor, Outcome, ProcessResult,\n};\nuse rustc_hir::def_id::LocalDefId;\nuse rustc_infer::infer::DefineOpaqueTypes;\nuse rustc_infer::traits::{\n    FromSolverError, PolyTraitObligation, PredicateObligations, ProjectionCacheKey, SelectionError,\n    TraitEngine,\n};\nuse rustc_middle::bug;\nuse rustc_middle::ty::abstract_const::NotConstEvaluatable;\nuse rustc_middle::ty::error::{ExpectedFound, TypeError};\nuse rustc_middle::ty::{\n    self, Binder, Const, GenericArgsRef, TypeVisitable, TypeVisitableExt, TypingMode,\n    may_use_unstable_feature,\n};\nuse rustc_span::DUMMY_SP;\nuse thin_vec::{ThinVec, thin_vec};\nuse tracing::{debug, debug_span, instrument};\n\nuse super::effects::{self, HostEffectObligation};\nuse super::project::{self, ProjectAndUnifyResult};\nuse super::select::SelectionContext;\nuse super::{\n    EvaluationResult, FulfillmentError, FulfillmentErrorCode, PredicateObligation,\n    ScrubbedTraitError, const_evaluatable, wf,\n};\nuse crate::error_reporting::InferCtxtErrorExt;\nuse crate::infer::{InferCtxt, TyOrConstInferVar};\nuse crate::solve::StalledOnCoroutines;\nuse crate::traits::normalize::normalize_with_depth_to;\nuse crate::traits::project::{PolyProjectionObligation, ProjectionCacheKeyExt as _};\nuse crate::traits::query::evaluate_obligation::InferCtxtExt;\nuse crate::traits::{EvaluateConstErr, sizedness_fast_path};\n\npub(crate) type PendingPredicateObligations<'tcx> = ThinVec<PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>>;\n\nimpl<'tcx> ForestObligation for PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx> {\n    /// Note that we include both the `ParamEnv` and the `Predicate`,\n    /// as the `ParamEnv` can influence whether fulfillment succeeds\n    /// or fails.\n    type CacheKey = ty::ParamEnvAnd<'tcx, ty::Predicate<'tcx>>;\n\n    fn as_cache_key(&self) -> Self::CacheKey {\n        self.obligation.param_env.and(self.obligation.predicate)\n    }\n}\n\n/// The fulfillment context is used to drive trait resolution. It\n/// consists of a list of obligations that must be (eventually)\n/// satisfied. The job is to track which are satisfied, which yielded\n/// errors, and which are still pending. At any point, users can call\n/// `try_evaluate_obligations`, and the fulfillment context will try to do\n/// selection, retaining only those obligations that remain\n/// ambiguous. This may be helpful in pushing type inference\n/// along. Once all type inference constraints have been generated, the\n/// method `evaluate_obligations_error_on_ambiguity` can be used to report any remaining\n/// ambiguous cases as errors.\npub struct FulfillmentContext<'tcx, E: 'tcx> {\n    /// A list of all obligations that have been registered with this\n    /// fulfillment context.\n    predicates: ObligationForest<PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>>,\n\n    /// The snapshot in which this context was created. Using the context\n    /// outside of this snapshot leads to subtle bugs if the snapshot\n    /// gets rolled back. Because of this we explicitly check that we only\n    /// use the context in exactly this snapshot.\n    usable_in_snapshot: usize,\n\n    _errors: PhantomData<E>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\npub struct PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx> {\n    pub obligation: PredicateObligation<'tcx>,\n    // This is far more often read than modified, meaning that we\n    // should mostly optimize for reading speed, while modifying is not as relevant.\n    //\n    // For whatever reason using a boxed slice is slower than using a `Vec` here.\n    pub stalled_on: Vec<TyOrConstInferVar>,\n}\n\n// `PendingPredicateObligation` is used a lot. Make sure it doesn't unintentionally get bigger.\n#[cfg(target_pointer_width = \"64\")]\nrustc_data_structures::static_assert_size!(PendingPredicateObligation<'_>, 72);\n\nimpl<'tcx, E> FulfillmentContext<'tcx, E>\nwhere\n    E: FromSolverError<'tcx, OldSolverError<'tcx>>,\n{\n    /// Creates a new fulfillment context.\n    pub(super) fn new(infcx: &InferCtxt<'tcx>) -> FulfillmentContext<'tcx, E> {\n        assert!(\n            !infcx.next_trait_solver(),\n            \"old trait solver fulfillment context created when \\\n            infcx is set up for new trait solver\"\n        );\n        FulfillmentContext {\n            predicates: ObligationForest::new(),\n            usable_in_snapshot: infcx.num_open_snapshots(),\n            _errors: PhantomData,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Attempts to select obligations using `selcx`.\n    fn select(&mut self, selcx: SelectionContext<'_, 'tcx>) -> Vec<E> {\n        let span = debug_span!(\"select\", obligation_forest_size = ?self.predicates.len());\n        let _enter = span.enter();\n        let infcx = selcx.infcx;\n\n        // Process pending obligations.\n        let outcome: Outcome<_, _> =\n            self.predicates.process_obligations(&mut FulfillProcessor { selcx });\n\n        // FIXME: if we kept the original cache key, we could mark projection\n        // obligations as complete for the projection cache here.\n\n        let errors: Vec<E> = outcome\n            .errors\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|err| E::from_solver_error(infcx, OldSolverError(err)))\n            .collect();\n\n        debug!(\n            \"select({} predicates remaining, {} errors) done\",\n            self.predicates.len(),\n            errors.len()\n        );\n\n        errors\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx, E> TraitEngine<'tcx, E> for FulfillmentContext<'tcx, E>\nwhere\n    E: FromSolverError<'tcx, OldSolverError<'tcx>>,\n{\n    #[inline]\n    fn register_predicate_obligation(\n        &mut self,\n        infcx: &InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n        mut obligation: PredicateObligation<'tcx>,\n    ) {\n        assert_eq!(self.usable_in_snapshot, infcx.num_open_snapshots());\n        // this helps to reduce duplicate errors, as well as making\n        // debug output much nicer to read and so on.\n        debug_assert!(!obligation.param_env.has_non_region_infer());\n        obligation.predicate = infcx.resolve_vars_if_possible(obligation.predicate);\n\n        debug!(?obligation, \"register_predicate_obligation\");\n\n        self.predicates\n            .register_obligation(PendingPredicateObligation { obligation, stalled_on: vec![] });\n    }\n\n    fn collect_remaining_errors(&mut self, infcx: &InferCtxt<'tcx>) -> Vec<E> {\n        self.predicates\n            .to_errors(FulfillmentErrorCode::Ambiguity { overflow: None })\n            .into_iter()\n            .map(|err| E::from_solver_error(infcx, OldSolverError(err)))\n            .collect()\n    }\n\n    fn try_evaluate_obligations(&mut self, infcx: &InferCtxt<'tcx>) -> Vec<E> {\n        let selcx = SelectionContext::new(infcx);\n        self.select(selcx)\n    }\n\n    fn drain_stalled_obligations_for_coroutines(\n        &mut self,\n        infcx: &InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n    ) -> PredicateObligations<'tcx> {\n        let stalled_coroutines = match infcx.typing_mode() {\n            TypingMode::Analysis { defining_opaque_types_and_generators } => {\n                defining_opaque_types_and_generators\n            }\n            TypingMode::Coherence\n            | TypingMode::Borrowck { defining_opaque_types: _ }\n            | TypingMode::PostBorrowckAnalysis { defined_opaque_types: _ }\n            | TypingMode::PostAnalysis => return Default::default(),\n        };\n\n        if stalled_coroutines.is_empty() {\n            return Default::default();\n        }\n\n        let mut processor = DrainProcessor {\n            infcx,\n            removed_predicates: PredicateObligations::new(),\n            stalled_coroutines,\n        };\n        let outcome: Outcome<_, _> = self.predicates.process_obligations(&mut processor);\n        assert!(outcome.errors.is_empty());\n        return processor.removed_predicates;\n\n        struct DrainProcessor<'a, 'tcx> {\n            infcx: &'a InferCtxt<'tcx>,\n            removed_predicates: PredicateObligations<'tcx>,\n            stalled_coroutines: &'tcx ty::List<LocalDefId>,\n        }\n\n        impl<'tcx> ObligationProcessor for DrainProcessor<'_, 'tcx> {\n            type Obligation = PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>;\n            type Error = !;\n            type OUT = Outcome<Self::Obligation, Self::Error>;\n\n            fn needs_process_obligation(&self, pending_obligation: &Self::Obligation) -> bool {\n                self.infcx\n                    .resolve_vars_if_possible(pending_obligation.obligation.predicate)\n                    .visit_with(&mut StalledOnCoroutines {\n                        stalled_coroutines: self.stalled_coroutines,\n                        span: DUMMY_SP,\n                        cache: Default::default(),\n                    })\n                    .is_break()\n            }\n\n            fn process_obligation(\n                &mut self,\n                pending_obligation: &mut PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>,\n            ) -> ProcessResult<PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>, !> {\n                assert!(self.needs_process_obligation(pending_obligation));\n                self.removed_predicates.push(pending_obligation.obligation.clone());\n                ProcessResult::Changed(Default::default())\n            }\n\n            fn process_backedge<'c, I>(\n                &mut self,\n                cycle: I,\n                _marker: PhantomData<&'c PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>>,\n            ) -> Result<(), !>\n            where\n                I: Clone + Iterator<Item = &'c PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>>,\n            {\n                self.removed_predicates.extend(cycle.map(|c| c.obligation.clone()));\n                Ok(())\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn has_pending_obligations(&self) -> bool {\n        self.predicates.has_pending_obligations()\n    }\n\n    fn pending_obligations(&self) -> PredicateObligations<'tcx> {\n        self.predicates.map_pending_obligations(|o| o.obligation.clone())\n    }\n}\n\nstruct FulfillProcessor<'a, 'tcx> {\n    selcx: SelectionContext<'a, 'tcx>,\n}\n\nfn mk_pending<'tcx>(\n    parent: &PredicateObligation<'tcx>,\n    os: PredicateObligations<'tcx>,\n) -> PendingPredicateObligations<'tcx> {\n    os.into_iter()\n        .map(|mut o| {\n            o.set_depth_from_parent(parent.recursion_depth);\n            PendingPredicateObligation { obligation: o, stalled_on: vec![] }\n        })\n        .collect()\n}\n\nimpl<'a, 'tcx> ObligationProcessor for FulfillProcessor<'a, 'tcx> {\n    type Obligation = PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>;\n    type Error = FulfillmentErrorCode<'tcx>;\n    type OUT = Outcome<Self::Obligation, Self::Error>;\n\n    /// Compared to `needs_process_obligation` this and its callees\n    /// contain some optimizations that come at the price of false negatives.\n    ///\n    /// They\n    /// - reduce branching by covering only the most common case\n    /// - take a read-only view of the unification tables which allows skipping undo_log\n    ///   construction.\n    /// - bail out on value-cache misses in ena to avoid pointer chasing\n    /// - hoist RefCell locking out of the loop\n    #[inline]\n    fn skippable_obligations<'b>(\n        &'b self,\n        it: impl Iterator<Item = &'b Self::Obligation>,\n    ) -> usize {\n        let is_unchanged = self.selcx.infcx.is_ty_infer_var_definitely_unchanged();\n\n        it.take_while(|o| match o.stalled_on.as_slice() {\n            [o] => is_unchanged(*o),\n            _ => false,\n        })\n        .count()\n    }\n\n    /// Identifies whether a predicate obligation needs processing.\n    ///\n    /// This is always inlined because it has a single callsite and it is\n    /// called *very* frequently. Be careful modifying this code! Several\n    /// compile-time benchmarks are very sensitive to even small changes.\n    #[inline(always)]\n    fn needs_process_obligation(&self, pending_obligation: &Self::Obligation) -> bool {\n        // If we were stalled on some unresolved variables, first check whether\n        // any of them have been resolved; if not, don't bother doing more work\n        // yet.\n        let stalled_on = &pending_obligation.stalled_on;\n        match stalled_on.len() {\n            // This case is the hottest most of the time, being hit up to 99%\n            // of the time. `keccak` and `cranelift-codegen-0.82.1` are\n            // benchmarks that particularly stress this path.\n            1 => self.selcx.infcx.ty_or_const_infer_var_changed(stalled_on[0]),\n\n            // In this case we haven't changed, but wish to make a change. Note\n            // that this is a special case, and is not equivalent to the `_`\n            // case below, which would return `false` for an empty `stalled_on`\n            // vector.\n            //\n            // This case is usually hit only 1% of the time or less, though it\n            // reaches 20% in `wasmparser-0.101.0`.\n            0 => true,\n\n            // This case is usually hit only 1% of the time or less, though it\n            // reaches 95% in `mime-0.3.16`, 64% in `wast-54.0.0`, and 12% in\n            // `inflate-0.4.5`.\n            //\n            // The obvious way of writing this, with a call to `any()` and no\n            // closure, is currently slower than this version.\n            _ => (|| {\n                for &infer_var in stalled_on {\n                    if self.selcx.infcx.ty_or_const_infer_var_changed(infer_var) {\n                        return true;\n                    }\n                }\n                false\n            })(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Processes a predicate obligation and returns either:\n    /// - `Changed(v)` if the predicate is true, presuming that `v` are also true\n    /// - `Unchanged` if we don't have enough info to be sure\n    /// - `Error(e)` if the predicate does not hold\n    ///\n    /// This is called much less often than `needs_process_obligation`, so we\n    /// never inline it.\n    #[inline(never)]\n    #[instrument(level = \"debug\", skip(self, pending_obligation))]\n    fn process_obligation(\n        &mut self,\n        pending_obligation: &mut PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>,\n    ) -> ProcessResult<PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>, FulfillmentErrorCode<'tcx>> {\n        pending_obligation.stalled_on.truncate(0);\n\n        let obligation = &mut pending_obligation.obligation;\n\n        debug!(?obligation, \"pre-resolve\");\n\n        if obligation.predicate.has_non_region_infer() {\n            obligation.predicate = self.selcx.infcx.resolve_vars_if_possible(obligation.predicate);\n        }\n\n        let obligation = &pending_obligation.obligation;\n\n        let infcx = self.selcx.infcx;\n\n        if sizedness_fast_path(infcx.tcx, obligation.predicate, obligation.param_env) {\n            return ProcessResult::Changed(thin_vec![]);\n        }\n\n        if obligation.predicate.has_aliases() {\n            let mut obligations = PredicateObligations::new();\n            let predicate = normalize_with_depth_to(\n                &mut self.selcx,\n                obligation.param_env,\n                obligation.cause.clone(),\n                obligation.recursion_depth + 1,\n                obligation.predicate,\n                &mut obligations,\n            );\n            if predicate != obligation.predicate {\n                obligations.push(obligation.with(infcx.tcx, predicate));\n                return ProcessResult::Changed(mk_pending(obligation, obligations));\n            }\n        }\n        let binder = obligation.predicate.kind();\n        match binder.no_bound_vars() {\n            None => match binder.skip_binder() {\n                // Evaluation will discard candidates using the leak check.\n                // This means we need to pass it the bound version of our\n                // predicate.\n                ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::Trait(trait_ref)) => {\n                    let trait_obligation = obligation.with(infcx.tcx, binder.rebind(trait_ref));\n\n                    self.process_trait_obligation(\n                        obligation,\n                        trait_obligation,\n                        &mut pending_obligation.stalled_on,\n                    )\n                }\n                ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::Projection(data)) => {\n                    let project_obligation = obligation.with(infcx.tcx, binder.rebind(data));\n\n                    self.process_projection_obligation(\n                        obligation,\n                        project_obligation,\n                        &mut pending_obligation.stalled_on,\n                    )\n                }\n                ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::RegionOutlives(_))\n                | ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::TypeOutlives(_))\n                | ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::ConstArgHasType(..))\n                | ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::WellFormed(_))\n                | ty::PredicateKind::DynCompatible(_)\n                | ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(_)\n                | ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(_)\n                | ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::ConstEvaluatable(..))\n                | ty::PredicateKind::ConstEquate(..)\n                // FIXME(const_trait_impl): We may need to do this using the higher-ranked\n                // pred instead of just instantiating it with placeholders b/c of\n                // higher-ranked implied bound issues in the old solver.\n                | ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::HostEffect(..)) => {\n                    let pred = ty::Binder::dummy(infcx.enter_forall_and_leak_universe(binder));\n                    let mut obligations = PredicateObligations::with_capacity(1);\n                    obligations.push(obligation.with(infcx.tcx, pred));\n\n                    ProcessResult::Changed(mk_pending(obligation, obligations))\n                }\n                ty::PredicateKind::Ambiguous => ProcessResult::Unchanged,\n                ty::PredicateKind::NormalizesTo(..) => {\n                    bug!(\"NormalizesTo is only used by the new solver\")\n                }\n                ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(..) => {\n                    bug!(\"AliasRelate is only used by the new solver\")\n                }\n                ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::UnstableFeature(_)) => {\n                   unreachable!(\"unexpected higher ranked `UnstableFeature` goal\")\n                }\n            },\n            Some(pred) => match pred {\n                ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::Trait(data)) => {\n                    let trait_obligation = obligation.with(infcx.tcx, Binder::dummy(data));\n\n                    self.process_trait_obligation(\n                        obligation,\n                        trait_obligation,\n                        &mut pending_obligation.stalled_on,\n                    )\n                }\n\n                ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::HostEffect(data)) => {\n                    let host_obligation = obligation.with(infcx.tcx, data);\n\n                    self.process_host_obligation(\n                        obligation,\n                        host_obligation,\n                        &mut pending_obligation.stalled_on,\n                    )\n                }\n\n                ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::RegionOutlives(data)) => {\n                    if infcx.considering_regions {\n                        infcx.register_region_outlives_constraint(data, &obligation.cause);\n                    }\n\n                    ProcessResult::Changed(Default::default())\n                }\n\n                ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::TypeOutlives(ty::OutlivesPredicate(\n                    t_a,\n                    r_b,\n                ))) => {\n                    if infcx.considering_regions {\n                        infcx.register_type_outlives_constraint(t_a, r_b, &obligation.cause);\n                    }\n                    ProcessResult::Changed(Default::default())\n                }\n\n                ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::Projection(ref data)) => {\n                    let project_obligation = obligation.with(infcx.tcx, Binder::dummy(*data));\n\n                    self.process_projection_obligation(\n                        obligation,\n                        project_obligation,\n                        &mut pending_obligation.stalled_on,\n                    )\n                }\n\n                ty::PredicateKind::DynCompatible(trait_def_id) => {\n                    if !self.selcx.tcx().is_dyn_compatible(trait_def_id) {\n                        ProcessResult::Error(FulfillmentErrorCode::Select(\n                            SelectionError::Unimplemented,\n                        ))\n                    } else {\n                        ProcessResult::Changed(Default::default())\n                    }\n                }\n\n                ty::PredicateKind::Ambiguous => ProcessResult::Unchanged,\n                ty::PredicateKind::NormalizesTo(..) => {\n                    bug!(\"NormalizesTo is only used by the new solver\")\n                }\n                ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(..) => {\n                    bug!(\"AliasRelate is only used by the new solver\")\n                }\n                // Compute `ConstArgHasType` above the overflow check below.\n                // This is because this is not ever a useful obligation to report\n                // as the cause of an overflow.\n                ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::ConstArgHasType(ct, ty)) => {\n                    let ct = infcx.shallow_resolve_const(ct);\n                    let ct_ty = match ct.kind() {\n                        ty::ConstKind::Infer(var) => {\n                            let var = match var {\n                                ty::InferConst::Var(vid) => TyOrConstInferVar::Const(vid),\n                                ty::InferConst::Fresh(_) => {\n                                    bug!(\"encountered fresh const in fulfill\")\n                                }\n                            };\n                            pending_obligation.stalled_on.clear();\n                            pending_obligation.stalled_on.extend([var]);\n                            return ProcessResult::Unchanged;\n                        }\n                        ty::ConstKind::Error(_) => {\n                            return ProcessResult::Changed(PendingPredicateObligations::new());\n                        }\n                        ty::ConstKind::Value(cv) => cv.ty,\n                        ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(uv) => {\n                            infcx.tcx.type_of(uv.def).instantiate(infcx.tcx, uv.args)\n                        }\n                        // FIXME(generic_const_exprs): we should construct an alias like\n                        // `<lhs_ty as Add<rhs_ty>>::Output` when this is an `Expr` representing\n                        // `lhs + rhs`.\n                        ty::ConstKind::Expr(_) => {\n                            return ProcessResult::Changed(mk_pending(\n                                obligation,\n                                PredicateObligations::new(),\n                            ));\n                        }\n                        ty::ConstKind::Placeholder(_) => {\n                            bug!(\"placeholder const {:?} in old solver\", ct)\n                        }\n                        ty::ConstKind::Bound(_, _) => bug!(\"escaping bound vars in {:?}\", ct),\n                        ty::ConstKind::Param(param_ct) => {\n                            param_ct.find_const_ty_from_env(obligation.param_env)\n                        }\n                    };\n\n                    match infcx.at(&obligation.cause, obligation.param_env).eq(\n                        // Only really exercised by generic_const_exprs\n                        DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes,\n                        ct_ty,\n                        ty,\n                    ) {\n                        Ok(inf_ok) => ProcessResult::Changed(mk_pending(\n                            obligation,\n                            inf_ok.into_obligations(),\n                        )),\n                        Err(_) => ProcessResult::Error(FulfillmentErrorCode::Select(\n                            SelectionError::ConstArgHasWrongType { ct, ct_ty, expected_ty: ty },\n                        )),\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // General case overflow check. Allow `process_trait_obligation`\n                // and `process_projection_obligation` to handle checking for\n                // the recursion limit themselves. Also don't check some\n                // predicate kinds that don't give further obligations.\n                _ if !self\n                    .selcx\n                    .tcx()\n                    .recursion_limit()\n                    .value_within_limit(obligation.recursion_depth) =>\n                {\n                    self.selcx.infcx.err_ctxt().report_overflow_obligation(&obligation, false);\n                }\n\n                ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::WellFormed(term)) => {\n                    if term.is_trivially_wf(self.selcx.tcx()) {\n                        return ProcessResult::Changed(thin_vec![]);\n                    }\n\n                    match wf::obligations(\n                        self.selcx.infcx,\n                        obligation.param_env,\n                        obligation.cause.body_id,\n                        obligation.recursion_depth + 1,\n                        term,\n                        obligation.cause.span,\n                    ) {\n                        None => {\n                            pending_obligation.stalled_on =\n                                vec![TyOrConstInferVar::maybe_from_term(term).unwrap()];\n                            ProcessResult::Unchanged\n                        }\n                        Some(os) => ProcessResult::Changed(mk_pending(obligation, os)),\n                    }\n                }\n\n                ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(subtype) => {\n                    match self.selcx.infcx.subtype_predicate(\n                        &obligation.cause,\n                        obligation.param_env,\n                        Binder::dummy(subtype),\n                    ) {\n                        Err((a, b)) => {\n                            // None means that both are unresolved.\n                            pending_obligation.stalled_on =\n                                vec![TyOrConstInferVar::Ty(a), TyOrConstInferVar::Ty(b)];\n                            ProcessResult::Unchanged\n                        }\n                        Ok(Ok(ok)) => {\n                            ProcessResult::Changed(mk_pending(obligation, ok.obligations))\n                        }\n                        Ok(Err(err)) => {\n                            let expected_found = if subtype.a_is_expected {\n                                ExpectedFound::new(subtype.a, subtype.b)\n                            } else {\n                                ExpectedFound::new(subtype.b, subtype.a)\n                            };\n                            ProcessResult::Error(FulfillmentErrorCode::Subtype(expected_found, err))\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n\n                ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(coerce) => {\n                    match self.selcx.infcx.coerce_predicate(\n                        &obligation.cause,\n                        obligation.param_env,\n                        Binder::dummy(coerce),\n                    ) {\n                        Err((a, b)) => {\n                            // None means that both are unresolved.\n                            pending_obligation.stalled_on =\n                                vec![TyOrConstInferVar::Ty(a), TyOrConstInferVar::Ty(b)];\n                            ProcessResult::Unchanged\n                        }\n                        Ok(Ok(ok)) => {\n                            ProcessResult::Changed(mk_pending(obligation, ok.obligations))\n                        }\n                        Ok(Err(err)) => {\n                            let expected_found = ExpectedFound::new(coerce.b, coerce.a);\n                            ProcessResult::Error(FulfillmentErrorCode::Subtype(expected_found, err))\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n\n                ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::ConstEvaluatable(uv)) => {\n                    match const_evaluatable::is_const_evaluatable(\n                        self.selcx.infcx,\n                        uv,\n                        obligation.param_env,\n                        obligation.cause.span,\n                    ) {\n                        Ok(()) => ProcessResult::Changed(Default::default()),\n                        Err(NotConstEvaluatable::MentionsInfer) => {\n                            pending_obligation.stalled_on.clear();\n                            pending_obligation.stalled_on.extend(\n                                uv.walk().filter_map(TyOrConstInferVar::maybe_from_generic_arg),\n                            );\n                            ProcessResult::Unchanged\n                        }\n                        Err(\n                            e @ NotConstEvaluatable::MentionsParam\n                            | e @ NotConstEvaluatable::Error(_),\n                        ) => ProcessResult::Error(FulfillmentErrorCode::Select(\n                            SelectionError::NotConstEvaluatable(e),\n                        )),\n                    }\n                }\n\n                ty::PredicateKind::ConstEquate(c1, c2) => {\n                    let tcx = self.selcx.tcx();\n                    assert!(\n                        tcx.features().generic_const_exprs(),\n                        \"`ConstEquate` without a feature gate: {c1:?} {c2:?}\",\n                    );\n                    // FIXME: we probably should only try to unify abstract constants\n                    // if the constants depend on generic parameters.\n                    //\n                    // Let's just see where this breaks :shrug:\n                    {\n                        let c1 = tcx.expand_abstract_consts(c1);\n                        let c2 = tcx.expand_abstract_consts(c2);\n                        debug!(\"equating consts:\\nc1= {:?}\\nc2= {:?}\", c1, c2);\n\n                        use rustc_hir::def::DefKind;\n                        match (c1.kind(), c2.kind()) {\n                            (ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(a), ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(b))\n                                if a.def == b.def && tcx.def_kind(a.def) == DefKind::AssocConst =>\n                            {\n                                if let Ok(new_obligations) = infcx\n                                    .at(&obligation.cause, obligation.param_env)\n                                    // Can define opaque types as this is only reachable with\n                                    // `generic_const_exprs`\n                                    .eq(\n                                        DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes,\n                                        ty::AliasTerm::from(a),\n                                        ty::AliasTerm::from(b),\n                                    )\n                                {\n                                    return ProcessResult::Changed(mk_pending(\n                                        obligation,\n                                        new_obligations.into_obligations(),\n                                    ));\n                                }\n                            }\n                            (_, ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(_))\n                            | (ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(_), _) => (),\n                            (_, _) => {\n                                if let Ok(new_obligations) = infcx\n                                    .at(&obligation.cause, obligation.param_env)\n                                    // Can define opaque types as this is only reachable with\n                                    // `generic_const_exprs`\n                                    .eq(DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes, c1, c2)\n                                {\n                                    return ProcessResult::Changed(mk_pending(\n                                        obligation,\n                                        new_obligations.into_obligations(),\n                                    ));\n                                }\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n\n                    let stalled_on = &mut pending_obligation.stalled_on;\n\n                    let mut evaluate = |c: Const<'tcx>| {\n                        if let ty::ConstKind::Unevaluated(unevaluated) = c.kind() {\n                            match super::try_evaluate_const(\n                                self.selcx.infcx,\n                                c,\n                                obligation.param_env,\n                            ) {\n                                Ok(val) => Ok(val),\n                                e @ Err(EvaluateConstErr::HasGenericsOrInfers) => {\n                                    stalled_on.extend(\n                                        unevaluated\n                                            .args\n                                            .iter()\n                                            .filter_map(TyOrConstInferVar::maybe_from_generic_arg),\n                                    );\n                                    e\n                                }\n                                e @ Err(\n                                    EvaluateConstErr::EvaluationFailure(_)\n                                    | EvaluateConstErr::InvalidConstParamTy(_),\n                                ) => e,\n                            }\n                        } else {\n                            Ok(c)\n                        }\n                    };\n\n                    match (evaluate(c1), evaluate(c2)) {\n                        (Ok(c1), Ok(c2)) => {\n                            match self.selcx.infcx.at(&obligation.cause, obligation.param_env).eq(\n                                // Can define opaque types as this is only reachable with\n                                // `generic_const_exprs`\n                                DefineOpaqueTypes::Yes,\n                                c1,\n                                c2,\n                            ) {\n                                Ok(inf_ok) => ProcessResult::Changed(mk_pending(\n                                    obligation,\n                                    inf_ok.into_obligations(),\n                                )),\n                                Err(err) => {\n                                    ProcessResult::Error(FulfillmentErrorCode::ConstEquate(\n                                        ExpectedFound::new(c1, c2),\n                                        err,\n                                    ))\n                                }\n                            }\n                        }\n                        (Err(EvaluateConstErr::InvalidConstParamTy(e)), _)\n                        | (_, Err(EvaluateConstErr::InvalidConstParamTy(e))) => {\n                            ProcessResult::Error(FulfillmentErrorCode::Select(\n                                SelectionError::NotConstEvaluatable(NotConstEvaluatable::Error(e)),\n                            ))\n                        }\n                        (Err(EvaluateConstErr::EvaluationFailure(e)), _)\n                        | (_, Err(EvaluateConstErr::EvaluationFailure(e))) => {\n                            ProcessResult::Error(FulfillmentErrorCode::Select(\n                                SelectionError::NotConstEvaluatable(NotConstEvaluatable::Error(e)),\n                            ))\n                        }\n                        (Err(EvaluateConstErr::HasGenericsOrInfers), _)\n                        | (_, Err(EvaluateConstErr::HasGenericsOrInfers)) => {\n                            if c1.has_non_region_infer() || c2.has_non_region_infer() {\n                                ProcessResult::Unchanged\n                            } else {\n                                // Two different constants using generic parameters ~> error.\n                                let expected_found = ExpectedFound::new(c1, c2);\n                                ProcessResult::Error(FulfillmentErrorCode::ConstEquate(\n                                    expected_found,\n                                    TypeError::ConstMismatch(expected_found),\n                                ))\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                ty::PredicateKind::Clause(ty::ClauseKind::UnstableFeature(symbol)) => {\n                    if may_use_unstable_feature(self.selcx.infcx, obligation.param_env, symbol) {\n                        ProcessResult::Changed(Default::default())\n                    } else {\n                        ProcessResult::Unchanged\n                    }\n                }\n            },\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[inline(never)]\n    fn process_backedge<'c, I>(\n        &mut self,\n        cycle: I,\n        _marker: PhantomData<&'c PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>>,\n    ) -> Result<(), FulfillmentErrorCode<'tcx>>\n    where\n        I: Clone + Iterator<Item = &'c PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        if self.selcx.coinductive_match(cycle.clone().map(|s| s.obligation.predicate)) {\n            debug!(\"process_child_obligations: coinductive match\");\n            Ok(())\n        } else {\n            let cycle = cycle.map(|c| c.obligation.clone()).collect();\n            Err(FulfillmentErrorCode::Cycle(cycle))\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'a, 'tcx> FulfillProcessor<'a, 'tcx> {\n    #[instrument(level = \"debug\", skip(self, obligation, stalled_on))]\n    fn process_trait_obligation(\n        &mut self,\n        obligation: &PredicateObligation<'tcx>,\n        trait_obligation: PolyTraitObligation<'tcx>,\n        stalled_on: &mut Vec<TyOrConstInferVar>,\n    ) -> ProcessResult<PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>, FulfillmentErrorCode<'tcx>> {\n        let infcx = self.selcx.infcx;\n        if obligation.predicate.is_global() && !matches!(infcx.typing_mode(), TypingMode::Coherence)\n        {\n            // no type variables present, can use evaluation for better caching.\n            // FIXME: consider caching errors too.\n            if infcx.predicate_must_hold_considering_regions(obligation) {\n                debug!(\n                    \"selecting trait at depth {} evaluated to holds\",\n                    obligation.recursion_depth\n                );\n                return ProcessResult::Changed(Default::default());\n            }\n        }\n\n        match self.selcx.poly_select(&trait_obligation) {\n            Ok(Some(impl_source)) => {\n                debug!(\"selecting trait at depth {} yielded Ok(Some)\", obligation.recursion_depth);\n                ProcessResult::Changed(mk_pending(obligation, impl_source.nested_obligations()))\n            }\n            Ok(None) => {\n                debug!(\"selecting trait at depth {} yielded Ok(None)\", obligation.recursion_depth);\n\n                // This is a bit subtle: for the most part, the\n                // only reason we can fail to make progress on\n                // trait selection is because we don't have enough\n                // information about the types in the trait.\n                stalled_on.clear();\n                stalled_on.extend(args_infer_vars(\n                    &self.selcx,\n                    trait_obligation.predicate.map_bound(|pred| pred.trait_ref.args),\n                ));\n\n                debug!(\n                    \"process_predicate: pending obligation {:?} now stalled on {:?}\",\n                    infcx.resolve_vars_if_possible(obligation.clone()),\n                    stalled_on\n                );\n\n                ProcessResult::Unchanged\n            }\n            Err(selection_err) => {\n                debug!(\"selecting trait at depth {} yielded Err\", obligation.recursion_depth);\n\n                ProcessResult::Error(FulfillmentErrorCode::Select(selection_err))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn process_projection_obligation(\n        &mut self,\n        obligation: &PredicateObligation<'tcx>,\n        project_obligation: PolyProjectionObligation<'tcx>,\n        stalled_on: &mut Vec<TyOrConstInferVar>,\n    ) -> ProcessResult<PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>, FulfillmentErrorCode<'tcx>> {\n        let tcx = self.selcx.tcx();\n        let infcx = self.selcx.infcx;\n        if obligation.predicate.is_global() && !matches!(infcx.typing_mode(), TypingMode::Coherence)\n        {\n            // no type variables present, can use evaluation for better caching.\n            // FIXME: consider caching errors too.\n            if infcx.predicate_must_hold_considering_regions(obligation) {\n                if let Some(key) = ProjectionCacheKey::from_poly_projection_obligation(\n                    &mut self.selcx,\n                    &project_obligation,\n                ) {\n                    // If `predicate_must_hold_considering_regions` succeeds, then we've\n                    // evaluated all sub-obligations. We can therefore mark the 'root'\n                    // obligation as complete, and skip evaluating sub-obligations.\n                    infcx\n                        .inner\n                        .borrow_mut()\n                        .projection_cache()\n                        .complete(key, EvaluationResult::EvaluatedToOk);\n                }\n                return ProcessResult::Changed(Default::default());\n            } else {\n                debug!(\"Does NOT hold: {:?}\", obligation);\n            }\n        }\n\n        match project::poly_project_and_unify_term(&mut self.selcx, &project_obligation) {\n            ProjectAndUnifyResult::Holds(os) => ProcessResult::Changed(mk_pending(obligation, os)),\n            ProjectAndUnifyResult::FailedNormalization => {\n                stalled_on.clear();\n                stalled_on.extend(args_infer_vars(\n                    &self.selcx,\n                    project_obligation.predicate.map_bound(|pred| pred.projection_term.args),\n                ));\n                ProcessResult::Unchanged\n            }\n            // Let the caller handle the recursion\n            ProjectAndUnifyResult::Recursive => {\n                let mut obligations = PredicateObligations::with_capacity(1);\n                obligations.push(project_obligation.with(tcx, project_obligation.predicate));\n\n                ProcessResult::Changed(mk_pending(obligation, obligations))\n            }\n            ProjectAndUnifyResult::MismatchedProjectionTypes(e) => {\n                ProcessResult::Error(FulfillmentErrorCode::Project(e))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn process_host_obligation(\n        &mut self,\n        obligation: &PredicateObligation<'tcx>,\n        host_obligation: HostEffectObligation<'tcx>,\n        stalled_on: &mut Vec<TyOrConstInferVar>,\n    ) -> ProcessResult<PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>, FulfillmentErrorCode<'tcx>> {\n        match effects::evaluate_host_effect_obligation(&mut self.selcx, &host_obligation) {\n            Ok(nested) => ProcessResult::Changed(mk_pending(obligation, nested)),\n            Err(effects::EvaluationFailure::Ambiguous) => {\n                stalled_on.clear();\n                stalled_on.extend(args_infer_vars(\n                    &self.selcx,\n                    ty::Binder::dummy(host_obligation.predicate.trait_ref.args),\n                ));\n                ProcessResult::Unchanged\n            }\n            Err(effects::EvaluationFailure::NoSolution) => {\n                ProcessResult::Error(FulfillmentErrorCode::Select(SelectionError::Unimplemented))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Returns the set of inference variables contained in `args`.\nfn args_infer_vars<'tcx>(\n    selcx: &SelectionContext<'_, 'tcx>,\n    args: ty::Binder<'tcx, GenericArgsRef<'tcx>>,\n) -> impl Iterator<Item = TyOrConstInferVar> {\n    selcx\n        .infcx\n        .resolve_vars_if_possible(args)\n        .skip_binder() // ok because this check doesn't care about regions\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|arg| arg.has_non_region_infer())\n        .flat_map(|arg| {\n            let mut walker = arg.walk();\n            while let Some(c) = walker.next() {\n                if !c.has_non_region_infer() {\n                    walker.visited.remove(&c);\n                    walker.skip_current_subtree();\n                }\n            }\n            walker.visited.into_iter()\n        })\n        .filter_map(TyOrConstInferVar::maybe_from_generic_arg)\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct OldSolverError<'tcx>(\n    Error<PendingPredicateObligation<'tcx>, FulfillmentErrorCode<'tcx>>,\n);\n\nimpl<'tcx> FromSolverError<'tcx, OldSolverError<'tcx>> for FulfillmentError<'tcx> {\n    fn from_solver_error(_infcx: &InferCtxt<'tcx>, error: OldSolverError<'tcx>) -> Self {\n        let mut iter = error.0.backtrace.into_iter();\n        let obligation = iter.next().unwrap().obligation;\n        // The root obligation is the last item in the backtrace - if there's only\n        // one item, then it's the same as the main obligation\n        let root_obligation = iter.next_back().map_or_else(|| obligation.clone(), |e| e.obligation);\n        FulfillmentError::new(obligation, error.0.error, root_obligation)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> FromSolverError<'tcx, OldSolverError<'tcx>> for ScrubbedTraitError<'tcx> {\n    fn from_solver_error(_infcx: &InferCtxt<'tcx>, error: OldSolverError<'tcx>) -> Self {\n        match error.0.error {\n            FulfillmentErrorCode::Select(_)\n            | FulfillmentErrorCode::Project(_)\n            | FulfillmentErrorCode::Subtype(_, _)\n            | FulfillmentErrorCode::ConstEquate(_, _) => ScrubbedTraitError::TrueError,\n            FulfillmentErrorCode::Ambiguity { overflow: _ } => ScrubbedTraitError::Ambiguity,\n            FulfillmentErrorCode::Cycle(cycle) => ScrubbedTraitError::Cycle(cycle),\n        }\n    }\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        "                         }",
        "                         Ok(Err(err)) => {",
        "                             let expected_found = if subtype.a_is_expected {",
        "-                                ExpectedFound::new(subtype.a, subtype.b)",
        "+                                ExpectedFound::new(subtype.sub_ty, subtype.super_ty)",
        "                             } else {",
        "-                                ExpectedFound::new(subtype.b, subtype.a)",
        "+                                ExpectedFound::new(subtype.super_ty, subtype.sub_ty)",
        "                             };",
        "                             ProcessResult::Error(FulfillmentErrorCode::Subtype(expected_found, err))",
        "                         }"
      ],
      "newLines": 9,
      "newStart": 612,
      "oldLines": 9,
      "oldStart": 612
    }
  ],
  "userModified": false
}

  "file": {
    "content": "        self.term.print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::SubtypeRelation<'tcx> {\n        self.a.print(p)?;\n        write!(p, \" <: \")?;\n        p.reset_type_limit();\n        self.b.print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::CoercePredicate<'tcx> {\n        self.a.print(p)?;\n        write!(p, \" -> \")?;\n        p.reset_type_limit();\n        self.b.print(p)?;",
    "filePath": "/workspace/code/compiler/rustc_middle/src/ty/print/pretty.rs",

    "content": "    ty::ExistentialProjection<'tcx> {\n        let name = p.tcx().associated_item(self.def_id).name();\n        // The args don't contain the self ty (as it has been erased) but the corresp.\n        // generics do as the trait always has a self ty param. We need to offset.\n        let args = &self.args[p.tcx().generics_of(self.def_id).parent_count - 1..];\n        p.print_path_with_generic_args(|p| write!(p, \"{name}\"), args)?;\n        write!(p, \" = \")?;\n        self.term.print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::ProjectionPredicate<'tcx> {\n        self.projection_term.print(p)?;\n        write!(p, \" == \")?;\n        p.reset_type_limit();\n        self.term.print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::SubtypeRelation<'tcx> {\n        self.a.print(p)?;\n        write!(p, \" <: \")?;\n        p.reset_type_limit();\n        self.b.print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::CoercePredicate<'tcx> {\n        self.a.print(p)?;\n        write!(p, \" -> \")?;\n        p.reset_type_limit();\n        self.b.print(p)?;\n    }",

  "originalFile": "use std::cell::Cell;\nuse std::fmt::{self, Write as _};\nuse std::iter;\nuse std::ops::{Deref, DerefMut};\n\nuse rustc_abi::{ExternAbi, Size};\nuse rustc_apfloat::Float;\nuse rustc_apfloat::ieee::{Double, Half, Quad, Single};\nuse rustc_data_structures::fx::{FxIndexMap, IndexEntry};\nuse rustc_data_structures::unord::UnordMap;\nuse rustc_hir as hir;\nuse rustc_hir::LangItem;\nuse rustc_hir::def::{self, CtorKind, DefKind, Namespace};\nuse rustc_hir::def_id::{DefIdMap, DefIdSet, LOCAL_CRATE, ModDefId};\nuse rustc_hir::definitions::{DefKey, DefPathDataName};\nuse rustc_hir::limit::Limit;\nuse rustc_macros::{Lift, extension};\nuse rustc_session::cstore::{ExternCrate, ExternCrateSource};\nuse rustc_span::{FileNameDisplayPreference, Ident, Symbol, kw, sym};\nuse rustc_type_ir::{Upcast as _, elaborate};\nuse smallvec::SmallVec;\n\n// `pretty` is a separate module only for organization.\nuse super::*;\nuse crate::mir::interpret::{AllocRange, GlobalAlloc, Pointer, Provenance, Scalar};\nuse crate::query::{IntoQueryParam, Providers};\nuse crate::ty::{\n    ConstInt, Expr, GenericArgKind, ParamConst, ScalarInt, Term, TermKind, TraitPredicate,\n    TypeFoldable, TypeSuperFoldable, TypeSuperVisitable, TypeVisitable, TypeVisitableExt,\n};\n\nthread_local! {\n    static FORCE_IMPL_FILENAME_LINE: Cell<bool> = const { Cell::new(false) };\n    static SHOULD_PREFIX_WITH_CRATE_NAME: Cell<bool> = const { Cell::new(false) };\n    static SHOULD_PREFIX_WITH_CRATE: Cell<bool> = const { Cell::new(false) };\n    static NO_TRIMMED_PATH: Cell<bool> = const { Cell::new(false) };\n    static FORCE_TRIMMED_PATH: Cell<bool> = const { Cell::new(false) };\n    static REDUCED_QUERIES: Cell<bool> = const { Cell::new(false) };\n    static NO_VISIBLE_PATH: Cell<bool> = const { Cell::new(false) };\n    static NO_VISIBLE_PATH_IF_DOC_HIDDEN: Cell<bool> = const { Cell::new(false) };\n    static RTN_MODE: Cell<RtnMode> = const { Cell::new(RtnMode::ForDiagnostic) };\n}\n\n/// Rendering style for RTN types.\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Debug)]\npub enum RtnMode {\n    /// Print the RTN type as an impl trait with its path, i.e.e `impl Sized { T::method(..) }`.\n    ForDiagnostic,\n    /// Print the RTN type as an impl trait, i.e. `impl Sized`.\n    ForSignature,\n    /// Print the RTN type as a value path, i.e. `T::method(..): ...`.\n    ForSuggestion,\n}\n\nmacro_rules! define_helper {\n    ($($(#[$a:meta])* fn $name:ident($helper:ident, $tl:ident);)+) => {\n        $(\n            #[must_use]\n            pub struct $helper(bool);\n\n            impl $helper {\n                pub fn new() -> $helper {\n                    $helper($tl.replace(true))\n                }\n            }\n\n            $(#[$a])*\n            pub macro $name($e:expr) {\n                {\n                    let _guard = $helper::new();\n                    $e\n                }\n            }\n\n            impl Drop for $helper {\n                fn drop(&mut self) {\n                    $tl.set(self.0)\n                }\n            }\n\n            pub fn $name() -> bool {\n                $tl.get()\n            }\n        )+\n    }\n}\n\ndefine_helper!(\n    /// Avoids running select queries during any prints that occur\n    /// during the closure. This may alter the appearance of some\n    /// types (e.g. forcing verbose printing for opaque types).\n    /// This method is used during some queries (e.g. `explicit_item_bounds`\n    /// for opaque types), to ensure that any debug printing that\n    /// occurs during the query computation does not end up recursively\n    /// calling the same query.\n    fn with_reduced_queries(ReducedQueriesGuard, REDUCED_QUERIES);\n    /// Force us to name impls with just the filename/line number. We\n    /// normally try to use types. But at some points, notably while printing\n    /// cycle errors, this can result in extra or suboptimal error output,\n    /// so this variable disables that check.\n    fn with_forced_impl_filename_line(ForcedImplGuard, FORCE_IMPL_FILENAME_LINE);\n    /// Adds the crate name prefix to paths where appropriate.\n    /// Unlike `with_crate_prefix`, this unconditionally uses `tcx.crate_name` instead of sometimes\n    /// using `crate::` for local items.\n    ///\n    /// Overrides `with_crate_prefix`.\n\n    // This function is not currently used in-tree, but it's used by a downstream rustc-driver in\n    // Ferrocene. Please check with them before removing it.\n    fn with_resolve_crate_name(CrateNamePrefixGuard, SHOULD_PREFIX_WITH_CRATE_NAME);\n    /// Adds the `crate::` prefix to paths where appropriate.\n    ///\n    /// Ignored if `with_resolve_crate_name` is active.\n    fn with_crate_prefix(CratePrefixGuard, SHOULD_PREFIX_WITH_CRATE);\n    /// Prevent path trimming if it is turned on. Path trimming affects `Display` impl\n    /// of various rustc types, for example `std::vec::Vec` would be trimmed to `Vec`,\n    /// if no other `Vec` is found.\n    fn with_no_trimmed_paths(NoTrimmedGuard, NO_TRIMMED_PATH);\n    fn with_forced_trimmed_paths(ForceTrimmedGuard, FORCE_TRIMMED_PATH);\n    /// Prevent selection of visible paths. `Display` impl of DefId will prefer\n    /// visible (public) reexports of types as paths.\n    fn with_no_visible_paths(NoVisibleGuard, NO_VISIBLE_PATH);\n    /// Prevent selection of visible paths if the paths are through a doc hidden path.\n    fn with_no_visible_paths_if_doc_hidden(NoVisibleIfDocHiddenGuard, NO_VISIBLE_PATH_IF_DOC_HIDDEN);\n);\n\n#[must_use]\npub struct RtnModeHelper(RtnMode);\n\nimpl RtnModeHelper {\n    pub fn with(mode: RtnMode) -> RtnModeHelper {\n        RtnModeHelper(RTN_MODE.with(|c| c.replace(mode)))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Drop for RtnModeHelper {\n    fn drop(&mut self) {\n        RTN_MODE.with(|c| c.set(self.0))\n    }\n}\n\n/// Print types for the purposes of a suggestion.\n///\n/// Specifically, this will render RPITITs as `T::method(..)` which is suitable for\n/// things like where-clauses.\npub macro with_types_for_suggestion($e:expr) {{\n    let _guard = $crate::ty::print::pretty::RtnModeHelper::with(RtnMode::ForSuggestion);\n    $e\n}}\n\n/// Print types for the purposes of a signature suggestion.\n///\n/// Specifically, this will render RPITITs as `impl Trait` rather than `T::method(..)`.\npub macro with_types_for_signature($e:expr) {{\n    let _guard = $crate::ty::print::pretty::RtnModeHelper::with(RtnMode::ForSignature);\n    $e\n}}\n\n/// Avoids running any queries during prints.\npub macro with_no_queries($e:expr) {{\n    $crate::ty::print::with_reduced_queries!($crate::ty::print::with_forced_impl_filename_line!(\n        $crate::ty::print::with_no_trimmed_paths!($crate::ty::print::with_no_visible_paths!(\n            $crate::ty::print::with_forced_impl_filename_line!($e)\n        ))\n    ))\n}}\n\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum WrapBinderMode {\n    ForAll,\n    Unsafe,\n}\nimpl WrapBinderMode {\n    pub fn start_str(self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            WrapBinderMode::ForAll => \"for<\",\n            WrapBinderMode::Unsafe => \"unsafe<\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// The \"region highlights\" are used to control region printing during\n/// specific error messages. When a \"region highlight\" is enabled, it\n/// gives an alternate way to print specific regions. For now, we\n/// always print those regions using a number, so something like \"`'0`\".\n///\n/// Regions not selected by the region highlight mode are presently\n/// unaffected.\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Default)]\npub struct RegionHighlightMode<'tcx> {\n    /// If enabled, when we see the selected region, use \"`'N`\"\n    /// instead of the ordinary behavior.\n    highlight_regions: [Option<(ty::Region<'tcx>, usize)>; 3],\n\n    /// If enabled, when printing a \"free region\" that originated from\n    /// the given `ty::BoundRegionKind`, print it as \"`'1`\". Free regions that would ordinarily\n    /// have names print as normal.\n    ///\n    /// This is used when you have a signature like `fn foo(x: &u32,\n    /// y: &'a u32)` and we want to give a name to the region of the\n    /// reference `x`.\n    highlight_bound_region: Option<(ty::BoundRegionKind, usize)>,\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> RegionHighlightMode<'tcx> {\n    /// If `region` and `number` are both `Some`, invokes\n    /// `highlighting_region`.\n    pub fn maybe_highlighting_region(\n        &mut self,\n        region: Option<ty::Region<'tcx>>,\n        number: Option<usize>,\n    ) {\n        if let Some(k) = region\n            && let Some(n) = number\n        {\n            self.highlighting_region(k, n);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Highlights the region inference variable `vid` as `'N`.\n    pub fn highlighting_region(&mut self, region: ty::Region<'tcx>, number: usize) {\n        let num_slots = self.highlight_regions.len();\n        let first_avail_slot =\n            self.highlight_regions.iter_mut().find(|s| s.is_none()).unwrap_or_else(|| {\n                bug!(\"can only highlight {} placeholders at a time\", num_slots,)\n            });\n        *first_avail_slot = Some((region, number));\n    }\n\n    /// Convenience wrapper for `highlighting_region`.\n    pub fn highlighting_region_vid(\n        &mut self,\n        tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n        vid: ty::RegionVid,\n        number: usize,\n    ) {\n        self.highlighting_region(ty::Region::new_var(tcx, vid), number)\n    }\n\n    /// Returns `Some(n)` with the number to use for the given region, if any.\n    fn region_highlighted(&self, region: ty::Region<'tcx>) -> Option<usize> {\n        self.highlight_regions.iter().find_map(|h| match h {\n            Some((r, n)) if *r == region => Some(*n),\n            _ => None,\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Highlight the given bound region.\n    /// We can only highlight one bound region at a time. See\n    /// the field `highlight_bound_region` for more detailed notes.\n    pub fn highlighting_bound_region(&mut self, br: ty::BoundRegionKind, number: usize) {\n        assert!(self.highlight_bound_region.is_none());\n        self.highlight_bound_region = Some((br, number));\n    }\n}\n\n/// Trait for printers that pretty-print using `fmt::Write` to the printer.\npub trait PrettyPrinter<'tcx>: Printer<'tcx> + fmt::Write {\n    /// Like `print_def_path` but for value paths.\n    fn pretty_print_value_path(\n        &mut self,\n        def_id: DefId,\n        args: &'tcx [GenericArg<'tcx>],\n    ) -> Result<(), PrintError> {\n        self.print_def_path(def_id, args)\n    }\n\n    fn pretty_print_in_binder<T>(&mut self, value: &ty::Binder<'tcx, T>) -> Result<(), PrintError>\n    where\n        T: Print<'tcx, Self> + TypeFoldable<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        value.as_ref().skip_binder().print(self)\n    }\n\n    fn wrap_binder<T, F: FnOnce(&T, &mut Self) -> Result<(), fmt::Error>>(\n        &mut self,\n        value: &ty::Binder<'tcx, T>,\n        _mode: WrapBinderMode,\n        f: F,\n    ) -> Result<(), PrintError>\n    where\n        T: TypeFoldable<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        f(value.as_ref().skip_binder(), self)\n    }\n\n    /// Prints comma-separated elements.\n    fn comma_sep<T>(&mut self, mut elems: impl Iterator<Item = T>) -> Result<(), PrintError>\n    where\n        T: Print<'tcx, Self>,\n    {\n        if let Some(first) = elems.next() {\n            first.print(self)?;\n            for elem in elems {\n                self.write_str(\", \")?;\n                elem.print(self)?;\n            }\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Prints `{f: t}` or `{f as t}` depending on the `cast` argument\n    fn typed_value(\n        &mut self,\n        f: impl FnOnce(&mut Self) -> Result<(), PrintError>,\n        t: impl FnOnce(&mut Self) -> Result<(), PrintError>,\n        conversion: &str,\n    ) -> Result<(), PrintError> {\n        self.write_str(\"{\")?;\n        f(self)?;\n        self.write_str(conversion)?;\n        t(self)?;\n        self.write_str(\"}\")?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Prints `(...)` around what `f` prints.\n    fn parenthesized(\n        &mut self,\n        f: impl FnOnce(&mut Self) -> Result<(), PrintError>,\n    ) -> Result<(), PrintError> {\n        self.write_str(\"(\")?;\n        f(self)?;\n        self.write_str(\")\")?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Prints `(...)` around what `f` prints if `parenthesized` is true, otherwise just prints `f`.\n    fn maybe_parenthesized(\n        &mut self,\n        f: impl FnOnce(&mut Self) -> Result<(), PrintError>,\n        parenthesized: bool,\n    ) -> Result<(), PrintError> {\n        if parenthesized {\n            self.parenthesized(f)?;\n        } else {\n            f(self)?;\n        }\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    /// Prints `<...>` around what `f` prints.\n    fn generic_delimiters(\n        &mut self,\n        f: impl FnOnce(&mut Self) -> Result<(), PrintError>,\n    ) -> Result<(), PrintError>;\n\n    fn should_truncate(&mut self) -> bool {\n        false\n    }\n\n    /// Returns `true` if the region should be printed in optional positions,\n    /// e.g., `&'a T` or `dyn Tr + 'b`. (Regions like the one in `Cow<'static, T>`\n    /// will always be printed.)\n    fn should_print_optional_region(&self, region: ty::Region<'tcx>) -> bool;\n\n    fn reset_type_limit(&mut self) {}\n\n    // Defaults (should not be overridden):\n\n    /// If possible, this returns a global path resolving to `def_id` that is visible\n    /// from at least one local module, and returns `true`. If the crate defining `def_id` is\n    /// declared with an `extern crate`, the path is guaranteed to use the `extern crate`.\n    fn try_print_visible_def_path(&mut self, def_id: DefId) -> Result<bool, PrintError> {\n        if with_no_visible_paths() {\n            return Ok(false);\n        }\n\n        let mut callers = Vec::new();\n        self.try_print_visible_def_path_recur(def_id, &mut callers)\n    }\n\n    // Given a `DefId`, produce a short name. For types and traits, it prints *only* its name,\n    // For associated items on traits it prints out the trait's name and the associated item's name.\n    // For enum variants, if they have an unique name, then we only print the name, otherwise we\n    // print the enum name and the variant name. Otherwise, we do not print anything and let the\n    // caller use the `print_def_path` fallback.\n    fn force_print_trimmed_def_path(&mut self, def_id: DefId) -> Result<bool, PrintError> {\n        let key = self.tcx().def_key(def_id);\n        let visible_parent_map = self.tcx().visible_parent_map(());\n        let kind = self.tcx().def_kind(def_id);\n\n        let get_local_name = |this: &Self, name, def_id, key: DefKey| {\n            if let Some(visible_parent) = visible_parent_map.get(&def_id)\n                && let actual_parent = this.tcx().opt_parent(def_id)\n                && let DefPathData::TypeNs(_) = key.disambiguated_data.data\n                && Some(*visible_parent) != actual_parent\n            {\n                this.tcx()\n                    // FIXME(typed_def_id): Further propagate ModDefId\n                    .module_children(ModDefId::new_unchecked(*visible_parent))\n                    .iter()\n                    .filter(|child| child.res.opt_def_id() == Some(def_id))\n                    .find(|child| child.vis.is_public() && child.ident.name != kw::Underscore)\n                    .map(|child| child.ident.name)\n                    .unwrap_or(name)\n            } else {\n                name\n            }\n        };\n        if let DefKind::Variant = kind\n            && let Some(symbol) = self.tcx().trimmed_def_paths(()).get(&def_id)\n        {\n            // If `Assoc` is unique, we don't want to talk about `Trait::Assoc`.\n            self.write_str(get_local_name(self, *symbol, def_id, key).as_str())?;\n            return Ok(true);\n        }\n        if let Some(symbol) = key.get_opt_name() {\n            if let DefKind::AssocConst | DefKind::AssocFn | DefKind::AssocTy = kind\n                && let Some(parent) = self.tcx().opt_parent(def_id)\n                && let parent_key = self.tcx().def_key(parent)\n                && let Some(symbol) = parent_key.get_opt_name()\n            {\n                // Trait\n                self.write_str(get_local_name(self, symbol, parent, parent_key).as_str())?;\n                self.write_str(\"::\")?;\n            } else if let DefKind::Variant = kind\n                && let Some(parent) = self.tcx().opt_parent(def_id)\n                && let parent_key = self.tcx().def_key(parent)\n                && let Some(symbol) = parent_key.get_opt_name()\n            {\n                // Enum\n\n                // For associated items and variants, we want the \"full\" path, namely, include\n                // the parent type in the path. For example, `Iterator::Item`.\n                self.write_str(get_local_name(self, symbol, parent, parent_key).as_str())?;\n                self.write_str(\"::\")?;\n            } else if let DefKind::Struct\n            | DefKind::Union\n            | DefKind::Enum\n            | DefKind::Trait\n            | DefKind::TyAlias\n            | DefKind::Fn\n            | DefKind::Const\n            | DefKind::Static { .. } = kind\n            {\n            } else {\n                // If not covered above, like for example items out of `impl` blocks, fallback.\n                return Ok(false);\n            }\n            self.write_str(get_local_name(self, symbol, def_id, key).as_str())?;\n            return Ok(true);\n        }\n        Ok(false)\n    }\n\n    /// Try to see if this path can be trimmed to a unique symbol name.\n    fn try_print_trimmed_def_path(&mut self, def_id: DefId) -> Result<bool, PrintError> {\n        if with_forced_trimmed_paths() && self.force_print_trimmed_def_path(def_id)? {\n            return Ok(true);\n        }\n        if self.tcx().sess.opts.unstable_opts.trim_diagnostic_paths\n            && self.tcx().sess.opts.trimmed_def_paths\n            && !with_no_trimmed_paths()\n            && !with_crate_prefix()\n            && let Some(symbol) = self.tcx().trimmed_def_paths(()).get(&def_id)\n        {\n            write!(self, \"{}\", Ident::with_dummy_span(*symbol))?;\n            Ok(true)\n        } else {\n            Ok(false)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Does the work of `try_print_visible_def_path`, building the\n    /// full definition path recursively before attempting to\n    /// post-process it into the valid and visible version that\n    /// accounts for re-exports.\n    ///\n    /// This method should only be called by itself or\n    /// `try_print_visible_def_path`.\n    ///\n    /// `callers` is a chain of visible_parent's leading to `def_id`,\n    /// to support cycle detection during recursion.\n    ///\n    /// This method returns false if we can't print the visible path, so\n    /// `print_def_path` can fall back on the item's real definition path.\n    fn try_print_visible_def_path_recur(\n        &mut self,\n        def_id: DefId,\n        callers: &mut Vec<DefId>,\n    ) -> Result<bool, PrintError> {\n        debug!(\"try_print_visible_def_path: def_id={:?}\", def_id);\n\n        // If `def_id` is a direct or injected extern crate, return the\n        // path to the crate followed by the path to the item within the crate.\n        if let Some(cnum) = def_id.as_crate_root() {\n            if cnum == LOCAL_CRATE {\n                self.print_crate_name(cnum)?;\n                return Ok(true);\n            }\n\n            // In local mode, when we encounter a crate other than\n            // LOCAL_CRATE, execution proceeds in one of two ways:\n            //\n            // 1. For a direct dependency, where user added an\n            //    `extern crate` manually, we put the `extern\n            //    crate` as the parent. So you wind up with\n            //    something relative to the current crate.\n            // 2. For an extern inferred from a path or an indirect crate,\n            //    where there is no explicit `extern crate`, we just prepend\n            //    the crate name.\n            match self.tcx().extern_crate(cnum) {\n                Some(&ExternCrate { src, dependency_of, span, .. }) => match (src, dependency_of) {\n                    (ExternCrateSource::Extern(def_id), LOCAL_CRATE) => {\n                        // NOTE(eddyb) the only reason `span` might be dummy,\n                        // that we're aware of, is that it's the `std`/`core`\n                        // `extern crate` injected by default.\n                        // FIXME(eddyb) find something better to key this on,\n                        // or avoid ending up with `ExternCrateSource::Extern`,\n                        // for the injected `std`/`core`.\n                        if span.is_dummy() {\n                            self.print_crate_name(cnum)?;\n                            return Ok(true);\n                        }\n\n                        // Disable `try_print_trimmed_def_path` behavior within\n                        // the `print_def_path` call, to avoid infinite recursion\n                        // in cases where the `extern crate foo` has non-trivial\n                        // parents, e.g. it's nested in `impl foo::Trait for Bar`\n                        // (see also issues #55779 and #87932).\n                        with_no_visible_paths!(self.print_def_path(def_id, &[])?);\n\n                        return Ok(true);\n                    }\n                    (ExternCrateSource::Path, LOCAL_CRATE) => {\n                        self.print_crate_name(cnum)?;\n                        return Ok(true);\n                    }\n                    _ => {}\n                },\n                None => {\n                    self.print_crate_name(cnum)?;\n                    return Ok(true);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        if def_id.is_local() {\n            return Ok(false);\n        }\n\n        let visible_parent_map = self.tcx().visible_parent_map(());\n\n        let mut cur_def_key = self.tcx().def_key(def_id);\n        debug!(\"try_print_visible_def_path: cur_def_key={:?}\", cur_def_key);\n\n        // For a constructor, we want the name of its parent rather than <unnamed>.\n        if let DefPathData::Ctor = cur_def_key.disambiguated_data.data {\n            let parent = DefId {\n                krate: def_id.krate,\n                index: cur_def_key\n                    .parent\n                    .expect(\"`DefPathData::Ctor` / `VariantData` missing a parent\"),\n            };\n\n            cur_def_key = self.tcx().def_key(parent);\n        }\n\n        let Some(visible_parent) = visible_parent_map.get(&def_id).cloned() else {\n            return Ok(false);\n        };\n\n        if self.tcx().is_doc_hidden(visible_parent) && with_no_visible_paths_if_doc_hidden() {\n            return Ok(false);\n        }\n\n        let actual_parent = self.tcx().opt_parent(def_id);\n        debug!(\n            \"try_print_visible_def_path: visible_parent={:?} actual_parent={:?}\",\n            visible_parent, actual_parent,\n        );\n\n        let mut data = cur_def_key.disambiguated_data.data;\n        debug!(\n            \"try_print_visible_def_path: data={:?} visible_parent={:?} actual_parent={:?}\",\n            data, visible_parent, actual_parent,\n        );\n\n        match data {\n            // In order to output a path that could actually be imported (valid and visible),\n            // we need to handle re-exports correctly.\n            //\n            // For example, take `std::os::unix::process::CommandExt`, this trait is actually\n            // defined at `std::sys::unix::ext::process::CommandExt` (at time of writing).\n            //\n            // `std::os::unix` reexports the contents of `std::sys::unix::ext`. `std::sys` is\n            // private so the \"true\" path to `CommandExt` isn't accessible.\n            //\n            // In this case, the `visible_parent_map` will look something like this:\n            //\n            // (child) -> (parent)\n            // `std::sys::unix::ext::process::CommandExt` -> `std::sys::unix::ext::process`\n            // `std::sys::unix::ext::process` -> `std::sys::unix::ext`\n            // `std::sys::unix::ext` -> `std::os`\n            //\n            // This is correct, as the visible parent of `std::sys::unix::ext` is in fact\n            // `std::os`.\n            //\n            // When printing the path to `CommandExt` and looking at the `cur_def_key` that\n            // corresponds to `std::sys::unix::ext`, we would normally print `ext` and then go\n            // to the parent - resulting in a mangled path like\n            // `std::os::ext::process::CommandExt`.\n            //\n            // Instead, we must detect that there was a re-export and instead print `unix`\n            // (which is the name `std::sys::unix::ext` was re-exported as in `std::os`). To\n            // do this, we compare the parent of `std::sys::unix::ext` (`std::sys::unix`) with\n            // the visible parent (`std::os`). If these do not match, then we iterate over\n            // the children of the visible parent (as was done when computing\n            // `visible_parent_map`), looking for the specific child we currently have and then\n            // have access to the re-exported name.\n            DefPathData::TypeNs(ref mut name) if Some(visible_parent) != actual_parent => {\n                // Item might be re-exported several times, but filter for the one\n                // that's public and whose identifier isn't `_`.\n                let reexport = self\n                    .tcx()\n                    // FIXME(typed_def_id): Further propagate ModDefId\n                    .module_children(ModDefId::new_unchecked(visible_parent))\n                    .iter()\n                    .filter(|child| child.res.opt_def_id() == Some(def_id))\n                    .find(|child| child.vis.is_public() && child.ident.name != kw::Underscore)\n                    .map(|child| child.ident.name);\n\n                if let Some(new_name) = reexport {\n                    *name = new_name;\n                } else {\n                    // There is no name that is public and isn't `_`, so bail.\n                    return Ok(false);\n                }\n            }\n            // Re-exported `extern crate` (#43189).\n            DefPathData::CrateRoot => {\n                data = DefPathData::TypeNs(self.tcx().crate_name(def_id.krate));\n            }\n            _ => {}\n        }\n        debug!(\"try_print_visible_def_path: data={:?}\", data);\n\n        if callers.contains(&visible_parent) {\n            return Ok(false);\n        }\n        callers.push(visible_parent);\n        // HACK(eddyb) this bypasses `print_path_with_simple`'s prefix printing to avoid\n        // knowing ahead of time whether the entire path will succeed or not.\n        // To support printers that do not implement `PrettyPrinter`, a `Vec` or\n        // linked list on the stack would need to be built, before any printing.\n        match self.try_print_visible_def_path_recur(visible_parent, callers)? {\n            false => return Ok(false),\n            true => {}\n        }\n        callers.pop();\n        self.print_path_with_simple(\n            |_| Ok(()),\n            &DisambiguatedDefPathData { data, disambiguator: 0 },\n        )?;\n        Ok(true)\n    }\n\n    fn pretty_print_path_with_qualified(\n        &mut self,\n        self_ty: Ty<'tcx>,\n        trait_ref: Option<ty::TraitRef<'tcx>>,\n    ) -> Result<(), PrintError> {\n        if trait_ref.is_none() {\n            // Inherent impls. Try to print `Foo::bar` for an inherent\n            // impl on `Foo`, but fallback to `<Foo>::bar` if self-type is\n            // anything other than a simple path.\n            match self_ty.kind() {\n                ty::Adt(..)\n                | ty::Foreign(_)\n                | ty::Bool\n                | ty::Char\n                | ty::Str\n                | ty::Int(_)\n                | ty::Uint(_)\n                | ty::Float(_) => {\n                    return self_ty.print(self);\n                }\n\n                _ => {}\n            }\n        }\n\n        self.generic_delimiters(|p| {\n            self_ty.print(p)?;\n            if let Some(trait_ref) = trait_ref {\n                write!(p, \" as \")?;\n                trait_ref.print_only_trait_path().print(p)?;\n            }\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn pretty_print_path_with_impl(\n        &mut self,\n        print_prefix: impl FnOnce(&mut Self) -> Result<(), PrintError>,\n        self_ty: Ty<'tcx>,\n        trait_ref: Option<ty::TraitRef<'tcx>>,\n    ) -> Result<(), PrintError> {\n        print_prefix(self)?;\n\n        self.generic_delimiters(|p| {\n            write!(p, \"impl \")?;\n            if let Some(trait_ref) = trait_ref {\n                trait_ref.print_only_trait_path().print(p)?;\n                write!(p, \" for \")?;\n            }\n            self_ty.print(p)?;\n\n            Ok(())\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn pretty_print_type(&mut self, ty: Ty<'tcx>) -> Result<(), PrintError> {\n        match *ty.kind() {\n            ty::Bool => write!(self, \"bool\")?,\n            ty::Char => write!(self, \"char\")?,\n            ty::Int(t) => write!(self, \"{}\", t.name_str())?,\n            ty::Uint(t) => write!(self, \"{}\", t.name_str())?,\n            ty::Float(t) => write!(self, \"{}\", t.name_str())?,\n            ty::Pat(ty, pat) => {\n                write!(self, \"(\")?;\n                ty.print(self)?;\n                write!(self, \") is {pat:?}\")?;\n            }\n            ty::RawPtr(ty, mutbl) => {\n                write!(self, \"*{} \", mutbl.ptr_str())?;\n                ty.print(self)?;\n            }\n            ty::Ref(r, ty, mutbl) => {\n                write!(self, \"&\")?;\n                if self.should_print_optional_region(r) {\n                    r.print(self)?;\n                    write!(self, \" \")?;\n                }\n                ty::TypeAndMut { ty, mutbl }.print(self)?;\n            }\n            ty::Never => write!(self, \"!\")?,\n            ty::Tuple(tys) => {\n                write!(self, \"(\")?;\n                self.comma_sep(tys.iter())?;\n                if tys.len() == 1 {\n                    write!(self, \",\")?;\n                }\n                write!(self, \")\")?;\n            }\n            ty::FnDef(def_id, args) => {\n                if with_reduced_queries() {\n                    self.print_def_path(def_id, args)?;\n                } else {\n                    let mut sig = self.tcx().fn_sig(def_id).instantiate(self.tcx(), args);\n                    if
