num_available))\n            }\n        }\n\n        debug!(\"name_all_regions\");\n\n        // Replace any anonymous late-bound regions with named\n        // variants, using new unique identifiers, so that we can\n        // clearly differentiate between named and unnamed regions in\n        // the output. We'll probably want to tweak this over time to\n        // decide just how much information to give.\n        if self.binder_depth == 0 {\n            self.prepare_region_info(value);\n        }\n\n        debug!(\"self.used_region_names: {:?}\", self.used_region_names);\n\n        let mut empty = true;\n        let mut start_or_continue = |p: &mut Self, start: &str, cont: &str| {\n            let w = if empty {\n                empty = false;\n                start\n            } else {\n                cont\n            };\n            let _ = write!(p, \"{w}\");\n        };\n        let do_continue = |p: &mut Self, cont: Symbol| {\n            let _ = write!(p, \"{cont}\");\n        };\n\n        let possible_names = ('a'..='z').rev().map(|s| Symbol::intern(&format!(\"'{s}\")));\n\n        let mut available_names = possible_names\n            .filter(|name| !self.used_region_names.contains(name))\n            .collect::<Vec<_>>();\n        debug!(?available_names);\n        let num_available = available_names.len();\n\n        let mut region_index = self.region_index;\n        let mut next_name = |this: &Self| {\n            let mut name;\n\n            loop {\n                name = name_by_region_index(region_index, &mut available_names, num_available);\n                region_index += 1;\n\n                if !this.used_region_names.contains(&name) {\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n\n            name\n        };\n\n        // If we want to print verbosely, then print *all* binders, even if they\n        // aren't named. Eventually, we might just want this as the default, but\n        // this is not *quite* right and changes the ordering of some output\n        // anyways.\n        let (new_value, map) = if self.should_print_verbose() {\n            for var in value.bound_vars().iter() {\n                start_or_continue(self, mode.start_str(), \", \");\n                write!(self, \"{var:?}\")?;\n            }\n            // Unconditionally render `unsafe<>`.\n            if value.bound_vars().is_empty() && mode == WrapBinderMode::Unsafe {\n                start_or_continue(self, mode.start_str(), \"\");\n            }\n            start_or_continue(self, \"\", \"> \");\n            (value.clone().skip_binder(), UnordMap::default())\n        } else {\n            let tcx = self.tcx;\n\n            let trim_path = with_forced_trimmed_paths();\n            // Closure used in `RegionFolder` to create names for anonymous late-bound\n            // regions. We use two `DebruijnIndex`es (one for the currently folded\n            // late-bound region and the other for the binder level) to determine\n            // whether a name has already been created for the currently folded region,\n            // see issue #102392.\n            let mut name = |lifetime_idx: Option<ty::DebruijnIndex>,\n                            binder_level_idx: ty::DebruijnIndex,\n                            br: ty::BoundRegion| {\n                let (name, kind) = if let Some(name) = br.kind.get_name(tcx) {\n                    (name, br.kind)\n                } else {\n                    let name = next_name(self);\n                    (name, ty::BoundRegionKind::NamedAnon(name))\n                };\n\n                if let Some(lt_idx) = lifetime_idx {\n                    if lt_idx > binder_level_idx {\n                        return ty::Region::new_bound(\n                            tcx,\n                            ty::INNERMOST,\n                            ty::BoundRegion { var: br.var, kind },\n                        );\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // Unconditionally render `unsafe<>`.\n                if !trim_path || mode == WrapBinderMode::Unsafe {\n                    start_or_continue(self, mode.start_str(), \", \");\n                    do_continue(self, name);\n                }\n                ty::Region::new_bound(tcx, ty::INNERMOST, ty::BoundRegion { var: br.var, kind })\n            };\n            let mut folder = RegionFolder {\n                tcx,\n                current_index: ty::INNERMOST,\n                name: &mut name,\n                region_map: UnordMap::default(),\n            };\n            let new_value = value.clone().skip_binder().fold_with(&mut folder);\n            let region_map = folder.region_map;\n\n            if mode == WrapBinderMode::Unsafe && region_map.is_empty() {\n                start_or_continue(self, mode.start_str(), \"\");\n            }\n            start_or_continue(self, \"\", \"> \");\n\n            (new_value, region_map)\n        };\n\n        self.binder_depth += 1;\n        self.region_index = region_index;\n        Ok((new_value, map))\n    }\n\n    fn prepare_region_info<T>(&mut self, value: &ty::Binder<'tcx, T>)\n    where\n        T: TypeFoldable<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n    {\n        struct RegionNameCollector<'tcx> {\n            tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,\n            used_region_names: FxHashSet<Symbol>,\n            type_collector: SsoHashSet<Ty<'tcx>>,\n        }\n\n        impl<'tcx> RegionNameCollector<'tcx> {\n            fn new(tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>) -> Self {\n                RegionNameCollector {\n                    tcx,\n                    used_region_names: Default::default(),\n                    type_collector: SsoHashSet::new(),\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        impl<'tcx> ty::TypeVisitor<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for RegionNameCollector<'tcx> {\n            fn visit_region(&mut self, r: ty::Region<'tcx>) {\n                trace!(\"address: {:p}\", r.0.0);\n\n                // Collect all named lifetimes. These allow us to prevent duplication\n                // of already existing lifetime names when introducing names for\n                // anonymous late-bound regions.\n                if let Some(name) = r.get_name(self.tcx) {\n                    self.used_region_names.insert(name);\n                }\n            }\n\n            // We collect types in order to prevent really large types from compiling for\n            // a really long time. See issue #83150 for why this is necessary.\n            fn visit_ty(&mut self, ty: Ty<'tcx>) {\n                let not_previously_inserted = self.type_collector.insert(ty);\n                if not_previously_inserted {\n                    ty.super_visit_with(self)\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        let mut collector = RegionNameCollector::new(self.tcx());\n        value.visit_with(&mut collector);\n        self.used_region_names = collector.used_region_names;\n        self.region_index = 0;\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx, T, P: PrettyPrinter<'tcx>> Print<'tcx, P> for ty::Binder<'tcx, T>\nwhere\n    T: Print<'tcx, P> + TypeFoldable<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n{\n    fn print(&self, p: &mut P) -> Result<(), PrintError> {\n        p.pretty_print_in_binder(self)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx, T, P: PrettyPrinter<'tcx>> Print<'tcx, P> for ty::OutlivesPredicate<'tcx, T>\nwhere\n    T: Print<'tcx, P>,\n{\n    fn print(&self, p: &mut P) -> Result<(), PrintError> {\n        self.0.print(p)?;\n        write!(p, \": \")?;\n        self.1.print(p)?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n/// Wrapper type for `ty::TraitRef` which opts-in to pretty printing only\n/// the trait path. That is, it will print `Trait<U>` instead of\n/// `<T as Trait<U>>`.\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, TypeFoldable, TypeVisitable, Lift, Hash)]\npub struct TraitRefPrintOnlyTraitPath<'tcx>(ty::TraitRef<'tcx>);\n\nimpl<'tcx> rustc_errors::IntoDiagArg for TraitRefPrintOnlyTraitPath<'tcx> {\n    fn into_diag_arg(self, path: &mut Option<std::path::PathBuf>) -> rustc_errors::DiagArgValue {\n        ty::tls::with(|tcx| {\n            let trait_ref = tcx.short_string(self, path);\n            rustc_errors::DiagArgValue::Str(std::borrow::Cow::Owned(trait_ref))\n        })\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> fmt::Debug for TraitRefPrintOnlyTraitPath<'tcx> {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        fmt::Display::fmt(self, f)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Wrapper type for `ty::TraitRef` which opts-in to pretty printing only\n/// the trait path, and additionally tries to \"sugar\" `Fn(...)` trait bounds.\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, TypeFoldable, TypeVisitable, Lift, Hash)]\npub struct TraitRefPrintSugared<'tcx>(ty::TraitRef<'tcx>);\n\nimpl<'tcx> rustc_errors::IntoDiagArg for TraitRefPrintSugared<'tcx> {\n    fn into_diag_arg(self, path: &mut Option<std::path::PathBuf>) -> rustc_errors::DiagArgValue {\n        ty::tls::with(|tcx| {\n            let trait_ref = tcx.short_string(self, path);\n            rustc_errors::DiagArgValue::Str(std::borrow::Cow::Owned(trait_ref))\n        })\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> fmt::Debug for TraitRefPrintSugared<'tcx> {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        fmt::Display::fmt(self, f)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Wrapper type for `ty::TraitRef` which opts-in to pretty printing only\n/// the trait name. That is, it will print `Trait` instead of\n/// `<T as Trait<U>>`.\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, TypeFoldable, TypeVisitable, Lift)]\npub struct TraitRefPrintOnlyTraitName<'tcx>(ty::TraitRef<'tcx>);\n\nimpl<'tcx> fmt::Debug for TraitRefPrintOnlyTraitName<'tcx> {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        fmt::Display::fmt(self, f)\n    }\n}\n\n#[extension(pub trait PrintTraitRefExt<'tcx>)]\nimpl<'tcx> ty::TraitRef<'tcx> {\n    fn print_only_trait_path(self) -> TraitRefPrintOnlyTraitPath<'tcx> {\n        TraitRefPrintOnlyTraitPath(self)\n    }\n\n    fn print_trait_sugared(self) -> TraitRefPrintSugared<'tcx> {\n        TraitRefPrintSugared(self)\n    }\n\n    fn print_only_trait_name(self) -> TraitRefPrintOnlyTraitName<'tcx> {\n        TraitRefPrintOnlyTraitName(self)\n    }\n}\n\n#[extension(pub trait PrintPolyTraitRefExt<'tcx>)]\nimpl<'tcx> ty::Binder<'tcx, ty::TraitRef<'tcx>> {\n    fn print_only_trait_path(self) -> ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitRefPrintOnlyTraitPath<'tcx>> {\n        self.map_bound(|tr| tr.print_only_trait_path())\n    }\n\n    fn print_trait_sugared(self) -> ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitRefPrintSugared<'tcx>> {\n        self.map_bound(|tr| tr.print_trait_sugared())\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, TypeFoldable, TypeVisitable, Lift, Hash)]\npub struct TraitPredPrintModifiersAndPath<'tcx>(ty::TraitPredicate<'tcx>);\n\nimpl<'tcx> fmt::Debug for TraitPredPrintModifiersAndPath<'tcx> {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        fmt::Display::fmt(self, f)\n    }\n}\n\n#[extension(pub trait PrintTraitPredicateExt<'tcx>)]\nimpl<'tcx> ty::TraitPredicate<'tcx> {\n    fn print_modifiers_and_trait_path(self) -> TraitPredPrintModifiersAndPath<'tcx> {\n        TraitPredPrintModifiersAndPath(self)\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, TypeFoldable, TypeVisitable, Lift, Hash)]\npub struct TraitPredPrintWithBoundConstness<'tcx>(\n    ty::TraitPredicate<'tcx>,\n    Option<ty::BoundConstness>,\n);\n\nimpl<'tcx> fmt::Debug for TraitPredPrintWithBoundConstness<'tcx> {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        fmt::Display::fmt(self, f)\n    }\n}\n\n#[extension(pub trait PrintPolyTraitPredicateExt<'tcx>)]\nimpl<'tcx> ty::PolyTraitPredicate<'tcx> {\n    fn print_modifiers_and_trait_path(\n        self,\n    ) -> ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitPredPrintModifiersAndPath<'tcx>> {\n        self.map_bound(TraitPredPrintModifiersAndPath)\n    }\n\n    fn print_with_bound_constness(\n        self,\n        constness: Option<ty::BoundConstness>,\n    ) -> ty::Binder<'tcx, TraitPredPrintWithBoundConstness<'tcx>> {\n        self.map_bound(|trait_pred| TraitPredPrintWithBoundConstness(trait_pred, constness))\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Copy, Clone, Lift)]\npub struct PrintClosureAsImpl<'tcx> {\n    pub closure: ty::ClosureArgs<TyCtxt<'tcx>>,\n}\n\nmacro_rules! forward_display_to_print {\n    ($($ty:ty),+) => {\n        // Some of the $ty arguments may not actually use 'tcx\n        $(#[allow(unused_lifetimes)] impl<'tcx> fmt::Display for $ty {\n            fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n                ty::tls::with(|tcx| {\n                    let mut p = FmtPrinter::new(tcx, Namespace::TypeNS);\n                    tcx.lift(*self)\n                        .expect(\"could not lift for printing\")\n                        .print(&mut p)?;\n                    f.write_str(&p.into_buffer())?;\n                    Ok(())\n                })\n            }\n        })+\n    };\n}\n\nmacro_rules! define_print {\n    (($self:ident, $p:ident): $($ty:ty $print:block)+) => {\n        $(impl<'tcx, P: PrettyPrinter<'tcx>> Print<'tcx, P> for $ty {\n            fn print(&$self, $p: &mut P) -> Result<(), PrintError> {\n                let _: () = $print;\n                Ok(())\n            }\n        })+\n    };\n}\n\nmacro_rules! define_print_and_forward_display {\n    (($self:ident, $p:ident): $($ty:ty $print:block)+) => {\n        define_print!(($self, $p): $($ty $print)*);\n        forward_display_to_print!($($ty),+);\n    };\n}\n\nforward_display_to_print! {\n    ty::Region<'tcx>,\n    Ty<'tcx>,\n    &'tcx ty::List<ty::PolyExistentialPredicate<'tcx>>,\n    ty::Const<'tcx>\n}\n\ndefine_print! {\n    (self, p):\n\n    ty::FnSig<'tcx> {\n        write!(p, \"{}\", self.safety.prefix_str())?;\n\n        if self.abi != ExternAbi::Rust {\n            write!(p, \"extern {} \", self.abi)?;\n        }\n\n        write!(p, \"fn\")?;\n        p.pretty_print_fn_sig(self.inputs(), self.c_variadic, self.output())?;\n    }\n\n    ty::TraitRef<'tcx> {\n        write!(p, \"<{} as {}>\", self.self_ty(), self.print_only_trait_path())?;\n    }\n\n    ty::AliasTy<'tcx> {\n        let alias_term: ty::AliasTerm<'tcx> = (*self).into();\n        alias_term.print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::AliasTerm<'tcx> {\n        match self.kind(p.tcx()) {\n            ty::AliasTermKind::InherentTy | ty::AliasTermKind::InherentConst => p.pretty_print_inherent_projection(*self)?,\n            ty::AliasTermKind::ProjectionTy => {\n                if !(p.should_print_verbose() || with_reduced_queries())\n                    && p.tcx().is_impl_trait_in_trait(self.def_id)\n                {\n                    p.pretty_print_rpitit(self.def_id, self.args)?;\n                } else {\n                    p.print_def_path(self.def_id, self.args)?;\n                }\n            }\n            ty::AliasTermKind::FreeTy\n            | ty::AliasTermKind::FreeConst\n            | ty::AliasTermKind::OpaqueTy\n            | ty::AliasTermKind::UnevaluatedConst\n            | ty::AliasTermKind::ProjectionConst => {\n                p.print_def_path(self.def_id, self.args)?;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    ty::TraitPredicate<'tcx> {\n        self.trait_ref.self_ty().print(p)?;\n        write!(p, \": \")?;\n        if let ty::PredicatePolarity::Negative = self.polarity {\n            write!(p, \"!\")?;\n        }\n        self.trait_ref.print_trait_sugared().print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::HostEffectPredicate<'tcx> {\n        let constness = match self.constness {\n            ty::BoundConstness::Const => { \"const\" }\n            ty::BoundConstness::Maybe => { \"[const]\" }\n        };\n        self.trait_ref.self_ty().print(p)?;\n        write!(p, \": {constness} \")?;\n        self.trait_ref.print_trait_sugared().print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::TypeAndMut<'tcx> {\n        write!(p, \"{}\", self.mutbl.prefix_str())?;\n        self.ty.print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::ClauseKind<'tcx> {\n        match *self {\n            ty::ClauseKind::Trait(ref data) => data.print(p)?,\n            ty::ClauseKind::RegionOutlives(predicate) => predicate.print(p)?,\n            ty::ClauseKind::TypeOutlives(predicate) => predicate.print(p)?,\n            ty::ClauseKind::Projection(predicate) => predicate.print(p)?,\n            ty::ClauseKind::HostEffect(predicate) => predicate.print(p)?,\n            ty::ClauseKind::ConstArgHasType(ct, ty) => {\n                write!(p, \"the constant `\")?;\n                ct.print(p)?;\n                write!(p, \"` has type `\")?;\n                ty.print(p)?;\n                write!(p, \"`\")?;\n            },\n            ty::ClauseKind::WellFormed(term) => {\n                term.print(p)?;\n                write!(p, \" well-formed\")?;\n            }\n            ty::ClauseKind::ConstEvaluatable(ct) => {\n                write!(p, \"the constant `\")?;\n                ct.print(p)?;\n                write!(p, \"` can be evaluated\")?;\n            }\n            ty::ClauseKind::UnstableFeature(symbol) => {\n                write!(p, \"feature({symbol}) is enabled\")?;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    ty::PredicateKind<'tcx> {\n        match *self {\n            ty::PredicateKind::Clause(data) => data.print(p)?,\n            ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(predicate) => predicate.print(p)?,\n            ty::PredicateKind::Coerce(predicate) => predicate.print(p)?,\n            ty::PredicateKind::DynCompatible(trait_def_id) => {\n                write!(p, \"the trait `\")?;\n                p.print_def_path(trait_def_id, &[])?;\n                write!(p, \"` is dyn-compatible\")?;\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::ConstEquate(c1, c2) => {\n                write!(p, \"the constant `\")?;\n                c1.print(p)?;\n                write!(p, \"` equals `\")?;\n                c2.print(p)?;\n                write!(p, \"`\")?;\n            }\n            ty::PredicateKind::Ambiguous => write!(p, \"ambiguous\")?,\n            ty::PredicateKind::NormalizesTo(data) => data.print(p)?,\n            ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(t1, t2, dir) => {\n                t1.print(p)?;\n                write!(p, \" {dir} \")?;\n                t2.print(p)?;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    ty::ExistentialPredicate<'tcx> {\n        match *self {\n            ty::ExistentialPredicate::Trait(x) => x.print(p)?,\n            ty::ExistentialPredicate::Projection(x) => x.print(p)?,\n            ty::ExistentialPredicate::AutoTrait(def_id) => p.print_def_path(def_id, &[])?,\n        }\n    }\n\n    ty::ExistentialTraitRef<'tcx> {\n        // Use a type that can't appear in defaults of type parameters.\n        let dummy_self = Ty::new_fresh(p.tcx(), 0);\n        let trait_ref = self.with_self_ty(p.tcx(), dummy_self);\n        trait_ref.print_only_trait_path().print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::ExistentialProjection<'tcx> {\n        let name = p.tcx().associated_item(self.def_id).name();\n        // The args don't contain the self ty (as it has been erased) but the corresp.\n        // generics do as the trait always has a self ty param. We need to offset.\n        let args = &self.args[p.tcx().generics_of(self.def_id).parent_count - 1..];\n        p.print_path_with_generic_args(|p| write!(p, \"{name}\"), args)?;\n        write!(p, \" = \")?;\n        self.term.print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::ProjectionPredicate<'tcx> {\n        self.projection_term.print(p)?;\n        write!(p, \" == \")?;\n        p.reset_type_limit();\n        self.term.print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::SubtypeRelation<'tcx> {\n        self.a.print(p)?;\n        write!(p, \" <: \")?;\n        p.reset_type_limit();\n        self.b.print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::CoercePredicate<'tcx> {\n        self.a.print(p)?;\n        write!(p, \" -> \")?;\n        p.reset_type_limit();\n        self.b.print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::NormalizesTo<'tcx> {\n        self.alias.print(p)?;\n        write!(p, \" normalizes-to \")?;\n        p.reset_type_limit();\n        self.term.print(p)?;\n    }\n}\n\ndefine_print_and_forward_display! {\n    (self, p):\n\n    &'tcx ty::List<Ty<'tcx>> {\n        write!(p, \"{{\")?;\n        p.comma_sep(self.iter())?;\n        write!(p, \"}}\")?;\n    }\n\n    TraitRefPrintOnlyTraitPath<'tcx> {\n        p.print_def_path(self.0.def_id, self.0.args)?;\n    }\n\n    TraitRefPrintSugared<'tcx> {\n        if !with_reduced_queries()\n            && p.tcx().trait_def(self.0.def_id).paren_sugar\n            && let ty::Tuple(args) = self.0.args.type_at(1).kind()\n        {\n            write!(p, \"{}(\", p.tcx().item_name(self.0.def_id))?;\n            for (i, arg) in args.iter().enumerate() {\n                if i > 0 {\n                    write!(p, \", \")?;\n                }\n                arg.print(p)?;\n            }\n            write!(p, \")\")?;\n        } else {\n            p.print_def_path(self.0.def_id, self.0.args)?;\n        }\n    }\n\n    TraitRefPrintOnlyTraitName<'tcx> {\n        p.print_def_path(self.0.def_id, &[])?;\n    }\n\n    TraitPredPrintModifiersAndPath<'tcx> {\n        if let ty::PredicatePolarity::Negative = self.0.polarity {\n            write!(p, \"!\")?;\n        }\n        self.0.trait_ref.print_trait_sugared().print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    TraitPredPrintWithBoundConstness<'tcx> {\n        self.0.trait_ref.self_ty().print(p)?;\n        write!(p, \": \")?;\n        if let Some(constness) = self.1 {\n            p.pretty_print_bound_constness(constness)?;\n        }\n        if let ty::PredicatePolarity::Negative = self.0.polarity {\n            write!(p, \"!\")?;\n        }\n        self.0.trait_ref.print_trait_sugared().print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    PrintClosureAsImpl<'tcx> {\n        p.pretty_print_closure_as_impl(self.closure)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::ParamTy {\n        write!(p, \"{}\", self.name)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::PlaceholderType {\n        match self.bound.kind {\n            ty::BoundTyKind::Anon => write!(p, \"{self:?}\")?,\n            ty::BoundTyKind::Param(def_id) => match p.should_print_verbose() {\n                true => write!(p, \"{self:?}\")?,\n                false => write!(p, \"{}\", p.tcx().item_name(def_id))?,\n            },\n        }\n    }\n\n    ty::ParamConst {\n        write!(p, \"{}\", self.name)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::Term<'tcx> {\n      match self.kind() {\n        ty::TermKind::Ty(ty) => ty.print(p)?,\n        ty::TermKind::Const(c) => c.print(p)?,\n      }\n    }\n\n    ty::Predicate<'tcx> {\n        self.kind().print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    ty::Clause<'tcx> {\n        self.kind().print(p)?;\n    }\n\n    GenericArg<'tcx> {\n        match self.kind() {\n            GenericArgKind::Lifetime(lt) => lt.print(p)?,\n            GenericArgKind::Type(ty) => ty.print(p)?,\n            GenericArgKind::Const(ct) => ct.print(p)?,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nfn for_each_def(tcx: TyCtxt<'_>, mut collect_fn: impl for<'b> FnMut(&'b Ident, Namespace, DefId)) {\n    // Iterate all (non-anonymous) local crate items no matter where they are defined.\n    for id in tcx.hir_free_items() {\n        if matches!(tcx.def_kind(id.owner_id), DefKind::Use) {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        let item = tcx.hir_item(id);\n        let Some(ident) = item.kind.ident() else { continue };\n\n        let def_id = item.owner_id.to_def_id();\n        let ns = tcx.def_kind(def_id).ns().unwrap_or(Namespace::TypeNS);\n        collect_fn(&ident, ns, def_id);\n    }\n\n    // Now take care of extern crate items.\n    let queue = &mut Vec::new();\n    let mut seen_defs: DefIdSet = Default::default();\n\n    for &cnum in tcx.crates(()).iter() {\n        // Ignore crates that are not direct dependencies.\n        match tcx.extern_crate(cnum) {\n            None => continue,\n            Some(extern_crate) => {\n                if !extern_crate.is_direct() {\n                    continue;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        queue.push(cnum.as_def_id());\n    }\n\n    // Iterate external crate defs but be mindful about visibility\n    while let Some(def) = queue.pop() {\n        for child in tcx.module_children(def).iter() {\n            if !child.vis.is_public() {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            match child.res {\n                def::Res::Def(DefKind::AssocTy, _) => {}\n                def::Res::Def(DefKind::TyAlias, _) => {}\n                def::Res::Def(defkind, def_id) => {\n                    if let Some(ns) = defkind.ns() {\n                        collect_fn(&child.ident, ns, def_id);\n                    }\n\n                    if defkind.is_module_like() && seen_defs.insert(def_id) {\n                        queue.push(def_id);\n                    }\n                }\n                _ => {}\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// The purpose of this function is to collect public symbols names that are unique across all\n/// crates in the build. Later, when printing about types we can use those names instead of the\n/// full exported path to them.\n///\n/// So essentially, if a symbol name can only be imported from one place for a type, and as\n/// long as it was not glob-imported anywhere in the current crate, we can trim its printed\n/// path and print only the name.\n///\n/// This has wide implications on error messages with types, for example, shortening\n/// `std::vec::Vec` to just `Vec`, as long as there is no other `Vec` importable anywhere.\n///\n/// The implementation uses similar import discovery logic to that of 'use' suggestions.\n///\n/// See also [`with_no_trimmed_paths!`].\n// this is pub to be able to intra-doc-link it\npub fn trimmed_def_paths(tcx: TyCtxt<'_>, (): ()) -> DefIdMap<Symbol> {\n    // Trimming paths is expensive and not optimized, since we expect it to only be used for error\n    // reporting. Record the fact that we did it, so we can abort if we later found it was\n    // unnecessary.\n    //\n    // The `rustc_middle::ty::print::with_no_trimmed_paths` wrapper can be used to suppress this\n    // checking, in exchange for full paths being formatted.\n    tcx.sess.record_trimmed_def_paths();\n\n    // Once constructed, unique namespace+symbol pairs will have a `Some(_)` entry, while\n    // non-unique pairs will have a `None` entry.\n    let unique_symbols_rev: &mut FxIndexMap<(Namespace, Symbol), Option<DefId>> =\n        &mut FxIndexMap::default();\n\n    for symbol_set in tcx.resolutions(()).glob_map.values() {\n        for symbol in symbol_set {\n            unique_symbols_rev.insert((Namespace::TypeNS, *symbol), None);\n            unique_symbols_rev.insert((Namespace::ValueNS, *symbol), None);\n            unique_symbols_rev.insert((Namespace::MacroNS, *symbol), None);\n        }\n    }\n\n    for_each_def(tcx, |ident, ns, def_id| match unique_symbols_rev.entry((ns, ident.name)) {\n        IndexEntry::Occupied(mut v) => match v.get() {\n            None => {}\n            Some(existing) => {\n                if *existing != def_id {\n                    v.insert(None);\n                }\n            }\n        },\n        IndexEntry::Vacant(v) => {\n            v.insert(Some(def_id));\n        }\n    });\n\n    // Put the symbol from all the unique namespace+symbol pairs into `map`.\n    let mut map: DefIdMap<Symbol> = Default::default();\n    for ((_, symbol), opt_def_id) in unique_symbols_rev.drain(..) {\n        use std::collections::hash_map::Entry::{Occupied, Vacant};\n\n        if let Some(def_id) = opt_def_id {\n            match map.entry(def_id) {\n                Occupied(mut v) => {\n                    // A single DefId can be known under multiple names (e.g.,\n                    // with a `pub use ... as ...;`). We need to ensure that the\n                    // name placed in this map is chosen deterministically, so\n                    // if we find multiple names (`symbol`) resolving to the\n                    // same `def_id`, we prefer the lexicographically smallest\n                    // name.\n                    //\n                    // Any stable ordering would be fine here though.\n                    if *v.get() != symbol && v.get().as_str() > symbol.as_str() {\n                        v.insert(symbol);\n                    }\n                }\n                Vacant(v) => {\n                    v.insert(symbol);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    map\n}\n\npub fn provide(providers: &mut Providers) {\n    *providers = Providers { trimmed_def_paths, ..*providers };\n}\n\npub struct OpaqueFnEntry<'tcx> {\n    kind: ty::ClosureKind,\n    return_ty: Option<ty::Binder<'tcx, Term<'tcx>>>,\n}\n",
  "replaceAll": false,
  "structuredPatch": [

        "         p.reset_type_limit();",
        "-        self.b.print(p)?;",
        "+        self.super_ty.print(p)?;",

        "     ty::CoercePredicate<'tcx> {"

  "originalFile": "use tracing::{debug, instrument};\n\nuse self::combine::{PredicateEmittingRelation, super_combine_consts, super_combine_tys};\nuse crate::data_structures::DelayedSet;\npub use crate::relate::*;\nuse crate::solve::Goal;\nuse crate::{self as ty, InferCtxtLike, Interner};\n\npub trait RelateExt: InferCtxtLike {\n    fn relate<T: Relate<Self::Interner>>(\n        &self,\n        param_env: <Self::Interner as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        lhs: T,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        rhs: T,\n        span: <Self::Interner as Interner>::Span,\n    ) -> Result<\n        Vec<Goal<Self::Interner, <Self::Interner as Interner>::Predicate>>,\n        TypeError<Self::Interner>,\n    >;\n\n    fn eq_structurally_relating_aliases<T: Relate<Self::Interner>>(\n        &self,\n        param_env: <Self::Interner as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        lhs: T,\n        rhs: T,\n        span: <Self::Interner as Interner>::Span,\n    ) -> Result<\n        Vec<Goal<Self::Interner, <Self::Interner as Interner>::Predicate>>,\n        TypeError<Self::Interner>,\n    >;\n}\n\nimpl<Infcx: InferCtxtLike> RelateExt for Infcx {\n    fn relate<T: Relate<Self::Interner>>(\n        &self,\n        param_env: <Self::Interner as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        lhs: T,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        rhs: T,\n        span: <Self::Interner as Interner>::Span,\n    ) -> Result<\n        Vec<Goal<Self::Interner, <Self::Interner as Interner>::Predicate>>,\n        TypeError<Self::Interner>,\n    > {\n        let mut relate =\n            SolverRelating::new(self, StructurallyRelateAliases::No, variance, param_env, span);\n        relate.relate(lhs, rhs)?;\n        Ok(relate.goals)\n    }\n\n    fn eq_structurally_relating_aliases<T: Relate<Self::Interner>>(\n        &self,\n        param_env: <Self::Interner as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        lhs: T,\n        rhs: T,\n        span: <Self::Interner as Interner>::Span,\n    ) -> Result<\n        Vec<Goal<Self::Interner, <Self::Interner as Interner>::Predicate>>,\n        TypeError<Self::Interner>,\n    > {\n        let mut relate = SolverRelating::new(\n            self,\n            StructurallyRelateAliases::Yes,\n            ty::Invariant,\n            param_env,\n            span,\n        );\n        relate.relate(lhs, rhs)?;\n        Ok(relate.goals)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Enforce that `a` is equal to or a subtype of `b`.\npub struct SolverRelating<'infcx, Infcx, I: Interner> {\n    infcx: &'infcx Infcx,\n    // Immutable fields.\n    structurally_relate_aliases: StructurallyRelateAliases,\n    param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n    span: I::Span,\n    // Mutable fields.\n    ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    goals: Vec<Goal<I, I::Predicate>>,\n    /// The cache only tracks the `ambient_variance` as it's the\n    /// only field which is mutable and which meaningfully changes\n    /// the result when relating types.\n    ///\n    /// The cache does not track whether the state of the\n    /// `Infcx` has been changed or whether we've added any\n    /// goals to `self.goals`. Whether a goal is added once or multiple\n    /// times is not really meaningful.\n    ///\n    /// Changes in the inference state may delay some type inference to\n    /// the next fulfillment loop. Given that this loop is already\n    /// necessary, this is also not a meaningful change. Consider\n    /// the following three relations:\n    /// ```text\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ?0 eq u32\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ```\n    /// Without a cache, the second `Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>` would eagerly\n    /// constrain `?1` to `u32`. When using the cache entry from the\n    /// first time we've related these types, this only happens when\n    /// later proving the `Subtype(?0, ?1)` goal from the first relation.\n    cache: DelayedSet<(ty::Variance, I::Ty, I::Ty)>,\n}\n\nimpl<'infcx, Infcx, I> SolverRelating<'infcx, Infcx, I>\nwhere\n    Infcx: InferCtxtLike<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    pub fn new(\n        infcx: &'infcx Infcx,\n        structurally_relate_aliases: StructurallyRelateAliases,\n        ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n        param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n        span: I::Span,\n    ) -> Self {\n        SolverRelating {\n            infcx,\n            structurally_relate_aliases,\n            span,\n            ambient_variance,\n            param_env,\n            goals: vec![],\n            cache: Default::default(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<Infcx, I> TypeRelation<I> for SolverRelating<'_, Infcx, I>\nwhere\n    Infcx: InferCtxtLike<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    fn cx(&self) -> I {\n        self.infcx.cx()\n    }\n\n    fn relate_item_args(\n        &mut self,\n        item_def_id: I::DefId,\n        a_arg: I::GenericArgs,\n        b_arg: I::GenericArgs,\n    ) -> RelateResult<I, I::GenericArgs> {\n        if self.ambient_variance == ty::Invariant {\n            // Avoid fetching the variance if we are in an invariant\n            // context; no need, and it can induce dependency cycles\n            // (e.g., #41849).\n            relate_args_invariantly(self, a_arg, b_arg)\n        } else {\n            let tcx = self.cx();\n            let opt_variances = tcx.variances_of(item_def_id);\n            relate_args_with_variances(self, item_def_id, opt_variances, a_arg, b_arg, false)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn relate_with_variance<T: Relate<I>>(\n        &mut self,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        _info: VarianceDiagInfo<I>,\n        a: T,\n        b: T,\n    ) -> RelateResult<I, T> {\n        let old_ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance;\n        self.ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance.xform(variance);\n        debug!(?self.ambient_variance, \"new ambient variance\");\n\n        let r = if self.ambient_variance == ty::Bivariant { Ok(a) } else { self.relate(a, b) };\n\n        self.ambient_variance = old_ambient_variance;\n        r\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn tys(&mut self, a: I::Ty, b: I::Ty) -> RelateResult<I, I::Ty> {\n        if a == b {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        let infcx = self.infcx;\n        let a = infcx.shallow_resolve(a);\n        let b = infcx.shallow_resolve(b);\n\n        if self.cache.contains(&(self.ambient_variance, a, b)) {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        match (a.kind(), b.kind()) {\n            (ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_id)), ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_id))) => {\n                match self.ambient_variance {\n                    ty::Covariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `A <: B` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.goals.push(Goal::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate {\n                                a_is_expected: true,\n                                a,\n                                b,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Contravariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `B <: A` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.goals.push(Goal::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate {\n                                a_is_expected: false,\n                                a: b,\n                                b: a,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Invariant => {\n                        infcx.equate_ty_vids_raw(a_id, b_id);\n                    }\n                    ty::Bivariant => {\n                        unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            (ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_vid)), _) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var_raw(self, true, a_vid, self.ambient_variance, b)?;\n            }\n            (_, ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var_raw(\n                    self,\n                    false,\n                    b_vid,\n                    self.ambient_variance.xform(ty::Contravariant),\n                    a,\n                )?;\n            }\n\n            _ => {\n                super_combine_tys(self.infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        assert!(self.cache.insert((self.ambient_variance, a, b)));\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn regions(&mut self, a: I::Region, b: I::Region) -> RelateResult<I, I::Region> {\n        match self.ambient_variance {\n            // Subtype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('a: 'b) => SubRegion('b, 'a)\n            ty::Covariant => self.infcx.sub_regions(b, a, self.span),\n            // Suptype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('b: 'a) => SubRegion('a, 'b)\n            ty::Contravariant => self.infcx.sub_regions(a, b, self.span),\n            ty::Invariant => self.infcx.equate_regions(a, b, self.span),\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn consts(&mut self, a: I::Const, b: I::Const) -> RelateResult<I, I::Const> {\n        super_combine_consts(self.infcx, self, a, b)\n    }\n\n    fn binders<T>(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Binder<I, T>,\n        b: ty::Binder<I, T>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<I, ty::Binder<I, T>>\n    where\n        T: Relate<I>,\n    {\n        // If they're equal, then short-circuit.\n        if a == b {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        // If they have no bound vars, relate normally.\n        if let Some(a_inner) = a.no_bound_vars()\n            && let Some(b_inner) = b.no_bound_vars()\n        {\n            self.relate(a_inner, b_inner)?;\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        match self.ambient_variance {\n            // Checks whether `for<..> sub <: for<..> sup` holds.\n            //\n            // For this to hold, **all** instantiations of the super type\n            // have to be a super type of **at least one** instantiation of\n            // the subtype.\n            //\n            // This is implemented by first entering a new universe.\n            // We then replace all bound variables in `sup` with placeholders,\n            // and all bound variables in `sub` with inference vars.\n            // We can then just relate the two resulting types as normal.\n            //\n            // Note: this is a subtle algorithm. For a full explanation, please see\n            // the [rustc dev guide][rd]\n            //\n            // [rd]: https://rustc-dev-guide.rust-lang.org/borrow_check/region_inference/placeholders_and_universes.html\n            ty::Covariant => {\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                    let a = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(a);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n            }\n            ty::Contravariant => {\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                    let b = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(b);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n            }\n\n            // When **equating** binders, we check that there is a 1-to-1\n            // correspondence between the bound vars in both types.\n            //\n            // We do so by separately instantiating one of the binders with\n            // placeholders and the other with inference variables and then\n            // equating the instantiated types.\n            //\n            // We want `for<..> A == for<..> B` -- therefore we want\n            // `exists<..> A == for<..> B` and `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n            // Check if `exists<..> A == for<..> B`\n            ty::Invariant => {\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                    let a = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(a);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n\n                // Check if `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                    let b = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(b);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n            }\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        }\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<Infcx, I> PredicateEmittingRelation<Infcx> for SolverRelating<'_, Infcx, I>\nwhere\n    Infcx: InferCtxtLike<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    fn span(&self) -> I::Span {\n        Span::dummy()\n    }\n\n    fn param_env(&self) -> I::ParamEnv {\n        self.param_env\n    }\n\n    fn structurally_relate_aliases(&self) -> StructurallyRelateAliases {\n        self.structurally_relate_aliases\n    }\n\n    fn register_predicates(\n        &mut self,\n        obligations: impl IntoIterator<Item: ty::Upcast<I, I::Predicate>>,\n    ) {\n        self.goals.extend(\n            obligations.into_iter().map(|pred| Goal::new(self.infcx.cx(), self.param_env, pred)),\n        );\n    }\n\n    fn register_goals(&mut self, obligations: impl IntoIterator<Item = Goal<I, I::Predicate>>) {\n        self.goals.extend(obligations);\n    }\n\n    fn register_alias_relate_predicate(&mut self, a: I::Ty, b: I::Ty) {\n        self.register_predicates([ty::Binder::dummy(match self.ambient_variance {\n            ty::Covariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            // a :> b is b <: a\n            ty::Contravariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                b.into(),\n                a.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            ty::Invariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Equate,\n            ),\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        })]);\n    }\n}\n",

  "originalFile": "use tracing::{debug, instrument};\n\nuse self::combine::{PredicateEmittingRelation, super_combine_consts, super_combine_tys};\nuse crate::data_structures::DelayedSet;\npub use crate::relate::*;\nuse crate::solve::Goal;\nuse crate::{self as ty, InferCtxtLike, Interner};\n\npub trait RelateExt: InferCtxtLike {\n    fn relate<T: Relate<Self::Interner>>(\n        &self,\n        param_env: <Self::Interner as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        lhs: T,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        rhs: T,\n        span: <Self::Interner as Interner>::Span,\n    ) -> Result<\n        Vec<Goal<Self::Interner, <Self::Interner as Interner>::Predicate>>,\n        TypeError<Self::Interner>,\n    >;\n\n    fn eq_structurally_relating_aliases<T: Relate<Self::Interner>>(\n        &self,\n        param_env: <Self::Interner as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        lhs: T,\n        rhs: T,\n        span: <Self::Interner as Interner>::Span,\n    ) -> Result<\n        Vec<Goal<Self::Interner, <Self::Interner as Interner>::Predicate>>,\n        TypeError<Self::Interner>,\n    >;\n}\n\nimpl<Infcx: InferCtxtLike> RelateExt for Infcx {\n    fn relate<T: Relate<Self::Interner>>(\n        &self,\n        param_env: <Self::Interner as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        lhs: T,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        rhs: T,\n        span: <Self::Interner as Interner>::Span,\n    ) -> Result<\n        Vec<Goal<Self::Interner, <Self::Interner as Interner>::Predicate>>,\n        TypeError<Self::Interner>,\n    > {\n        let mut relate =\n            SolverRelating::new(self, StructurallyRelateAliases::No, variance, param_env, span);\n        relate.relate(lhs, rhs)?;\n        Ok(relate.goals)\n    }\n\n    fn eq_structurally_relating_aliases<T: Relate<Self::Interner>>(\n        &self,\n        param_env: <Self::Interner as Interner>::ParamEnv,\n        lhs: T,\n        rhs: T,\n        span: <Self::Interner as Interner>::Span,\n    ) -> Result<\n        Vec<Goal<Self::Interner, <Self::Interner as Interner>::Predicate>>,\n        TypeError<Self::Interner>,\n    > {\n        let mut relate = SolverRelating::new(\n            self,\n            StructurallyRelateAliases::Yes,\n            ty::Invariant,\n            param_env,\n            span,\n        );\n        relate.relate(lhs, rhs)?;\n        Ok(relate.goals)\n    }\n}\n\n/// Enforce that `a` is equal to or a subtype of `b`.\npub struct SolverRelating<'infcx, Infcx, I: Interner> {\n    infcx: &'infcx Infcx,\n    // Immutable fields.\n    structurally_relate_aliases: StructurallyRelateAliases,\n    param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n    span: I::Span,\n    // Mutable fields.\n    ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n    goals: Vec<Goal<I, I::Predicate>>,\n    /// The cache only tracks the `ambient_variance` as it's the\n    /// only field which is mutable and which meaningfully changes\n    /// the result when relating types.\n    ///\n    /// The cache does not track whether the state of the\n    /// `Infcx` has been changed or whether we've added any\n    /// goals to `self.goals`. Whether a goal is added once or multiple\n    /// times is not really meaningful.\n    ///\n    /// Changes in the inference state may delay some type inference to\n    /// the next fulfillment loop. Given that this loop is already\n    /// necessary, this is also not a meaningful change. Consider\n    /// the following three relations:\n    /// ```text\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ?0 eq u32\n    /// Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>\n    /// ```\n    /// Without a cache, the second `Vec<?0> sub Vec<?1>` would eagerly\n    /// constrain `?1` to `u32`. When using the cache entry from the\n    /// first time we've related these types, this only happens when\n    /// later proving the `Subtype(?0, ?1)` goal from the first relation.\n    cache: DelayedSet<(ty::Variance, I::Ty, I::Ty)>,\n}\n\nimpl<'infcx, Infcx, I> SolverRelating<'infcx, Infcx, I>\nwhere\n    Infcx: InferCtxtLike<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    pub fn new(\n        infcx: &'infcx Infcx,\n        structurally_relate_aliases: StructurallyRelateAliases,\n        ambient_variance: ty::Variance,\n        param_env: I::ParamEnv,\n        span: I::Span,\n    ) -> Self {\n        SolverRelating {\n            infcx,\n            structurally_relate_aliases,\n            span,\n            ambient_variance,\n            param_env,\n            goals: vec![],\n            cache: Default::default(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<Infcx, I> TypeRelation<I> for SolverRelating<'_, Infcx, I>\nwhere\n    Infcx: InferCtxtLike<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    fn cx(&self) -> I {\n        self.infcx.cx()\n    }\n\n    fn relate_item_args(\n        &mut self,\n        item_def_id: I::DefId,\n        a_arg: I::GenericArgs,\n        b_arg: I::GenericArgs,\n    ) -> RelateResult<I, I::GenericArgs> {\n        if self.ambient_variance == ty::Invariant {\n            // Avoid fetching the variance if we are in an invariant\n            // context; no need, and it can induce dependency cycles\n            // (e.g., #41849).\n            relate_args_invariantly(self, a_arg, b_arg)\n        } else {\n            let tcx = self.cx();\n            let opt_variances = tcx.variances_of(item_def_id);\n            relate_args_with_variances(self, item_def_id, opt_variances, a_arg, b_arg, false)\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn relate_with_variance<T: Relate<I>>(\n        &mut self,\n        variance: ty::Variance,\n        _info: VarianceDiagInfo<I>,\n        a: T,\n        b: T,\n    ) -> RelateResult<I, T> {\n        let old_ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance;\n        self.ambient_variance = self.ambient_variance.xform(variance);\n        debug!(?self.ambient_variance, \"new ambient variance\");\n\n        let r = if self.ambient_variance == ty::Bivariant { Ok(a) } else { self.relate(a, b) };\n\n        self.ambient_variance = old_ambient_variance;\n        r\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn tys(&mut self, a: I::Ty, b: I::Ty) -> RelateResult<I, I::Ty> {\n        if a == b {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        let infcx = self.infcx;\n        let a = infcx.shallow_resolve(a);\n        let b = infcx.shallow_resolve(b);\n\n        if self.cache.contains(&(self.ambient_variance, a, b)) {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        match (a.kind(), b.kind()) {\n            (ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_id)), ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_id))) => {\n                match self.ambient_variance {\n                    ty::Covariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `A <: B` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.goals.push(Goal::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypeRelation {\n                                a_is_expected: true,\n                                sub_ty: a,\n                                super_ty: b,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Contravariant => {\n                        // can't make progress on `B <: A` if both A and B are\n                        // type variables, so record an obligation.\n                        self.goals.push(Goal::new(\n                            self.cx(),\n                            self.param_env,\n                            ty::Binder::dummy(ty::PredicateKind::Subtype(ty::SubtypePredicate {\n                                a_is_expected: false,\n                                a: b,\n                                b: a,\n                            })),\n                        ));\n                    }\n                    ty::Invariant => {\n                        infcx.equate_ty_vids_raw(a_id, b_id);\n                    }\n                    ty::Bivariant => {\n                        unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            (ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(a_vid)), _) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var_raw(self, true, a_vid, self.ambient_variance, b)?;\n            }\n            (_, ty::Infer(ty::TyVar(b_vid))) => {\n                infcx.instantiate_ty_var_raw(\n                    self,\n                    false,\n                    b_vid,\n                    self.ambient_variance.xform(ty::Contravariant),\n                    a,\n                )?;\n            }\n\n            _ => {\n                super_combine_tys(self.infcx, self, a, b)?;\n            }\n        }\n\n        assert!(self.cache.insert((self.ambient_variance, a, b)));\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn regions(&mut self, a: I::Region, b: I::Region) -> RelateResult<I, I::Region> {\n        match self.ambient_variance {\n            // Subtype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('a: 'b) => SubRegion('b, 'a)\n            ty::Covariant => self.infcx.sub_regions(b, a, self.span),\n            // Suptype(&'a u8, &'b u8) => Outlives('b: 'a) => SubRegion('a, 'b)\n            ty::Contravariant => self.infcx.sub_regions(a, b, self.span),\n            ty::Invariant => self.infcx.equate_regions(a, b, self.span),\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n\n    #[instrument(skip(self), level = \"trace\")]\n    fn consts(&mut self, a: I::Const, b: I::Const) -> RelateResult<I, I::Const> {\n        super_combine_consts(self.infcx, self, a, b)\n    }\n\n    fn binders<T>(\n        &mut self,\n        a: ty::Binder<I, T>,\n        b: ty::Binder<I, T>,\n    ) -> RelateResult<I, ty::Binder<I, T>>\n    where\n        T: Relate<I>,\n    {\n        // If they're equal, then short-circuit.\n        if a == b {\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        // If they have no bound vars, relate normally.\n        if let Some(a_inner) = a.no_bound_vars()\n            && let Some(b_inner) = b.no_bound_vars()\n        {\n            self.relate(a_inner, b_inner)?;\n            return Ok(a);\n        }\n\n        match self.ambient_variance {\n            // Checks whether `for<..> sub <: for<..> sup` holds.\n            //\n            // For this to hold, **all** instantiations of the super type\n            // have to be a super type of **at least one** instantiation of\n            // the subtype.\n            //\n            // This is implemented by first entering a new universe.\n            // We then replace all bound variables in `sup` with placeholders,\n            // and all bound variables in `sub` with inference vars.\n            // We can then just relate the two resulting types as normal.\n            //\n            // Note: this is a subtle algorithm. For a full explanation, please see\n            // the [rustc dev guide][rd]\n            //\n            // [rd]: https://rustc-dev-guide.rust-lang.org/borrow_check/region_inference/placeholders_and_universes.html\n            ty::Covariant => {\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                    let a = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(a);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n            }\n            ty::Contravariant => {\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                    let b = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(b);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n            }\n\n            // When **equating** binders, we check that there is a 1-to-1\n            // correspondence between the bound vars in both types.\n            //\n            // We do so by separately instantiating one of the binders with\n            // placeholders and the other with inference variables and then\n            // equating the instantiated types.\n            //\n            // We want `for<..> A == for<..> B` -- therefore we want\n            // `exists<..> A == for<..> B` and `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n            // Check if `exists<..> A == for<..> B`\n            ty::Invariant => {\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(b, |b| {\n                    let a = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(a);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n\n                // Check if `exists<..> B == for<..> A`.\n                self.infcx.enter_forall(a, |a| {\n                    let b = self.infcx.instantiate_binder_with_infer(b);\n                    self.relate(a, b)\n                })?;\n            }\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        }\n        Ok(a)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<Infcx, I> PredicateEmittingRelation<Infcx> for SolverRelating<'_, Infcx, I>\nwhere\n    Infcx: InferCtxtLike<Interner = I>,\n    I: Interner,\n{\n    fn span(&self) -> I::Span {\n        Span::dummy()\n    }\n\n    fn param_env(&self) -> I::ParamEnv {\n        self.param_env\n    }\n\n    fn structurally_relate_aliases(&self) -> StructurallyRelateAliases {\n        self.structurally_relate_aliases\n    }\n\n    fn register_predicates(\n        &mut self,\n        obligations: impl IntoIterator<Item: ty::Upcast<I, I::Predicate>>,\n    ) {\n        self.goals.extend(\n            obligations.into_iter().map(|pred| Goal::new(self.infcx.cx(), self.param_env, pred)),\n        );\n    }\n\n    fn register_goals(&mut self, obligations: impl IntoIterator<Item = Goal<I, I::Predicate>>) {\n        self.goals.extend(obligations);\n    }\n\n    fn register_alias_relate_predicate(&mut self, a: I::Ty, b: I::Ty) {\n        self.register_predicates([ty::Binder::dummy(match self.ambient_variance {\n            ty::Covariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            // a :> b is b <: a\n            ty::Contravariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                b.into(),\n                a.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Subtype,\n            ),\n            ty::Invariant => ty::PredicateKind::AliasRelate(\n                a.into(),\n                b.into(),\n                ty::AliasRelationDirection::Equate,\n            ),\n            ty::Bivariant => {\n                unreachable!(\"Expected bivariance to be handled in relate_with_variance\")\n            }\n        })]);\n    }\n}\n",

{

  "newString": "    HostEffectPredicate, NormalizesTo, OutlivesPredicate, PolyCoercePredicate,\n    PolyExistentialPredicate, PolyExistentialProjection, PolyExistentialTraitRef,\n    PolyProjectionPredicate, PolyRegionOutlivesPredicate, PolySubtypeRelation, PolyTraitPredicate,\n    PolyTraitRef, PolyTypeOutlivesPredicate, Predicate, PredicateKind, ProjectionPredicate,\n    RegionOutlivesPredicate, SubtypeRelation, TraitPredicate, TraitRef, TypeOutlivesPredicate,\n};",
  "oldString": "    HostEffectPredicate, NormalizesTo, OutlivesPredicate, PolyCoercePredicate,\n    PolyExistentialPredicate, PolyExistentialProjection, PolyExistentialTraitRef,\n    PolyProjectionPredicate, PolyRegionOutlivesPredicate, PolySubtypePredicate, PolyTraitPredicate,\n    PolyTraitRef, PolyTypeOutlivesPredicate, Predicate, PredicateKind, ProjectionPredicate,\n    RegionOutlivesPredicate, SubtypePredicate, TraitPredicate, TraitRef, TypeOutlivesPredicate,\n};",
  "originalFile": "//! Defines how the compiler represents types internally.\n//!\n//! Two important entities in this module are:\n//!\n//! - [`rustc_middle::ty::Ty`], used to represent the semantics of a type.\n//! - [`rustc_middle::ty::TyCtxt`], the central data structure in the compiler.\n//!\n//! For more information, see [\"The `ty` module: representing types\"] in the rustc-dev-guide.\n//!\n//! [\"The `ty` module: representing types\"]: https://rustc-dev-guide.rust-lang.org/ty.html\n\n#![allow(rustc::usage_of_ty_tykind)]\n\nuse std::assert_matches::assert_matches;\nuse std::fmt::Debug;\nuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\nuse std::marker::PhantomData;\nuse std::num::NonZero;\nuse std::ptr::NonNull;\nuse std::{fmt, iter, str};\n\npub use adt::*;\npub use assoc::*;\npub use generic_args::{GenericArgKind, TermKind, *};\npub use generics::*;\npub use intrinsic::IntrinsicDef;\nuse rustc_abi::{Align, FieldIdx, Integer, IntegerType, ReprFlags, ReprOptions, VariantIdx};\nuse rustc_ast::expand::typetree::{FncTree, Kind, Type, TypeTree};\nuse rustc_ast::node_id::NodeMap;\npub use rustc_ast_ir::{Movability, Mutability, try_visit};\nuse rustc_data_structures::fx::{FxHashMap, FxHashSet, FxIndexMap, FxIndexSet};\nuse rustc_data_structures::intern::Interned;\nuse rustc_data_structures::stable_hasher::{HashStable, StableHasher};\nuse rustc_data_structures::steal::Steal;\nuse rustc_data_structures::unord::{UnordMap, UnordSet};\nuse rustc_errors::{Diag, ErrorGuaranteed, LintBuffer};\nuse rustc_hir::attrs::{AttributeKind, StrippedCfgItem};\nuse rustc_hir::def::{CtorKind, CtorOf, DefKind, DocLinkResMap, LifetimeRes, Res};\nuse rustc_hir::def_id::{CrateNum, DefId, DefIdMap, LocalDefId, LocalDefIdMap};\nuse rustc_hir::{LangItem, attrs as attr, find_attr};\nuse rustc_index::IndexVec;\nuse rustc_index::bit_set::BitMatrix;\nuse rustc_macros::{\n    Decodable, Encodable, HashStable, TyDecodable, TyEncodable, TypeFoldable, TypeVisitable,\n    extension,\n};\nuse rustc_query_system::ich::StableHashingContext;\nuse rustc_serialize::{Decodable, Encodable};\npub use rustc_session::lint::RegisteredTools;\nuse rustc_span::hygiene::MacroKind;\nuse rustc_span::{DUMMY_SP, ExpnId, ExpnKind, Ident, Span, Symbol, sym};\npub use rustc_type_ir::data_structures::{DelayedMap, DelayedSet};\npub use rustc_type_ir::fast_reject::DeepRejectCtxt;\n#[allow(\n    hidden_glob_reexports,\n    rustc::usage_of_type_ir_inherent,\n    rustc::non_glob_import_of_type_ir_inherent\n)]\nuse rustc_type_ir::inherent;\npub use rustc_type_ir::relate::VarianceDiagInfo;\npub use rustc_type_ir::solve::{CandidatePreferenceMode, SizedTraitKind};\npub use rustc_type_ir::*;\n#[allow(hidden_glob_reexports, unused_imports)]\nuse rustc_type_ir::{InferCtxtLike, Interner};\nuse tracing::{debug, instrument, trace};\npub use vtable::*;\nuse {rustc_ast as ast, rustc_hir as hir};\n\npub use self::closure::{\n    BorrowKind, CAPTURE_STRUCT_LOCAL, CaptureInfo, CapturedPlace, ClosureTypeInfo,\n    MinCaptureInformationMap, MinCaptureList, RootVariableMinCaptureList, UpvarCapture, UpvarId,\n    UpvarPath, analyze_coroutine_closure_captures, is_ancestor_or_same_capture,\n    place_to_string_for_capture,\n};\npub use self::consts::{\n    AnonConstKind, AtomicOrdering, Const, ConstInt, ConstKind, ConstToValTreeResult, Expr,\n    ExprKind, ScalarInt, SimdAlign, UnevaluatedConst, ValTree, ValTreeKind, Value,\n};\npub use self::context::{\n    CtxtInterners, CurrentGcx, Feed, FreeRegionInfo, GlobalCtxt, Lift, TyCtxt, TyCtxtFeed, tls,\n};\npub use self::fold::*;\npub use self::instance::{Instance, InstanceKind, ReifyReason, UnusedGenericParams};\npub use self::list::{List, ListWithCachedTypeInfo};\npub use self::opaque_types::OpaqueTypeKey;\npub use self::pattern::{Pattern, PatternKind};\npub use self::predicate::{\n    AliasTerm, ArgOutlivesPredicate, Clause, ClauseKind, CoercePredicate, ExistentialPredicate,\n    ExistentialPredicateStableCmpExt, ExistentialProjection, ExistentialTraitRef,\n    HostEffectPredicate, NormalizesTo, OutlivesPredicate, PolyCoercePredicate,\n    PolyExistentialPredicate, PolyExistentialProjection, PolyExistentialTraitRef,\n    PolyProjectionPredicate, PolyRegionOutlivesPredicate, PolySubtypePredicate, PolyTraitPredicate,\n    PolyTraitRef, PolyTypeOutlivesPredicate, Predicate, PredicateKind, ProjectionPredicate,\n    RegionOutlivesPredicate, SubtypePredicate, TraitPredicate, TraitRef, TypeOutlivesPredicate,\n};\npub use self::region::{\n    BoundRegion, BoundRegionKind, EarlyParamRegion, LateParamRegion, LateParamRegionKind, Region,\n    RegionKind, RegionVid,\n};\npub use self::sty::{\n    AliasTy, Article, Binder, BoundTy, BoundTyKind, BoundVariableKind, CanonicalPolyFnSig,\n    CoroutineArgsExt, EarlyBinder, FnSig, InlineConstArgs, InlineConstArgsParts, ParamConst,\n    ParamTy, PolyFnSig, TyKind, TypeAndMut, TypingMode, UpvarArgs,\n};\npub use self::trait_def::TraitDef;\npub use self::typeck_results::{\n    CanonicalUserType, CanonicalUserTypeAnnotation, CanonicalUserTypeAnnotations, IsIdentity,\n    Rust2024IncompatiblePatInfo, TypeckResults, UserType, UserTypeAnnotationIndex, UserTypeKind,\n};\nuse crate::error::{OpaqueHiddenTypeMismatch, TypeMismatchReason};\nuse crate::metadata::ModChild;\nuse crate::middle::privacy::EffectiveVisibilities;\nuse crate::mir::{Body, CoroutineLayout, CoroutineSavedLocal, SourceInfo};\nuse crate::query::{IntoQueryParam, Providers};\nuse crate::ty;\nuse crate::ty::codec::{TyDecoder, TyEncoder};\npub use crate::ty::diagnostics::*;\nuse crate::ty::fast_reject::SimplifiedType;\nuse crate::ty::layout::LayoutError;\nuse crate::ty::util::Discr;\nuse crate::ty::walk::TypeWalker;\n\npub mod abstract_const;\npub mod adjustment;\npub mod cast;\npub mod codec;\npub mod error;\npub mod fast_reject;\npub mod inhabitedness;\npub mod layout;\npub mod normalize_erasing_regions;\npub mod offload_meta;\npub mod pattern;\npub mod print;\npub mod relate;\npub mod significant_drop_order;\npub mod trait_def;\npub mod util;\npub mod vtable;\n\nmod adt;\nmod assoc;\nmod closure;\nmod consts;\nmod context;\nmod diagnostics;\nmod elaborate_impl;\nmod erase_regions;\nmod fold;\nmod generic_args;\nmod generics;\nmod impls_ty;\nmod instance;\nmod intrinsic;\nmod list;\nmod opaque_types;\nmod predicate;\nmod region;\nmod structural_impls;\n#[allow(hidden_glob_reexports)]\nmod sty;\nmod typeck_results;\nmod visit;\n\n// Data types\n\n#[derive(Debug, HashStable)]\npub struct ResolverGlobalCtxt {\n    pub visibilities_for_hashing: Vec<(LocalDefId, Visibility)>,\n    /// Item with a given `LocalDefId` was defined during macro expansion with ID `ExpnId`.\n    pub expn_that_defined: UnordMap<LocalDefId, ExpnId>,\n    pub effective_visibilities: EffectiveVisibilities,\n    pub extern_crate_map: UnordMap<LocalDefId, CrateNum>,\n    pub maybe_unused_trait_imports: FxIndexSet<LocalDefId>,\n    pub module_children: LocalDefIdMap<Vec<ModChild>>,\n    pub glob_map: FxIndexMap<LocalDefId, FxIndexSet<Symbol>>,\n    pub main_def: Option<MainDefinition>,\n    pub trait_impls: FxIndexMap<DefId, Vec<LocalDefId>>,\n    /// A list of proc macro LocalDefIds, written out in the order in which\n    /// they are declared in the static array generated by proc_macro_harness.\n    pub proc_macros: Vec<LocalDefId>,\n    /// Mapping from ident span to path span for paths that don't exist as written, but that\n    /// exist under `std`. For example, wrote `str::from_utf8` instead of `std::str::from_utf8`.\n    pub confused_type_with_std_module: FxIndexMap<Span, Span>,\n    pub doc_link_resolutions: FxIndexMap<LocalDefId, DocLinkResMap>,\n    pub doc_link_traits_in_scope: FxIndexMap<LocalDefId, Vec<DefId>>,\n    pub all_macro_rules: UnordSet<Symbol>,\n    pub stripped_cfg_items: Vec<StrippedCfgItem>,\n}\n\n/// Resolutions that should only be used for lowering.\n/// This struct is meant to be consumed by lowering.\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct ResolverAstLowering {\n    pub legacy_const_generic_args: FxHashMap<DefId, Option<Vec<usize>>>,\n\n    /// Resolutions for nodes that have a single resolution.\n    pub partial_res_map: NodeMap<hir::def::PartialRes>,\n    /// Resolutions for import nodes, which have multiple resolutions in different namespaces.\n    pub import_res_map: NodeMap<hir::def::PerNS<Option<Res<ast::NodeId>>>>,\n    /// Resolutions for labels (node IDs of their corresponding blocks or loops).\n    pub label_res_map: NodeMap<ast::NodeId>,\n    /// Resolutions for lifetimes.\n    pub lifetimes_res_map: NodeMap<LifetimeRes>,\n    /// Lifetime parameters that lowering will have to introduce.\n    pub extra_lifetime_params_map: NodeMap<Vec<(Ident, ast::NodeId, LifetimeRes)>>,\n\n    pub next_node_id: ast::NodeId,\n\n    pub node_id_to_def_id: NodeMap<LocalDefId>,\n\n    pub trait_map: NodeMap<Vec<hir::TraitCandidate>>,\n    /// List functions and methods for which lifetime elision was successful.\n    pub lifetime_elision_allowed: FxHashSet<ast::NodeId>,\n\n    /// Lints that were emitted by the resolver and early lints.\n    pub lint_buffer: Steal<LintBuffer>,\n\n    /// Information about functions signatures for delegation items expansion\n    pub delegation_fn_sigs: LocalDefIdMap<DelegationFnSig>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\npub struct DelegationFnSig {\n    pub header: ast::FnHeader,\n    pub param_count: usize,\n    pub has_self: bool,\n    pub c_variadic: bool,\n    pub target_feature: bool,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, HashStable)]\npub struct MainDefinition {\n    pub res: Res<ast::NodeId>,\n    pub is_import: bool,\n    pub span: Span,\n}\n\nimpl MainDefinition {\n    pub fn opt_fn_def_id(self) -> Option<DefId> {\n        if let Res::Def(DefKind::Fn, def_id) = self.res { Some(def_id) } else { None }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, TyEncodable, TyDecodable, HashStable)]\npub struct ImplTraitHeader<'tcx> {\n    pub trait_ref: ty::EarlyBinder<'tcx, ty::TraitRef<'tcx>>,\n    pub polarity: ImplPolarity,\n    pub safety: hir::Safety,\n    pub constness: hir::Constness,\n}\n\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, TyEncodable, TyDecodable, HashStable, Debug)]\n#[derive(TypeFoldable, TypeVisitable, Default)]\npub enum Asyncness {\n    Yes,\n    #[default]\n    No,\n}\n\nimpl Asyncness {\n    pub fn is_async(self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Asyncness::Yes)\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Copy, Hash, Encodable, Decodable, HashStable)]\npub enum Visibility<Id = LocalDefId> {\n    /// Visible everywhere (including in other crates).\n    Public,\n    /// Visible only in the given crate-local module.\n    Restricted(Id),\n}\n\nimpl Visibility {\n    pub fn to_string(self, def_id: LocalDefId, tcx: TyCtxt<'_>) -> String {\n        match self {\n            ty::Visibility::Restricted(restricted_id) => {\n                if restricted_id.is_top_level_module() {\n                    \"pub(crate)\".to_string()\n                } else if restricted_id == tcx.parent_module_from_def_id(def_id).to_local_def_id() {\n                    \"pub(self)\".to_string()\n                } else {\n                    format!(\n                        \"pub(in crate{})\",\n                        tcx.def_path(restricted_id.to_def_id()).to_string_no_crate_verbose()\n                    )\n                }\n            }\n            ty::Visibility::Public => \"pub\".to_string(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Copy, Hash, TyEncodable, TyDecodable, HashStable)]\n#[derive(TypeFoldable, TypeVisitable)]\npub struct ClosureSizeProfileData<'tcx> {\n    /// Tuple containing the types of closure captures before the feature `capture_disjoint_fields`\n    pub before_feature_tys: Ty<'tcx>,\n    /// Tuple containing the types of closure captures after the feature `capture_disjoint_fields`\n    pub after_feature_tys: Ty<'tcx>,\n}\n\nimpl TyCtxt<'_> {\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn opt_parent(self, id: DefId) -> Option<DefId> {\n        self.def_key(id).parent.map(|index| DefId { index, ..id })\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    #[track_caller]\n    pub fn parent(self, id: DefId) -> DefId {\n        match self.opt_parent(id) {\n            Some(id) => id,\n            // not `unwrap_or_else` to avoid breaking caller tracking\n            None => bug!(\"{id:?} doesn't have a parent\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    #[track_caller]\n    pub fn opt_local_parent(self, id: LocalDefId) -> Option<LocalDefId> {\n        self.opt_parent(id.to_def_id()).map(DefId::expect_local)\n    }\n\n    #[inline]\n    #[track_caller]\n    pub fn local_parent(self, id: impl Into<LocalDefId>) -> LocalDefId {\n        self.parent(id.into().to_def_id()).expect_local()\n    }\n\n    pub fn is_descendant_of(self, mut descendant: DefId, ancestor: DefId) -> bool {\n        if descendant.krate != ancestor.krate {\n            return false;\n        }\n\n        while descendant != ancestor {\n            match self.opt_parent(descendant) {\n                Some(parent) => descendant = parent,\n                None => return false,\n            }\n        }\n        true\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<Id> Visibility<Id> {\n    pub fn is_public(self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, Visibility::Public)\n    }\n\n    pub fn map_id<OutId>(self, f: impl FnOnce(Id) -> OutId) -> Visibility<OutId> {\n        match self {\n            Visibility::Public => Visibility::Public,\n            Visibility::Restricted(id) => Visibility::Restricted(f(id)),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<Id: Into<DefId>> Visibility<Id> {\n    pub fn to_def_id(self) -> Visibility<DefId> {\n        self.map_id(Into::into)\n    }\n\n    /// Returns `true` if an item with this visibility is accessible from the given module.\n    pub fn is_accessible_from(self, module: impl Into<DefId>, tcx: TyCtxt<'_>) -> bool {\n        match self {\n            // Public items are visible everywhere.\n            Visibility::Public => true,\n            Visibility::Restricted(id) => tcx.is_descendant_of(module.into(), id.into()),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Returns `true` if this visibility is at least as accessible as the given visibility\n    pub fn is_at_least(self, vis: Visibility<impl Into<DefId>>, tcx: TyCtxt<'_>) -> bool {\n        match vis {\n            Visibility::Public => self.is_public(),\n            Visibility::Restricted(id) => self.is_accessible_from(id, tcx),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Visibility<DefId> {\n    pub fn expect_local(self) -> Visibility {\n        self.map_id(|id| id.expect_local())\n    }\n\n    /// Returns `true` if this item is visible anywhere in the local crate.\n    pub fn is_visible_locally(self) -> bool {\n        match self {\n            Visibility::Public => true,\n            Visibility::Restricted(def_id) => def_id.is_local(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// The crate variances map is computed during typeck and contains the\n/// variance of every item in the local crate. You should not use it\n/// directly, because to do so will make your pass dependent on the\n/// HIR of every item in the local crate. Instead, use\n/// `tcx.variances_of()` to get the variance for a *particular*\n/// item.\n#[derive(HashStable, Debug)]\npub struct CrateVariancesMap<'tcx> {\n    /// For each item with generics, maps to a vector of the variance\n    /// of its generics. If an item has no generics, it will have no\n    /// entry.\n    pub variances: DefIdMap<&'tcx [ty::Variance]>,\n}\n\n// Contains information needed to resolve types and (in the future) look up\n// the types of AST nodes.\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]\npub struct CReaderCacheKey {\n    pub cnum: Option<CrateNum>,\n    pub pos: usize,\n}\n\n/// Use this rather than `TyKind`, whenever possible.\n#[derive(Copy, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, HashStable)]\n#[rustc_diagnostic_item = \"Ty\"]\n#[rustc_pass_by_value]\npub struct Ty<'tcx>(Interned<'tcx, WithCachedTypeInfo<TyKind<'tcx>>>);\n\nimpl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::inherent::IntoKind for Ty<'tcx> {\n    type Kind = TyKind<'tcx>;\n\n    fn kind(self) -> TyKind<'tcx> {\n        *self.kind()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::Flags for Ty<'tcx> {\n    fn flags(&self) -> TypeFlags {\n        self.0.flags\n    }\n\n    fn outer_exclusive_binder(&self) -> DebruijnIndex {\n        self.0.outer_exclusive_binder\n    }\n}\n\n/// The crate outlives map is computed during typeck and contains the\n/// outlives of every item in the local crate. You should not use it\n/// directly, because to do so will make your pass dependent on the\n/// HIR of every item in the local crate. Instead, use\n/// `tcx.inferred_outlives_of()` to get the outlives for a *particular*\n/// item.\n#[derive(HashStable, Debug)]\npub struct CratePredicatesMap<'tcx> {\n    /// For each struct with outlive bounds, maps to a vector of the\n    /// predicate of its outlive bounds. If an item has no outlives\n    /// bounds, it will have no entry.\n    pub predicates: DefIdMap<&'tcx [(Clause<'tcx>, Span)]>,\n}\n\n#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, PartialOrd, Ord, Hash)]\npub struct Term<'tcx> {\n    ptr: NonNull<()>,\n    marker: PhantomData<(Ty<'tcx>, Const<'tcx>)>,\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::inherent::Term<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for Term<'tcx> {}\n\nimpl<'tcx> rustc_type_ir::inherent::IntoKind for Term<'tcx> {\n    type Kind = TermKind<'tcx>;\n\n    fn kind(self) -> Self::Kind {\n        self.kind()\n    }\n}\n\nunsafe impl<'tcx> rustc_data_structures::sync::DynSend for Term<'tcx> where\n    &'tcx (Ty<'tcx>, Const<'tcx>): rustc_data_structures::sync::DynSend\n{\n}\nunsafe impl<'tcx> rustc_data_structures::sync::DynSync for Term<'tcx> where\n    &'tcx (Ty<'tcx>, Const<'tcx>): rustc_data_structures::sync::DynSync\n{\n}\nunsafe impl<'tcx> Send for Term<'tcx> where &'tcx (Ty<'tcx>, Const<'tcx>): Send {}\nunsafe impl<'tcx> Sync for Term<'tcx> where &'tcx (Ty<'tcx>, Const<'tcx>): Sync {}\n\nimpl Debug for Term<'_> {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {\n        match self.kind() {\n            TermKind::Ty(ty) => write!(f, \"Term::Ty({ty:?})\"),\n            TermKind::Const(ct) => write!(f, \"Term::Const({ct:?})\"),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> From<Ty<'tcx>> for Term<'tcx> {\n    fn from(ty: Ty<'tcx>) -> Self {\n        TermKind::Ty(ty).pack()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> From<Const<'tcx>> for Term<'tcx> {\n    fn from(c: Const<'tcx>) -> Self {\n        TermKind::Const(c).pack()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'a, 'tcx> HashStable<StableHashingContext<'a>> for Term<'tcx> {\n    fn hash_stable(&self, hcx: &mut StableHashingContext<'a>, hasher: &mut StableHasher) {\n        self.kind().hash_stable(hcx, hasher);\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> TypeFoldable<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for Term<'tcx> {\n    fn try_fold_with<F: FallibleTypeFolder<TyCtxt<'tcx>>>(\n        self,\n        folder: &mut F,\n    ) -> Result<Self, F::Error> {\n        match self.kind() {\n            ty::TermKind::Ty(ty) => ty.try_fold_with(folder).map(Into::into),\n            ty::TermKind::Const(ct) => ct.try_fold_with(folder).map(Into::into),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn fold_with<F: TypeFolder<TyCtxt<'tcx>>>(self, folder: &mut F) -> Self {\n        match self.kind() {\n            ty::TermKind::Ty(ty) => ty.fold_with(folder).into(),\n            ty::TermKind::Const(ct) => ct.fold_with(folder).into(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> TypeVisitable<TyCtxt<'tcx>> for Term<'tcx> {\n    fn visit_with<V: TypeVisitor<TyCtxt<'tcx>>>(&self, visitor: &mut V) -> V::Result {\n        match self.kind() {\n            ty::TermKind::Ty(ty) => ty.visit_with(visitor),\n            ty::TermKind::Const(ct) => ct.visit_with(visitor),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx, E: TyEncoder<'tcx>> Encodable<E> for Term<'tcx> {\n    fn encode(&self, e: &mut E) {\n        self.kind().encode(e)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx, D: TyDecoder<'tcx>> Decodable<D> for Term<'tcx> {\n    fn decode(d: &mut D) -> Self {\n        let res: TermKind<'tcx> = Decodable::decode(d);\n        res.pack()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl<'tcx> Term<'tcx> {\n    #[inline]\n    pub fn kind(self) -> TermKind<'tcx> {\n        let ptr =\n            unsafe { self.ptr.map_addr(|addr| NonZero::new_unchecked(addr.get() & !TAG_MASK)) };\n        // SAFETY: use of `Interned::new_unchecked` here is ok because these\n        // pointers were originally created from `Interned` types in `pack()`,\n        // and this is just going in the other direction.\n        unsafe {\n            match self.ptr.addr().get() & TAG_MASK {\n                TYPE_TAG => TermKind::Ty(Ty(Interned::new_unchecked(\n                    ptr.cast::<WithCachedTypeInfo<ty::TyKind<'tcx>>>().as_ref(),\n                ))),\n                CONST_TAG => TermKind::Const(ty::Const(Interned::new_unchecked(\n                    ptr.cast::<WithCachedTypeInfo<ty::ConstKind<'tcx>>>().as_ref(),\n                ))),\n                _ => core::intrinsics::unreachable(),\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn as_type(&self) -> Option<Ty<'tcx>> {\n        if let TermKind::Ty(ty) = self.kind() { Some(ty) } else { None }\n    }\n\n    pub fn expect_type(&self) -> Ty<'tcx> {\n        self.as_type().expect(\"expected a type, but found a const\")\n    }\n\n    pub fn as_const(&self) -> Option<Const<'tcx>> {\n        if let
